Data Quality & Validation

  • Implement automated validation checks (row counts, duplicates, completeness, integrity)
  • Ensure accuracy and consistency of data before reporting
  • Identify and resolve root causes of data inconsistencies


Automation & ETL Processes

  • Support and maintain ETL pipelines and data workflows
  • Build automated processes for data ingestion, transformation, and validation
  • Implement scheduling/orchestration tools (cron, AWS services, Airflow, or equivalent)
  • Ensure processes run without manual intervention


Scripting & Process Automation

  • Develop scripts using Python, Bash, or PowerShell
  • Automate data pulls, file processing, and pipeline validation
  • Integrate scripts into end-to-end workflows


Reporting & Dashboards

  • Develop and maintain automated reports and dashboards
  • Support daily, weekly, and monthly reporting requirements
  • Ensure reports are accurate, consistent, and delivered on schedule


Benefits

  • Health insurance
  • Dental insurance
  • Vision insurance
  • Life Insurance
  • 401(k) Retirement Plan with matching
  • Paid Time Off
  • Paid Federal Holidays


Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in a technical field (Data Analytics, Information Technology, Computer Science, or related)

  • Strong experience working in sensitive or controlled data environments
  • Advanced SQL proficiency (Amazon Redshift, Oracle preferred)
  • Experience with large, relational datasets and data structures
  • Strong understanding of joins, aggregations, and query optimization
  • Experience troubleshooting data discrepancies and ensuring data integrity
  • Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipelines
  • Experience building recurring reports and dashboards
  • Strong attention to detail and data validation skills


Preferred Qualifications

  • 3-6 years of post-college professional experience
  • Experience with predictive analytics or modeling
  • Proficiency in Python or R for data analysis
  • Experience with automation tools and scheduling frameworks
  • Experience supporting executive-level decision-making

Title: Operations Data Analyst
Description:
ABOUT 49North

49North is a Canadian defence company delivering secure, multi-domain C4ISR capabilities that provide decision advantage in complex and contested environments. A wholly-owned subsidiary of MDA Space, building on more than five decades of trusted Canadian defence delivery, 49North operates as a sovereign Canadian provider of mission-critical C4ISR systems, bringing disciplined prime-contractor execution to defence and security programs.

Visit 49North | An MDA Space Company.

49North is rooted in the execution of large, complex, and mission-critical programs developed within 49NORTH. This legacy reflects a record of delivery where engineering rigour, operational reliability, and national trust are non-negotiable.

49North Defence strengthens Canada’s sovereign defence capability by delivering integrated mission systems and multi-domain C4ISR integration, enabling trusted Canadian and coalition operations, assured interoperability, and sustained readiness.

Role

The role of Operations Data Analyst reports to the Director, Mission Assurance and resides within the Administration function of the larger Programs & Operations Team of 49North, an MDA Space business. As an Operations Data Analyst, you will work with business stakeholders to realize business intelligence through structured data analysis, processing and visualization. This role also includes Process Specialist responsibilities, you will work with process stakeholders to define, realize (implement), deploy and maintain in-scope processes as well as administrate supporting systems, notably the 49North Business Process System (BPS). As an ISO 9001 certified Quality System and plans to implement Capability Maturity Model Integration (CMMI), you will help continuously improve select enterprise systems to meet local needs and assist in the deployment of OneMDA Corporate improvement initiatives.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities of the Operations Data Analyst includes those associated with continual improvement as a Process Specialist. In terms of data analysis, two aspects are considered: business intelligence initiation and business intelligence realization.

Business Intelligence Initiation

Works With Business Leadership And Performs The Following


Identifies the means to quantitatively or qualitatively support assessment of key business objectives and their corresponding Critical Success Factors through identification of Key Performance Indicators and associated metrics
Creates plans describing the bringing together of data sources, working with Corporate stakeholders (e.g. IT) as necessary


Business Intelligence Realization


Identifies and assesses data sources for suitability and takes actions to remediate data quality and integrity as necessary
Connects data sources in a way that enables collective analysis
Develops dashboards, reports and data visualizations that provide meaningful insights to performance
Performs analyses identifying trends, patterns, anomalies and opportunities for improvement
Provides and/or presents evidence-based data interpretations and recommendations


In terms of continual improvement, two aspects are considered: facilitating implementation and change initiatives and administrating supporting systems.

Implementation and Change Initiatives Facilitation

Sources of improvement efforts can include ongoing activities (e.g. maintaining the BPS), local business initiatives (e.g. implementing CMMI), and enabling the deployment of Corporate-sponsored OneMDA initiatives.


Collaborates with leaders and subject matter experts to understand business challenges and information needs
Administrates the identification, prioritization and effort reporting of process improvement initiatives
Assesses processes (e.g. preforms value stream mapping), including creating as-is and to-be depictions of process, to identify and eliminate inefficiencies, waste, and ineffectiveness
Facilitates discovery activities working with process stakeholders and subject matter experts
Creates/revises process descriptions (e.g. procedures) and associated process artifacts (e.g. forms, templates) and training materials


Supporting Systems Administration


Establishes and maintains the process improvement roadmap reflecting the priorities of Operational Excellence and, as applicable, upgrades to the content of the BPS
Maintains the BPS through the evaluation of process Change Requests (CRs) and administration of Change Control Board (CCB) meetings with Process Owners
Participates in the review of Lessons Learned reports to identify potential opportunities for improvement (OFIs)
Leads the disposition of OFI records
Serves as the local MS SharePoint administrator and maintains the BPS infrastructure and associated instructional information on use and administration of the BPS
Performs process change impact assessments for proposed or planned changes
Establishes and maintains a process improvement performance dashboard and periodically reports on process improvement performance


Requirements (experience/skills)


A two- or three-year accredited diploma or certificate program in data analysis
Minimum 2 years of experience in the definition, implementation, maintenance and administration of business processes
Working knowledge of and administrator-level hands-on experience with Power BI
Experience with database management systems and related query language, notably SQL
Microsoft SharePoint site-level administrator experience including HTML and Java scripting capabilities
Experience with a structured change implementation methodology as applied to business processes is required. Experience in process value-stream mapping is considered an asset
Willingness to extend one’s own technical capabilities through learning
Experience working within a quality management system or an ISO- or CMMI-certified workplace is considered an asset
Experience working with Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems (e.g. Infor LN) is considered an asset
History of taking the initiative while diplomatically keeping leadership stakeholders informed along the way
Strong interpersonal skills, particularly in the areas of peer collaboration and teamwork; comfortable interfacing with the senior management team and able to ‘sell’ improvement initiatives
Proven communication skills with an ability to present to multiple stakeholder groups and achieve their buy-in for improvement initiatives


We’re a dream team of purpose-driven, collaborative and passionate people, and we are constantly looking for others to join 49North to ignite new and innovative approaches to problem-solving that push us forward.

Comments/Special Considerations

Successful candidates must obtain and hold security clearance at the secret level, and pass security assessment for the Controlled Goods Program (CGP).

Benefits Statement

49North provides competitive compensation and benefits packages for its employees at all locations. As a team member of 49North, you and your qualified dependents are eligible to participate in a benefit plan that ensures a comprehensive level of protection through competitive health care including; extended healthcare and flexible drug plans, dental and vision benefits, disability income protection, life insurance, group retirement savings plans; and an employee and family assistance program.

Equal Opportunity Statement

49North is an equal opportunity employer prizing diversity and inclusion. We are committed to treating all employees and applicants for employment with respect and dignity. If you require assistance and/or reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please feel free to notify us.

Accommodations

49North is proud to provide accommodation(s) during the recruitment process. Should you require any accommodations, please indicate this on your application/cover letter and we will work with you to meet your accessibility needs.

#IND

The compensation range for this position is $75,000.00 - $122,760.00 annually. The specific compensation offered will be based on the qualifications and experience of the successful candidate.

Title: Business Intelligence & AI Analyst with Brightisle
Description:
Business Intelligence & AI Analyst

Turn Data into Insight. Turn Ideas into Action.

At Brightisle, We Believe Great Work Happens When People Are Empowered To Think Differently, Collaborate Openly, And Enjoy What They Do. Our Values Aren't Just Words On a Wall; They Guide How We Work Every Day

Find a Better Way – We encourage Bright ideas and nurture innovation, challenging the status quo

Make It Happen – We see challenges as opportunity and stay focused on solutions.

Everyone Matters – We value people and look for the good in everyone.

Be Honest and Kind – We are ethical, truthful and kind. A little humility goes a long way.

Have Fun – We seek opportunities enjoy laughter and happiness every day.

We're looking for a curious, analytical, and forward-thinking Business Intelligence & AI Analyst to help us transform data into meaningful insights, drive smarter decision-making, and explore innovative ways to use AI and automation across our organization.

What You'll Do

As our Business Intelligence & AI Analyst, you'll help shape how data and technology support strategic decision-making across the organization. In this role you will:


Build and maintain Power BI dashboards, executive scorecards, KPI reports, and performance metrics that help leaders make informed decisions.
Partner with teams across the organization to understand business needs and turn data into actionable insights.
Collect, integrate, validate, and analyze data from multiple systems and sources.
Identify trends, risks, opportunities, and performance improvement areas, and provide recommendations that drive results.
Translate complex information into compelling stories and visualizations that make data easy to understand.
Lead business intelligence and analytics projects from concept through implementation.
Explore opportunities to automate processes using tools such as Power Automate, Microsoft Copilot, and other AI-enabled solutions.

Title: Data Product Analyst, Customer Management Solutions
Description:
Closing Date (MM/DD/YYYY)

07/22/2026

Worker Type

Permanent

Language(s) Required

English

Term Duration (in Months)

Salary Range (plus eligible to receive a performance based incentive, applicable to position) :

$83,040 - $112,340

Why FCC?

At FCC, we’re proud to be 100% invested in Canadian agriculture and food. As a federal Crown corporation, we provide financing, knowledge resources and business management software to over 103,000 customers nationwide.

Here’s what you can expect when you join our team:


Competitive total rewards packages: market-aligned and performance-based salary and incentive programs, flexible and comprehensive group benefit and savings plans, and well-being support through benefits and wellness programs
Purpose-driven work: We build strong relationships, share knowledge and support the people who feed the world
Growth: Learning and development opportunities to help you thrive
Hybrid work options



How You’ll Make An Impact

Leverage your expertise in requirements management, user story development and business analysis to deliver solutions that enable organizational change, working in FCC’s modern AWS data environment.

As a Data Product Analyst, you’ll collaborate with domain partners and Agile teams to refine requirements, manage backlogs and ensure solutions are accurate, reliable, and ready for release. Through steady, iterative delivery, you’ll help enhance and simplify processes, guide decision-making, and provide value across complex technical and business domains.

In this role, you'll bring a data‑driven mindset, strong attention to detail and clear communication skills to drive improvement and business value in a collaborative Agile environment.

If you enjoy working with data, care deeply about quality and detail, and communicate with clarity and purpose, this role could be a great match.

What You’ll Do


Build, maintain, and enhance data capabilities supporting Customer Management Solutions business domains
Analyze, triage, and refine backlog items into clear, actionable user stories and requirements
Actively support backlog prioritization and process automation to ensure high-quality product increments are delivered on schedule
Validate and test data and solutions, including reconciling queries to ensure accuracy and completeness
Partner with team members and domain stakeholders to help uncover underlying needs and opportunities beyond initial stakeholder requests
Facilitate problem-solving and solution discussions, fostering alignment and continuous improvement
Plan, prioritize, and deliver work within agreed timelines by balancing scope, dependencies, and tradeoffs while adapting to change
Ensure a high standard of quality prior to product increment release



Required Qualifications

What you’ll bring to the team


A bachelor’s degree in computer science, software engineering, information systems or a related field, and at least three years of related experience; or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
Experience and knowledge with data warehouses and cloud environments (AWS)
Proven expertise in gathering and documenting requirements, troubleshooting technical issues and writing high-quality user stories
Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively with technical and non-technical partners.
Strong experience and knowledge with data warehouses and cloud environments (AWS)
Strong commitment to data quality and accuracy, and respect for compliance with related policies and procedures



Preferred Qualifications


Experience building and validating queries in AWS RedShift
Exposure and familiarity with Agile principles
Demonstrated Informal leadership through influence, collaboration, or continuous improvement initiatives
Lending or financial services domain knowledge



Not sure you meet every requirement? We encourage you to apply anyway.

You belong here

At FCC, we’re committed to creating an inclusive, equitable and accessible workplace – one that reflects the communities where we live, work and play. Our team is made stronger through diversity, and we’re dedicated to building a workforce that brings together a range of backgrounds, abilities and perspectives.

We encourage qualified applicants to apply, including members of these four employment equity groups:


Indigenous Peoples
Members of visible minority groups
Persons with disabilities
Women



Accessibility and accommodations

To support an inclusive and accessible candidate experience, we encourage anyone needing an adjustment or accommodation during any stage of the recruitment process to email us at: TalentSupplyRecherch@fcc-fac.ca. An HR partner will respond and work with applicants who request a reasonable accommodation. Information received in relation to accommodation requests will not impact hiring decisions.

Title: Group Disability Data Analytics Specialist
Description:
Permanent Full Time

If you’re someone who often asks “why,” this role may be a great fit. The Disability Data & Analysis team is seeking a naturally curious and analytically driven individual who can navigate complex data and uncover meaningful insights.

As a Data Analytics Specialist, you will apply strong analytical, technical, and critical thinking skills to explore data, identify trends, and deliver insights that enhance decision-making and improve business performance. You will play a key role in supporting operational and financial understanding through data. With a focus on continuous improvement, you will refine existing analyses and explore new methodologies to better support organizational leaders.

What you will do:

Analyze, Report, and Present Disability Morbidity Results


Deliver regular trending and variance analyses to support understanding of key disability morbidity KPIs
Collaborate with Group Division partners to leverage multiple data sources and assess operational impacts on financial results
Fulfill complex analytical requests from senior leadership, providing clear and actionable insights on business performance


Disability Analytics


Partner with business leaders to proactively identify opportunities for advanced analytics and data-driven solutions
Plan, manage, and execute a range of analytical projects using strong project management practices
Apply the analytics lifecycle to design, develop, and maintain advanced analytical models
Enhance data quality by conducting data profiling and identifying gaps or inconsistencies in key data sources


Insights and Storytelling


Translate complex data into clear, compelling insights tailored to the audience
Present findings, trends, and model performance to senior management, supporting informed decision-making


What you bring:


3+ years of experience in analytics, problem-solving, and data-driven decision-making
Post-secondary education with exposure to data science and/or statistical concepts
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel and Access
Experience with data tools such as SQL, Python/R, Power BI, or Tableau is considered an asset
Knowledge of group insurance and disability products is an asset
Experience in project planning and execution is an asset
Strong organizational and time management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities
Excellent communication and presentation skills
Reliability Status Security Clearance – this can only be completed with candidates who receive an offer of employment. This is a personal security status that is required as a condition of employment before an employee can gain access to Protected B information, assets or work sites as outlined by the Government of Canada  website.  The cost of submitting these checks will be covered by Canada Life.


The base salary for this position is between $59,800 - $99,700 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Grow with Canada Life

We’re united by a shared purpose: to improve the financial, physical and mental well-being of Canadians. Our company is trusted by 1 in 3 Canadians and contributes to the strength of communities across the country.

We’re looking for people who live our values everyday: we step up, we do the right thing, and we deliver – for our customers, communities and each other. Are you someone who always strives to do the right thing, who steps up for themselves and others, and who delivers with impact? Then we want to hear from you!

What we offer:

We’re committed to supporting our employees through every stage of their career. Here’s what you can expect as a full-time or part-time permanent team member:


Career Development: Opportunities for career advancement, access to industry-leading learning programs and up to $2,000 annually towards education reimbursement.
Health & Wellness: Flexible health and dental benefits, plus a $5,000 mental health benefit to support your well-being.
Time Off: In addition to regular vacation and personal days, we support community involvement with a volunteer day. 
Financial Security: Company-matching pension plan, share ownership program and additional investment options.
Rewards and Recognition: Employee recognition programs, service milestone celebrations, employee discounts and more!
Emphasis on Community: We provide a workplace where employees feel connected and supported through Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), mentorship programs, social clubs and events.


Learn more about Canada Life.

We’re committed to removing barriers and ensuring equal access to employment. Applicants requiring reasonable accommodation during the application process may contact talentacquisitioncanada@canadalife.com. All information provided will be handled in accordance with applicable laws and Canada Life policies.

Canada Life would like to thank all applicants, however only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About Us

At CAA Club Group, we are passionate about keeping our Members safe - whether they are on the road, at home, or travelling abroad. As a trusted Member-based organization, we provide products and services that are essential to Members’ safety and peace of mind. With over 2.6 million Members across Ontario and Manitoba, we advocate on issues such road safety and infrastructure, the environment, seniors’ mobility, and consumer protection. Associates share in each other’s successes, drive change and support each other while cultivating a great working environment to deliver on what is best for our Members. CAA SCO Systems & Services Inc is a member of the CAA Club Group of Companies.

Who We Are

Are you ready to join an award-winning, purpose-driven culture? Welcome to the CAA Club Group of Companies (CCG), where purpose leads to passion!

At CCG, we are committed to delivering an exceptional Associate experience. We offer:


Work-life harmony with access to an award-winning holistic wellness program,
Continuous learning through our robust corporate curriculum and education reimbursement program,
Incredible rewards, travel incentives, and product and service discounts,
Pay-for-performance and best-in-class recognition programs, and
Competitive benefits that include a defined contribution plan, personal spending account, and so much more.


Join our growing team where everyone belongs!

Position Details

What You Will Do


Create comprehensive and automated BI & Reporting solutions, while focusing on the front-end interface in Power BI.
Participate in the stakeholder requirement workshops/meetings and ensure business requirements are adequately captured in SOW.
In collaboration with BI Developer, support development of data solutions, including data models, data lifecycle management, data access and reporting elements.
Analyze complex data sets from various database servers and systems to identify insights and trends shaping in each LOB, functional area, and CCG enterprise.
Translate analysis into self-serve Power BI dashboards, including visuals, slicers, and measures with a focus on storytelling and user experience.
In collaboration with BI Developer, create mockup reports for proof-of-concept reviews.
Support lines of business in a variety of analytical needs including development of robust analytical plans, statistical analysis, key performance indicators, benchmarking, data visualizations, routine, and ad hoc reporting.
Utilize machine learning techniques to proactively identify opportunities for transforming descriptive to predictive analytics.
Provide analytics support on key projects/initiatives aligned to business or organizational strategies.
Create technical documents for all newly built data/reporting solutions.
Serve as a subject matter expert across Finance and assist users with reporting and analysis activities and tools, including ad hoc reporting.
Support end-user training, testing, documentation, and roll-out of reports.
Maintain knowledge of business processes and application design and understand cross-functional business relationships and their use of information.
Work in conjunction with other internal teams on collaborative projects, including data analytics initiatives.
Keep current on emerging tools and techniques and grow knowledge of the business.


Who You Are


Undergraduate degree or post-secondary education in quantitative field (Computer Science, Finance, Mathematics, Analytics, Economics, etc.).
Microsoft Power BI or appropriate Analytics certification is desirable.
3+ years of experience developing Power BI dashboards ensuring security, architecture, governance, and performance meets outlined objectives.
3+ years of experience working with databases and warehouses, with hands-on expertise in SQL-Server (SSIS, SSRS, SSAS tabular models).
3+ years of experience in analytical languages, including DAX, M, BEx, Power Query, etc.
Familiarity with ML concepts, frameworks, and libraries along with the ability to execute these in Python/R is considered a strong asset.
Experience working with SAP BI/BW/BPC is considered a strong plus.
Attention to detail with high aptitude for problem-solving.
Excellent communication skills, proven ability to discuss BI solutions at the appropriate level of detail with all levels of the organization.
Ability to adjust to changing priorities and participation in multiple projects.
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office products (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Access).


Please note that we may use AI tools to help us through the recruitment process.

Our Commitment

We are an equal opportunity employer and are committed to providing employment accommodation in accordance with all applicable Provincial Human Rights and Accessibility Legislation. CCG will provide accommodations to job applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. If you require an accommodation, please notify us and we will work with you to meet your needs.

We're looking for a curious, analytical, and forward-thinking Business Intelligence & AI Analyst to help us transform data into meaningful insights, drive smarter decision-making, and explore innovative ways to use AI and automation across our organization.

What You'll Do

As our Business Intelligence & AI Analyst, you'll help shape how data and technology support strategic decision-making across the organization. In this role you will:


Build and maintain Power BI dashboards, executive scorecards, KPI reports, and performance metrics that help leaders make informed decisions.
Partner with teams across the organization to understand business needs and turn data into actionable insights.
Collect, integrate, validate, and analyze data from multiple systems and sources.
Identify trends, risks, opportunities, and performance improvement areas, and provide recommendations that drive results.
Translate complex information into compelling stories and visualizations that make data easy to understand.
Lead business intelligence and analytics projects from concept through implementation.
Explore opportunities to automate processes using tools such as Power Automate, Microsoft Copilot, and other AI-enabled solutions.
Support the responsible adoption of AI technologies, workflows, and agents across the organization.
Monitor and improve data quality, consistency, and governance practices.
Provide training and support to help teams get the most value from reporting and BI tools.
Stay ahead of industry trends and bring new ideas that help us continuously improve.


You Might Be a Great Fit If You...


Love solving problems and finding better ways to do things.
Can balance big-picture thinking with strong attention to detail.
Enjoy working with people from different teams and helping them succeed.
Are naturally curious about AI, automation, and emerging technologies.
Can explain complex concepts in a simple, engaging way.
Take initiative, manage projects effectively, and follow through on commitments.
Believe that collaboration, kindness, and accountability lead to great results.


What You'll Bring


Diploma or degree in Business, Data Analytics, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field.
3+ years of experience in business intelligence, data analytics, reporting, or a similar role.
Strong experience with Power BI, advanced Excel, SQL, and data visualization/reporting tools.
Proven ability to analyze complex data, develop actionable insights, and communicate findings to diverse audiences.
Experience designing and automating reporting processes, workflows, and business solutions using tools such as Power Automate.
Practical experience using AI tools such as Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, or similar technologies in a business environment.
Strong project management, stakeholder engagement, communication, and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities.


Why Brightisle?

Because people matter here.

We're committed to creating a workplace where employees feel supported, respected, and empowered to do their best work. We work hard, collaborate often, celebrate successes, and genuinely enjoy being part of a team that makes a difference.

Ready to help us Find a Better Way?

If you love dashboards, data stories, process improvement, and emerging technologies, we'd love to hear from you. This posting will remain open until we have found the one!

At Brightisle, we are committed to creating an accessible and inclusive hiring experience. If you require accommodations at any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know by emailing hr@brightisle.ca. We'd be happy to work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Enterprise Data Analyst
Description:
INTRODUCTION

At Specialty Program Group Canada (SPG Canada), we are insurance entrepreneurs united by a bold vision: to aggregate strengths and deliver unmatched value as Canada’s largest Delegated Underwriting Authority Enterprise (DUAE).




We exist to satisfy insurance needs in our target markets faster, better, and even where others might not. We do this by uniting leading underwriting talent and specialty insurance brands under one coast-to-coast platform, delivering speed, service, and expertise at a national scale.




But what truly sets us apart is our people. At SPG Canada, you will be part of a collaborative, growing team that values innovation, professional development, and shared success. Our culture is built on entrepreneurial thinking, open communication, and a strong sense of purpose. We believe in empowering our employees to take ownership, contribute ideas, and grow their careers within a supportive and dynamic environment.




Our legacy MGA brands include Cansure, i3 Underwriting, Totten Insurance Group, Beacon, Anderson McTague & Associates, Mode, and Avro. Our value to our partners is further enhanced through our in-house claims management team, Specialty Claims Canada.




Together, we are building more than a market leader — we are building a place where talented professionals can thrive.




SPGCanada.ca




OVERVIEW

We are looking for an Enterprise Data Analyst to join our Data & Intelligence team.




Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.




The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.

The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Data & Intelligence team, which is responsible for the data used by products delivered in partnership to the field. The Analyst will work cross-functionally to drive improvements in our Data-as-a-Product solutions and will support data testing, data quality, and data governance projects as directed.




This is a hybrid position based in our Vancouver, BC office.




RESPONSIBILITIES

In this position you will:

Support various data warehouse and platform enhancement initiatives — new subject areas, improved data quality, faster data availability, internal compliance, and simplified usage — contributing to the regular, iterative release of data within the SPGC ecosystem.
Liaise with IT and Operations teams to ensure technical and business solutions fully meet stakeholder needs for BI reporting, ad-hoc extracts, data science projects, and customer relationship management.
Support business, contractual, and compliance data needs by creating and maintaining reporting pipelines and delivering one-off reports to internal and external partners.
Perform data profiling; plan, execute, and review user acceptance test (UAT) cases. Establish minimum viable product and acceptance criteria for development sprints and undertake UAT for data-related projects.
Execute data projects assigned by your line manager, ensuring business requirements are properly understood and user stories are well crafted to enable efficient, accurate ETL/ELT development.
Develop and prioritize development stories, participate in Agile ceremonies, and contribute to after-action reviews for data projects.
Establish programmatic processes to proactively measure and manage data quality (ideally automated) using SQL and related languages; coordinate remediation of data defects and data quality problems.
Collaborate with the Data & Intelligence team to implement common data definitions and metadata documentation. Serve as an EDW and data-source subject matter expert.




QUALIFICATIONS AND CHARACTERISTICS

You’re a good fit if you have:

Bachelor’s degree in a related quantitative field, or 5 years of related insurance industry data experience.
Strong, hands-on SQL proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, and data validation.
Microsoft Fabric experience (Google Cloud data architecture such as BigQuery accepted as an alternative platform background).
Strong, hands-on SQL and Python proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, data validation, and transformation/automation work in Microsoft Fabric notebooks.
Power BI and DAX for reporting and dashboarding.
Practical exposure to data warehousing, business intelligence, data modeling, and master data management concepts.
Demonstrated SCRUM/Agile experience.
Understanding of data governance, data integration, and master data management best practices.
A consultative style that leverages both business and technical resources.

Strong assets (nice to haves):

Familiarity with the EPIC broker management system.
Exposure to NetSuite / NetSuite Analytics Warehouse.




BENEFITS

By joining Specialty Program Group Canada, you’ll become part of a rapidly growing company focused on providing innovative insurance solutions to support independent brokers in Canada. As a member of our team, you’ll benefit from:

Dental care
Extended health care
Paid time-off
RRSP match
Vision care




SALARY RANGE

The expected salary range for this position is from $90,000 to $100,000 and will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. Specialty Program Group Canada and affiliated brands are proud to offer comprehensive benefit and total compensation packages which include extended health and dental benefits, disability insurance, RRSP matching, paid time off benefits, and a variety of potential bonus opportunities.




APPLY TODAY

Interested applicants are asked to send your cover letter and resume to careers@spgcanada.ca. Please note that only those chosen for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Developer
Description:
Your Job

DarkVision, a Koch Engineered Solutions company, is looking for a Data (Python) Developer to join the Data Development Team in North Vancouver, BC. In this role, you will work collaboratively with teammates and other departments in a fast-paced, ever-changing environment to develop and advance the data analysis deliverables supporting the multi-million-dollar operations of our customers.




You will support the Data Analysis Delivery team by writing and maintaining scripts to automate their analysis and deliverable workflows. You will work with the Product team to prototype new deliverables (inspection reports and 3D visualizations), and work with the Engineering Team to diagnose issues that affect Data Analysis Deliverables, and suggest improvements.




This regular full-time position is 100% on-site in our HQ in North Vancouver, BC, where employees enjoy full access to our facility amenities including a well-equipped gym, squash court, climbing wall, steam room, and more!




Immigration sponsorship or support is not available for this position.




Our Team

DarkVision’s acoustic imaging technology captures terabytes of high-fidelity data with each run. Our Data Analysis Development team plays a critical role in the commercial process by supporting the Data Analysis Delivery team in building and maintaining tools to streamline the analysis workflow, advancing and improving new deliverables, and improving processes for quality assurance.




There will always be new software tools and scripts to write and improve, new technologies to exploit and generate deliverables on, and new ways to make processes better, faster, and more sustainable and scalable. This entails a detail-oriented mindset, a default-to-action work ethic, and a multi-disciplinary perspective.




What You Will Do

Develop and maintain scripts to automate analysis workflows.
Develop and maintain analysis dashboards for higher level tracking of project status and Key Performance Indicators.
Develop and maintain analysis procedures that streamline a sustainable and scalable workflow.
Work with other analysts, Sales, Product, and Engineering teams to document and investigate issues, and prevent their reoccurrence.
Gather and analyze test data that will be used to prove out feasibility of new technology and deliverable products.
Develop new and innovative ways of representing and visualizing current data to generate new customer deliverables.
Work with the Product team in the data gathering, analysis, and writing of technical conferences papers.
Help analysts debug issues in their analysis workflow, and reproduce, document, and raise issues for other functional teams.
Interface with our Research, Machine Learning, Software, and Product teams to ensure the Data Team’s needs are met, and vice versa.




Who You Are (Basic Qualifications)

Able to work 100% on-site in our North Vancouver office.
Experience developing production code in Python (code used in a product or professional setting, not academia- or research-only applications).
Experience developing workflow automation scripts (to automate manual steps performed by analysts).
Data visualization experience.
Git experience.




What Will Put You Ahead

Excellent communication skills.
Detail oriented; meticulous in documentation.
Default-to-action personality.
Independent - can operate with little guidance while providing results at regular intervals for feedback.
Collaborative - works to meet the needs and goals of the team.
Image processing experience.
MATLAB experience.
3D data experience.
3D rendering experience.
Proficiency in PowerBI.
Ultrasonic imaging experience.
Software and algorithm development experience.
Atlassian product experience.




General Salary Range

For this role, we anticipate paying $70,000 - $115,000 per year. This role is eligible for variable pay, issued as a monetary bonus or in another form.




At Koch companies, we are entrepreneurs. This means we openly challenge the status quo, find new ways to create value and get rewarded for our individual contributions. Any compensation range provided for a role is an estimate determined by available market data. The actual amount may be higher or lower than the range provided considering each candidate’s knowledge, skills, abilities, and geographic location. If you have questions, please speak to your recruiter about the flexibility and detail of our compensation philosophy.




Who We Are

DarkVision Technologies Inc. is a Canada-based tech company disrupting the industrial imaging market since 2013. We have created the world’s most advanced acoustic-based imaging platform. We are packaging it into multiple new product lines, revolutionizing how our clients quantify and visualize the integrity of their critical assets.




Backed by Koch Industries, one of the world’s largest privately held companies, DarkVision’s team of Mechanical, Skunkworks, Electrical, Software, and Machine Learning Engineers is rapidly expanding to meet the demand for the company’s current and upcoming products.




We allow employees to work on cutting-edge technologies that blend science with real-world applications. We invite you to join our team for the exciting journey ahead as we become the global leader in industrial imaging.




At Koch, employees are empowered to do what they do best to make life better. Learn how our business philosophy helps employees unleash their potential while creating value for themselves and the company.




Additionally, everyone has individual work and personal needs. We seek to enable the best work environment that helps you and the business work together to produce superior results.




How To Apply

If you have the above qualifications, we would like to hear from you. We thank all applicants in advance, but please be advised that only those selected for an interview will be contacted.




We are an equal opportunity employer. If you require accommodation or assistance at any time during the application or selection processes, please submit a request by following the directions located in the FAQ section at the bottom of the kochcareers.com webpage.




Successful candidates will be required to complete a criminal background check.

Title: Intermediate Business Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Fleetway is seeking a qualified Intermediate Data Analyst to join our Technical Publications team, which is leading the development of cutting-edge documentation for the next generation of Royal Canadian Navy ships. We offer a modern, collaborative environment that leverages advanced technology to produce high-quality technical publications.

As an Intermediate Data Analyst, you will play a key role in supporting Irving Shipbuilding’s strategic goals and operational efficiency by analyzing data, identifying business needs, and recommending actionable solutions. This position is central to the development and delivery of the Technical Data Package (TDP) for the River Class Destroyer Program.

You’ll work closely with cross-functional teams to coordinate and maintain TDP deliverables, metrics, and dashboards. Your insights will help stakeholders and leadership make informed decisions at both project and management levels.

We're committed to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Fleetway is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals, including those from underrepresented groups, and we strive to create an environment where everyone feels respected, supported, and empowered to thrive.

We encourage applicants to self-identify if they belong to an underrepresented group, as part of our commitment to equitable hiring practices and fostering a truly inclusive workforce.

If you require accommodations during the recruitment process, please let us know—we're happy to help.

Responsibilities


Collaborate with stakeholders to gather and document business requirements.
Analyze data to support the development and delivery of Technical Data Packages (TDPs).
Maintain dashboards and reports to track project progress and performance.
Support project planning, testing, and change management activities.
Identify process improvements and recommend data-driven solutions.
Ensure alignment with compliance standards and organizational goals.


Qualifications

Education


A post-secondary degree in Computer Science, Business, Information Systems, or a related field.


Skills & Experience


2–5 years of experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar role.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Ability to work collaboratively across teams and disciplines
Detail-oriented with a focus on accuracy and quality
Adaptable and proactive in a dynamic project environment


Due to the nature of our work, successful candidates must be eligible to obtain required government security clearances.

About Us

Founded in 1882, J.D. Irving, Limited (JDI) operates in Eastern Canada (New-Brunswick, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, Québec, Ontario, Newfoundland and Labrador), as well as in USA. We are a team of 20,000 dedicated employees. At J.D. Irving, Limited, you are part of a dynamic team of professionals who lead with integrity and are passionate about delivering results. With a culture that nurtures talent and creates an inclusive workplace, you can grow your career across our various operating divisions.

We are committed to creating accessible environments for our colleagues, candidates, and customers. Requests for accommodation due to a disability (which may be visible or invisible, temporary, or permanent) can be made at any stage of application and employment. We encourage candidates to make their accommodation needs known so that we can provide equitable opportunities.

Worried you do not meet all the requirements? Studies have shown that many applicants apply only to job opportunities when they feel they meet 100% of the criteria. We promote an inclusive environment where everyone is appreciated, recognized, and empowered to contribute fully. If you are interested in this position but are worried you did not meet all the requirements, we would encourage you to still apply.

Title: Data Analyst II, Data AI Analytics
Description:
Position Title: Data Analyst II, Data AI Analytics

Position Type: Regular - Full-Time

Requisition ID: 43381

In every role, McCainers are ambitious, curious, and passionate about creating exceptional work experiences - together. With a customer-first mindset, we make doing business with McCain easy.

About The Role.

The Data Analyst II plays a critical role in strengthening McCain’s enterprise data foundation as part of our digital transformation journey. This role is responsible for ensuring data is accurate, trusted, secure, and well-governed across the organization. By leading data quality initiatives, supporting metadata management, and operationalizing governance tools and policies, the Data Analyst II enables business teams to confidently leverage data for operational, reporting, and strategic decision-making.

What You’ll Be Doing.


Support and maintain enterprise data quality frameworks, ensuring accuracy, completeness, consistency, and timeliness of data
Perform data profiling, cleansing, and monitoring using modern tools, including AI-enabled anomaly detection
Investigate data quality issues and contribute to root-cause analysis and remediation efforts
Collaborate with business and technical teams to define and track data quality KPIs and validation processes
Document business rules, validation logic, and exception handling procedures for critical datasets
Maintain enterprise data catalog and lineage, ensuring alignment with system changes and business needs
Partner with data engineers, architects, and stakeholders to capture data flows, transformations, and lineage
Utilize governance tools and AI-driven features to improve data classification, tagging, and discoverability
Support onboarding and training efforts for data governance tools, including developing user guides and documentation
Provide day-to-day support to users, troubleshooting governance tool issues and answering questions


What You’ll Need To Be Successful.


Bachelor’s degree in Management Information Systems, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Information Technology, or related field
Minimum of 2 years of experience in data governance, data management, data engineering, or a related discipline
Strong understanding of metadata standards, data lineage, and data classification practices
Proficiency in SQL and/or Python for data profiling, validation, and analysis
Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams, including data owners, stewards, IT, and business stakeholders
Experience working with modern data platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake, or AWS
Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment with minimal supervision
Preferred: Knowledge of CPG data structures, including product hierarchies and regulatory requirements
Preferred: Experience with governance tools such as Ataccama, Syniti, Collibra, MS Purview, Informatica, or Alation
Preferred: Exposure to AI governance and responsible AI practices


About The Team.

The role sits within McCain’s Global Data Governance team, a group focused on enabling business and technical teams to adopt consistent, high-quality data practices across regions and functions. The team partners closely with data engineering, architecture, analytics, and security teams to drive enterprise-wide data governance standards and solutions.

About McCain.

Click Here to learn more about McCain and how we provide opportunities to make an impact that matters.

Leadership principles.

At McCain, our leadership principles guide how we engage with customers, collaborate as a team, and achieve success. We focus on understanding customer needs, driving innovation, empowering people, and taking ownership to clear obstacles and deliver results.

The McCain Experience.

We are McCain. This statement is a testament to our collective strength and our individual value. Your contributions play a vital role in our success. Our winning culture is rooted in authenticity and trust, empowering us to bring out the best in one another. Here, you’ll find opportunities to learn, grow, and thrive. Join us and experience why we’re better together.

Compensation Package: $52,600.00 - $70,200.00 . CAD annually

The above reflects the target compensation range for the position at the time of posting. Hiring compensation will be determined based on experience, skill set, education and training, and other organizational needs. This posting is for an existing vacancy.

Benefits: At McCain, we’re on a mission to create a winning culture that puts employee safety and well-being at the heart of what we do, every day. We understand and appreciate that each person’s needs are unique and ensure our benefits & well-being programs reflect that. Employees are eligible for the following benefits: health coverage (medical, dental, vision, prescription drug), retirement savings benefits, and leave support including medical, family and bereavement. Well-being programs include vacation and holidays, company-supported volunteering time, and mental health resources. Coverages are aligned with applicable law and can vary by work level, location, and nature of the role. Additional benefit detail available during the application process.

Your well-being matters to us, and we’re here to provide you with the necessary resources to support you in being your best self at work — and at home.

McCain Foods is an equal opportunity employer. As a global family-owned company, we strive to be the employer of choice in the diverse communities around the world in which we live and work. We recognize that inclusion drives our creativity, resilience, and success and makes our business stronger. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected characteristic under applicable law.

McCain is an accessible employer. If you require an accommodation throughout the recruitment process (including alternate formats of materials or accessible meeting rooms), please let us know, and we will work with you to find appropriate solutions.

Your privacy is important to us. By submitting personal data or information to us, you agree this will be handled in accordance with McCain’s Global Privacy Policy and Global Employee Privacy Policy, as applicable. McCain leverages AI in the hiring process, though all final decisions are made by humans. You can understand our approach to AI and how your personal information is being handled here.

Title: Enterprise Data Analyst
Description:
Job Details

Description

Job Title: Enterprise Data Analyst

Pure Hothouse Foods, marketed under the Pure Flavor® brand, is a family of greenhouse vegetable growers who share a commitment to bringing A Life of Pure Flavor™ to communities everywhere. Our passion for sustainable greenhouse growing, strong support for our retail & foodservice customers, and focus on engaging consumers is built on a foundation drawn from generations of growing expertise. We are the next generation of vegetable growers, inspired to put quality, flavor, and customers first by providing greenhouse-grown vegetables from our farms that are strategically located throughout North America.

With a dedicated, energetic and highly skilled workforce, we believe our employees make the difference. We offer our employees the opportunity to advance their careers in our progressive, vibrant organization.

Job Summary

The Enterprise Data Analyst designs, builds, and supports enterprise data solutions and system integrations that enable reliable reporting, automation, AI-enabled business outcomes, and data-driven decision making. This role develops and maintains data pipelines, integrates business applications via APIs/EDI/flat files, improves data quality and governance, and partners with stakeholders to deliver analytics and AI-enable initiatives. The analyst works closely with IT, vendors, and business teams to lead technical delivery, troubleshoot data and integration issues, and continuously improve the organization's data capabilities.

Duties & Responsibilities

Enterprise Data Platform/Initiatives/Application & Data Integration/Reporting & Analytics Enablement/Project Leadership & Delivery/Data Governance & Quality/Documentation, Collaboration & Continuous Improvement


Develop, maintain, and optimize enterprise data models, pipelines, and datasets to support reporting, analytics, and operational needs.
Monitor data pipeline health and performance; troubleshoot failures and implement preventative improvements.
Partner with stakeholders to translate business requirements into scalable data solutions and cleats, reliable metrics.
Identify, prototype, and deliver AI-enabled use cases (e.g. document extraction, forecasting, anomaly detection, and copilots) in collaboration with business stakeholders.
Establish repeatable patterns for safe AI adoption, including data readiness, evaluation, and operational hand-off.
Design, build, and support integrations between business applications using API’s, EDI, flat-file exchanges.
Implement monitoring, alerting, and operational procedures for integrations; triage incidents and coordinate fixes with internal teams and vendors.
Design, develop, publish, and maintain Power BI reports and dashboards, including data modeling, DAX measures, and performance optimization.
Administer and govern Power BI workspaces (structure, access/permissions, deployment practices, refresh schedules, gateways, and publishing standards) to ensure secure, reliable, and scalable reporting.
Gather reporting requirements, define KPIs, and support business users through dashboard enhancements, data validation, and guidance on self-service reporting.
Lead technical workstreams for data and integration projects, including scope definition, effort estimation, timelines, and risk/issue management. Coordinate with cross‑functional teams and vendors to deliver solutions; provide clear status updates and ensure production readiness.
Implement data quality validation, reconciliation, and exception handling to improve trust in enterprise reporting and downstream processes.
Maintain data definitions and access standards in partnership with stakeholders; support appropriate security, privacy, and retention requirements.
Create and maintain documentation for data products, integrations, runbooks, and troubleshooting procedure; share knowledge with IT and business teams.
Provide operational support for enterprise data and integration, including participation in and on-call rotation as required.
Other core expectations as defined by the company.


Requirements And Skills


Completion of secondary school (high school diploma or equivalent) is required.
Minimum of three(3) years of experience in data, analytics, integration, or related IT roles.
Ability to self-manage work and lead technical delivery from initiation through deployment and support.
Strong SQL skills and experience with data modelling and transforming data for analytics and reporting.
Experience developing and administering Power BI semantic models and reports/dashboards (including Power Query and DAX), plus workspace governance (permissions, publishing standards, refresh schedules, and gateways).
Experience developing data pipelines and automation using tools/languages such as SQL, PowerShell, Python, and/or JavaScript.
Experience with integration methods such as REST/SOAP APIs, EDI, and flat-file exchanges, including incident troubleshooting and coordination with vendors.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and the ability to produce clear technical documentation.
Ability to work effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders and communicate status, risks, and options clearly.
Ability to maintain strict confidentiality of sensitive information.
Experience with modern data platforms and BI tools(e.g. Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, Azure data services), including semantic modelling, report performance tuning, and BI administration/governance practices preferred.
Experience with the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate) and integrating workflows with data sources preferred.
Exposure to AI/ML concepts and experience working within the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC), including testing and release management preferred.


Work Environment


Hours of work may vary depending on business needs, flexibility required; ability and willingness to travel with no travel restrictions
May work within a refrigerated warehouse, where temperatures range from 45-55°F. The noise level in the work environment can be loud due to packaging machinery, and forklift truck operation
Role routinely uses standard office equipment such as computers, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets and fax machines. Specific vision capabilities required; employee is to work in front of a computer, sitting for prolonged periods
The position may involves lifting to 30-pounds, occasionally up to 50-pounds


Compensation & Benefits

This position offers a salary range of $72,000–$85,000 CAD, with final compensation varying based on experience, skills, internal equity, and location. In addition, Pure Flavor offers a competitive total rewards package, including company-paid benefits, RRSP matching, and paid time off.

If you are looking for a challenging and rewarding career opportunity, submit your resume to Pure Hothouse Foods today!

Pure Flavor Farms is committed to providing an accessible and barrier-free environment. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. Please contact the Human Resources department if an accommodation is required.

Check us out at www.pure-flavor.com or follow us on Facebook, Twitter, Instagram or LinkedIn.

The team is dedicated to innovation and sustainability, contributing to impactful industry advancements.

In this role you can expect to have the responsibilities:

  • Develop and maintain systems for quality data management.
  • Create dashboards, reports, and visualizations to convey quality performance.
  • Oversee collection and validation of quality metrics for decision-making.
  • Analyze data trends and KPIs for improvements and risk management.
  • Collaborate with teams to identify trends and suggest optimizations.
  • Aid in the integration of automated quality inspection systems.
  • Conduct training on data-driven quality management practices.

This Role Comes With The Following Benefits

  • Competitive salary based on experience
  • Group insurance plan with health spending account
  • Paid vacation
  • Company-paid break from Christmas to New Year's
  • Matching RRSP program

This Role Requires You To Have

  • Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Data Science, Quality Management, Statistics, or related field
  • Experience in data analysis with Power BI, Tableau, SQL, Python
  • Proficiency in statistical analysis tools
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills

You Would Benefit From Having

  • Experience with quality management software
  • Familiarity with ERP systems

Smartrend Manufacturing seeks individuals driven by passion and dedication to quality, offering an environment of growth and professional satisfaction. Selected candidates will be contacted for an interview.

Title: Business & Data Analyst
Description:
Classification & Regular Hours




Hours per Week: 35




Salary Grade: 14




The base salary will be $67,680 - $96,590 per annum. Starting salary will be aligned with the successful candidate’s experience, skills, and demonstrated qualifications.




About Western

Since 1878, Western University has been committed to serving our communities through the pursuit of academic excellence and by providing students, faculty, and community members with life-long opportunities for intellectual, social, and cultural growth. We seek excellent students, faculty, and staff to join us in what has become known as the "Western Experience" - an opportunity to contribute to a better world through the development of new knowledge, new abilities, new connections, and new ways to make a difference.




About Us

Western Housing is a self-funded ancillary within the Division of Housing and Ancillary Services, with annual revenues in excess of $65 million and a staff of 60 full-time and more than 200 student employees. The eleven residences are occupied by students and fully operational throughout the academic year on a 24/7 basis. Western’s rental accommodations are comprised of 2 apartment buildings, 19 three-story walk-up apartment buildings and 170 townhouses, with annual revenues of $10,821,200, with 900 leases.




HASIT aims to implement secure, cost effective, and innovative computing technology solutions for our business partners. HASIT provides attentive, friendly, high-quality technical support to our customers, solving their issues in a timely fashion.




Responsibilities

The Business & Data Analyst is responsible for analyzing Housing’s activities and operations to inform strategic decision-making and define effective solutions. Under the direction of both the Manager of Residence Admissions and the Software Development Manager, this role translates business challenges into actionable stakeholder requirements and gathers data from Housing processes and information systems to provide accurate and timely insights.




The Analyst bridges Housing Admissions and the Housing and Ancillary Services IT team (HASWEB) to create or optimize business processes while facilitating communication across various departmental teams. Beyond managing day-to-day housing data systems, this role optimizes data capture, produces visual metrics and dashboards, and supports the development and analysis of student satisfaction assessments. The Business & Data Analyst actively contributes to the admissions process to ensure optimal housing occupancy levels.




Qualifications




Education:




Undergraduate degree in a related field
Social Science, Data Science, Information Technology/Computer Science preferred




Experience:




4 years of experience in the following:

Experience in business analysis and data analysis
Reporting including working with large data sets, data visualization, and statistical measures
Previous experience with project management




Preferred

Previous work experience in an academic environment institution
Previous experience with StarRez, Microsoft PowerBi, Tableau, SQL and/or other business intelligence tools
Developing and implementing data analyses, data collection systems and other strategies that optimize statistical efficiency and quality
Experience building visual presentations, dashboards, and other data visualization techniques




Knowledge, Skills & Abilities:




Knowledge of business systems analysis, data storage, and information retrieval techniques
Ability to elicit requirements and align solutions to strategic priorities
Knowledge of project management, business analysis, and change management principles, tools, and methods
Ability to map business processes and create de documentation
Advanced knowledge of Excel and common data formats
Knowledge of data structures and SQL query techniques
Knowledge of key statistical concepts and methods essential to finding structure in data
Knowledge of diverse methodologies to get insight from the data
Knowledge of quantitative and qualitative research methodologies and analyses
Familiar with best practices in maintaining sensitive and confidential data
Familiarity with accounting principles would be considered an asset
Ability to analyze business risk and recommend improvements in processes
Ability to deal with data anomalies such as missing values, outliers, unbalanced data, and data normalization
Ability to produce concise and accessible analysis of complex data through the effective use of charts and tables
Attention to detail with an ability to retrieve, analyze and synthesize sizable amounts of data while ensuring that information is accurate and comprehensive
Ability to collaborate across internal and external boundaries to meet common objectives, improve outcomes and support work beyond one's own unit
Verbal communication skills to clearly express ideas in an objective manner, and adapt communication style to suit the situation and audience
Proven ability and natural inclination to develop relationships by interacting with people in a professional, respectful, and diplomatic manner
Ability to work independently and effectively as a member of the team to achieve department goals
Exceptional organizational skills to manage competing priorities and an ability to adapt quickly to emerging priorities
Possess a reputation for resourcefulness with a strong sense of accountability and initiative




Western Values Diversity

The University invites applications from all qualified individuals. Western is committed to employment equity and diversity in the workplace and welcomes applications from women, members of racialized groups/visible minorities, Indigenous persons, persons with disabilities, persons of any sexual orientation, and persons of any gender identity or gender expression.




Accommodations are available for applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. If you require accommodations for interviews or other meetings, please contact Human Resources or phone 519-661-2194.




Please Note:

This position is an existing vacancy. This position is fully in-person, on-site. We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only those chosen for an interview will be contacted.




Interested applicants are asked to visit https://recruit.uwo.ca for further information and to apply online referencing job #43785 by 11:59PM on July 20, 2026

Title: Data Analyst (Head Office)
Description:
Description

Position at Performance Auto Group

Drive for Excellence

Looking to join a fast-paced and an ever expanding organization? Be a part of our dynamic team at Performance Auto Group! We are currently seeking a Data Analyst to join our team.

The Data Analyst plays a pivotal role in driving strategy and decision-making across Performance Auto Group. By leveraging SQL, Python, and Google Suite, this role translates complex datasets into actionable business intelligence, creating dashboards and reports that inform key stakeholders.

Working collaboratively across departments, the Data Analyst identifies performance trends, ensures data integrity, and optimizes analytical methodologies to support organizational growth.

In this role, maintaining meticulous order is essential. Beyond technical analysis, the Data Analyst must implement rigorous, structured data management practices to ensure accuracy, facilitate reproducibility, and guarantee that complex information remains accessible and reliable for the entire organization.

About Us

Performance Auto Group, is a leading automotive group, whose purpose is to be the preferred automotive group by fostering fair and respectful relationships that positively impact our team, customers, partners, and communities. Our core values as an organization are the principles we were built on that guide us today:

Great Experience: Every customer is entitled to a great experience

Integrity: We are honest and transparent in our actions

Diversity: We are stronger through the inclusion of people, brands and ideas

Innovation: We passionately embrace opportunities for new and continuous improvements

Empowerment: We encourage and support our people in taking initiative

Giving Back: We positively impact the communities in which we live, work and raise families

Here’s What You’ll Deliver


Lead the ongoing evolution of our measurement and analysis tools and processes;
Manage tracking codes and pixels, along with related technology, throughout our marketing channels;
Mine data from multiple systems and provide comprehensive analysis and insights;
Work collaboratively with team members to deliver actionable data in friendly, digestible formats, including dashboards and reports;
Assist with the development and execution of data-driven marketing campaigns, working with vendors and other team members to execute;
Actively participate in team meetings to drive improvements to campaign performance and overall website metrics;
Other duties as assigned.


What do you need to succeed?


Post-secondary degree/Diploma in Computer Science or related field required.
3-5 years of work experience as a Data Analyst or in a related field is preferred.
Proficient with Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager, and exposure to emerging tracking and data collection tools.
Experience with Google Data Studio or experience utilizing data visualization tools is highly desirable.
Must have a valid Ontario Driver's License and good driving record.
Proficient with SQL and Python
Expert command of spreadsheets and reporting tools.
Strong attention to detail, with excellent organization and project management skills.
Driven yet collaborative - you work well in an engaged, busy team environment.
Savvy and up-to-date with current digital marketing practices.
Proactive, organized, and results driven.
Proficient verbal and written communication skills.


What’s in it for you?


Excellent management support and guidance
Opportunities through our Internal career development program
Access to health, dental and vision insurance
Disability, critical illness and life insurance for the unexpected
Our Employee Assistance Plan (EAP) for you and your family's wellness
Discount on vehicles, service and parts for you and your family
Discounts to retailers and service providers across the country
Free and engaging employee events
Did we mention we love giving back to our communities?!


Job Type: Full Time - Permanent

Compensation (expected annual salary): $60,000 - $70,000 per year. The starting salary will be set within this range based on job-related factors such as relevant skills and experience.

Performance Auto Group is a leading automotive company in Ontario with over 2,000 employees, 23 brands and 44 locations in Brampton, Brantford, Bolton, Grimsby, Mississauga, Orangeville, St. Catharines, Huntsville and Toronto. Performance Auto Group also includes AutoPlanet, Canada’s largest indoor used car showroom.

This recruitment process involves the use of artificial intelligence, and this job posting is for an existing vacancy.

Performance Auto Group is an equal opportunity employer. Accommodations are available throughout the recruitment and selection process in accordance with applicable legislation. We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Power BI Developer
Description:
About The Role

Job Overview:

As a Power BI Developer, you will play a dual role—developing impactful business intelligence solutions while also building strong relationships with potential clients. You will design, develop, and maintain business analytics dashboards and reports using Power BI to provide actionable insights for clients. Additionally, you will help explain the value of Power BI and other technology solutions to prospective clients, ensuring they understand how our offerings can solve their business challenges.

Key Responsibilities

Client Engagement: Establish and maintain relationships with clients, understanding their data analytics needs and offering customized BI solutions.

Solution Development: Design, create, and manage Power BI reports and dashboards that transform raw data into meaningful insights.

Data Analysis: Work with clients to analyze business data, identify trends, and provide solutions that improve business performance.

Requirement Gathering: Collaborate with clients to understand their needs and translate them into effective Power BI solutions.

Technical Expertise: Advise clients on the best Power BI practices, data modeling, DAX expressions, and report optimization.

Sales Collaboration: Work closely with the sales team to present Power BI solutions to prospective clients, demonstrating the value of data-driven decision-making.

Continuous Improvement: Stay up-to-date with Power BI features, and incorporate new tools and methodologies to improve client solutions.

Training: Provide client training sessions and documentation to ensure users can efficiently navigate and use Power BI tools.

Lead Generation Support: Assist in generating leads through strategic conversations and presentations that highlight our BI solutions.

Requirements

Qualifications:


Proven experience (3+ years) working as a Power BI Developer or Business Intelligence Specialist.
Strong technical skills in Power BI, including data modeling, DAX (Data Analysis Expressions), Power Query, and visualization best practices.
Experience with data integration from multiple data sources like SQL, Excel, and cloud-based databases.
Strong understanding of database management and ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes.
Proven ability to understand client business challenges and develop technical solutions that address these issues.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain technical solutions to non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to manage multiple projects and client relationships simultaneously.
Familiarity with business process analysis and the ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights.


About The Company

BI Control is a company that offers a wide range of innovative services to drive the success and growth of organizations.

We are a forward-thinking startup providing cutting-edge business technology solutions to help organizations optimize their operations. Our services empower companies with the data insights needed to drive better decision-making and growth.

We are looking for a passionate Power BI Developer to join our growing team and engage with clients, building solutions that drive business performance.

Apply Now
