Title: Analyst, Insights and Pipeline Management
Description:
Job Title: Analyst Insights and Pipeline Management

Vacancies: This posting is for (1) new role.

Location: Vancouver, BC | Victoria, BC | Ottawa, ON | Calgary, AB | Halifax, NS | St John's, NL | Kelowna, BC| Prince George, BC (Detailed office location information can be found by visiting this link: https://cancer.ca/en/contact-us)

Work Model: Hybrid Work Model

Salary Band: 3 ($52,000 - $59,000 CAD)

HELP SHAPE THE FUTURE OF CANCER CARE IN CANADA

The Canadian Cancer Society works tirelessly to save lives, improve lives and drive collective action against cancer. Together with patients, volunteers, donors and communities across the country, we raise funds to invest in transformative cancer research, we provide a caring support system for everyone affected by cancer and we advocate to governments to create a healthier future for all. It takes a society to take on cancer – and the Canadian Cancer Society is leading the way

MAKING AN IMPACT

Under the direction of the Director, Insights and Pipeline Management, the Analyst supports the delivery of fundraising analytics, and pipeline management services for the Corporate Partnerships team. This role supports organizational fundraising by designing and maintaining data reporting, optimizing fundraiser portfolios, and ensuring the integrity and strategic use of prospect data.

What You’ll Be Doing


Data Analysis
Use Salesforce and analytics/visualization tools to generate insights that support strategic, data‑informed fundraising selection.
Retrieve, analyze, and interpret data to support performance management of prospect pools, pipelines, portfolios, forecasting, and fundraiser activity.
Act as the Corporate team’s lead representative in DAS project discussions.
Serve as the Salesforce subject‑matter expert for the Corporate Giving team, including managing updates, creating and editing reports, supporting process changes, and building campaigns.
Manage the time‑sensitive Gifts of Securities (GOS) process in accordance with CCS Gift Acceptance Policy, ensuring accuracy and compliance.
Prospect Development and Reporting
Implement prospect development strategies and data integrity initiatives; design and produce regular and ad‑hoc audit reports to ensure accuracy of ratings, stages, assignments, proposals, and prospect groups.
The Analyst keeps up to date on market trends and best practices to ensure continual service improvements.
Create efficiencies by streamlining tools, processes, and reports based on feedback from colleagues and observance of data input results. Identify opportunities to leverage technology to facilitate information delivery and work alongside the DAS team to enact updates.
Maintain and update the Business Procedures and Policies document to reinforce consistency in data and analytics practices that can also serve as a reference guide for fundraisers and securing required approvals.
Support fundraiser pipeline management
Provide recommendations to fundraising staff on how to interpret, comprehend, and leverage data visualization and predictive data model scores for the benefit of prospect discovery and pipeline management.
Enable quarterly pipeline review meetings with fundraisers to review portfolios to ensure fundraising activities are on track and moving through the solicitation cycle. Initiate and drive regular meetings with fundraisers to review pipelines to ensure data cleanliness prior to quarterly reviews.
Monitor portfolio composition and performance; provide recommendations on portfolio size, stage distribution, moves management, fundraising plans, and prospect stagnation or sunsetting.
Design and contribute toward training sessions on new reports and tools to fundraisers to ensure that KPIs and financial goals are achieved and data competency is maintained. Utilize strong data analysis and data presentation skills to present information clearly to staff at different levels of the department.
Drive corporate record clean‑up initiatives to strengthen authority-driven linkages and eliminate duplicate records across industry sectors.
Conduct lead‑generation and prospecting activities to refresh the corporate donor pool and identify new or existing prospects within the database.
Other duties as assigned
Contribute to our culture of diversity, inclusion, belonging and equity (DIBE) by ensuring that all staff feel represented, valued, and heard across all aspects of their identity, including gender, age, religion, ethnicity, nationality, race, and sexuality.


Qualifications


Demonstrated experience retrieving, interpreting, and synthesizing complex datasets using Salesforce and analytics/visualization tools to support evidence‑based decision‑making.
Hands‑on experience creating and modifying reports, managing data updates, supporting process changes, and serving as a subject matter expert for business users.
Experience working with technical teams (e.g., data/analytics) and frontline fundraisers to translate business needs into actionable insights, tools, and process improvements.
Experience implementing data‑integrity initiatives, conducting portfolio/pipeline reviews, and supporting prospect management strategies in a fundraising or CRM‑driven environment.
Ability to interpret complex data, identify trends, diagnose issues in pipelines or portfolios, and recommend clear, actionable solutions.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present data clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences and guide fundraisers in applying insights.
Commitment to accuracy, consistency, and governance in data entry, reporting, and documentation.
Ability to work effectively with fundraisers, leadership, and technical teams, fostering trust and shared ownership of data‑driven practices.
Bilingualism (French/English) is highly preferred, with French being an asset due to the organization’s nationwide operations and the need for effective communication across various regions.
Others may apply


What You Can Expect From Us

CCS provides impactful opportunities to transform the lives of Canadians affected by cancer. We are devoted to creating an inclusive workplace that celebrates diverse experiences and skills, encouraging all qualified candidates to join us. Our inspiring culture is rooted in our core values and commitment to meaningful change:

COURAGEOUS UNITED CARING RIGOROUS

We offer a competitive salary, excellent benefits, including paid parental leave, family sick time, and health insurance—and a fulfilling work environment where your efforts make a meaningful impact daily.

COME AS YOU ARE

At CCS, we celebrate individuality and the strength of diversity. We are committed to fostering an inclusive and equitable workplace that empowers everyone. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates, including those from equity-deserving groups such as BIPOC, 2SLGBTQI+ communities, individuals with disabilities, veterans, and others who contribute to the richness of our organization. Together, we unite Canadians to shape the future of cancer care.

How To Apply

Qualified candidates are invited to submit their resume, cover letter and salary expectations by July 27th, 2026.

We thank all candidates for their interest and advise that only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Other Information

CCS is dedicated to employment equity and encourages applications from all qualified candidates . In accordance with the local provincial Accessibility Act, accommodation will be provided as requested throughout the recruitment process . We want to make the interview process a great experience for you!

Please note that in keeping with the mandate of CCS to model and promote healthy lifestyles , employees are not permitted to smoke in or about CCS premises or while carrying out CCS business.

Privacy Disclosure

We collect your personal information through forms, by phone or in person to evaluate your candidacy for the role(s) you have applied for, to contact you regarding your candidacy, and to generate recruitment-related reports. If selected for a position at CCS the information provided will be used for the purposes of pre-employment checks and added to your employee file. We may share your personal information with third parties, including recruitment consultants, within or outside your province or territory or outside Canada to carry out the purposes identified above, or as required by law. We may contact you by mail, email, phone or text. You can exercise your right to access your information or have it corrected, unsubscribe from communications or withdraw your consent by selecting these options within the ADP system, or by contacting privacy@cancer.ca . For more information about our privacy practices, visit cancer.ca/privacy.

AI Disclosure

We are committed to transparency and, the responsible use of technology in our hiring process. Artificial intelligence (AI) tools may be used to assist with certain administrative or screening tasks; however, all hiring decisions are made by people. We review and approve AI-assisted outputs to ensure fairness, accuracy, and alignment with our values. For more information about our privacy practices, visit cancer.ca/privacy .

Title: Financial Data Analyst
Description:
About Northlake Financial

Northlake Financial team is growing! If you are a committed and collaborative professional looking to contribute to a hard-working, innovative team, this opportunity is for you.

Why Work for us?

Northlake understands the needs of its employees. Northlake Financial is revolutionizing the Canadian automotive finance industry with a full spectrum of dynamic rates and services for dealers. We specialize in the acquisition and servicing of prime and subprime automotive financing contracts. Our employees flourish in an exciting and engaging environment.

Purpose of a Data Analyst

The Data Analyst is responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data to support strategic and operational decision-making across the organization. This role requires a strong analytical mindset and the ability to leverage data, statistical methods, and technology to identify trends, improve business performance, and provide actionable insights to leadership.

Number of Positions Available: 1

Office Location: Barrie, ON (This role does not provide remote or hybrid work).

Responsibilities of a Data Analyst


Utilize database management tools to perform data mining and analysis.
Design, develop, and maintain data models, dashboards, and reporting solutions.
Analyze data and present findings, trends, and recommendations to management.
Generate and distribute daily, weekly, monthly, and ad hoc reports for department leaders.
Conduct data integrity reviews and ensure the accuracy and reliability of reporting.
Monitor key performance indicators and identify opportunities for operational improvement.
Manage relationships with third-party IT and technology service providers.
Participate in corporate strategic initiatives and cross-functional projects.
Ensure all reporting deadlines and deliverables are met in accordance with established timelines.
Identify and implement continuous improvement opportunities related to data collection, reporting, and analysis.
Consistently act in a manner that reflects positively on the organization and complies with company policies and procedures.


Requirements of a Data Analyst


Ability to work on-site at the designated company location.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Demonstrated attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Strong organizational and time-management skills.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment.
Commitment to continuous learning and professional development.
Customer-focused approach with a high level of accountability and professionalism.


Qualifications Of a Data Analyst


College diploma, university degree, or higher in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Information Technology, Business Analytics, or a related field.
Minimum 2 years of experience in a data analysis, reporting, or business intelligence role.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel.
Experience working with databases, SQL, and data visualization/reporting tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
Knowledge of statistical analysis and data modeling techniques.
Experience creating dashboards, reports, and data-driven recommendations for business stakeholders.
Familiarity with data governance and data integrity best practices is considered an asset.


Compensation of a Data Analyst


Base Salary starting at $45,000 annually 
Paid Wellness Days
Your Birthday Off
Comprehensive Benefits Package including health and dental coverage 
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for mental health and wellness support 
Bonusly employee recognition and rewards platform 


Northlake Financial appreciates all interest in this opportunity. We are an equal opportunity employer, and all employment decisions are based on business needs, job requirements, and individual qualifications. We do not discriminate on any grounds protected by applicable federal, provincial, or territorial human rights legislation.

Northlake Financial is committed to providing reasonable accommodation to applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment process, in accordance with applicable accessibility legislation. Applicants requiring accommodation are encouraged to notify the hiring manager.

About Northlake Financial

Northlake Financial team is growing! If you are a committed and collaborative professional looking to contribute to a hard-working, innovative team, this opportunity is for you.

Why Work for us?

Northlake understands the needs of its employees. Northlake Financial is revolutionizing the Canadian automotive finance industry with a full spectrum of dynamic rates and services for dealers. We specialize in the acquisition and servicing of prime and subprime automotive financing contracts. Our employees flourish in an exciting and engaging environment.

Purpose of a Data Analyst

The Data Analyst is responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data to support strategic and operational decision-making across the organization. This role requires a strong analytical mindset and the ability to leverage data, statistical methods, and technology to identify trends, improve business performance, and provide actionable insights to leadership.

Number of Positions Available: 1

Office Location: Barrie, ON (This role does not provide remote or hybrid work).

Responsibilities of a Data Analyst

  • Utilize database management tools to perform data mining and analysis.
  • Design, develop, and maintain data models, dashboards, and reporting solutions.
  • Analyze data and present findings, trends, and recommendations to management.
  • Generate and distribute daily, weekly, monthly, and ad hoc reports for department leaders.
  • Conduct data integrity reviews and ensure the accuracy and reliability of reporting.
  • Monitor key performance indicators and identify opportunities for operational improvement.
  • Manage relationships with third-party IT and technology service providers.
  • Participate in corporate strategic initiatives and cross-functional projects.
  • Ensure all reporting deadlines and deliverables are met in accordance with established timelines.
  • Identify and implement continuous improvement opportunities related to data collection, reporting, and analysis.
  • Consistently act in a manner that reflects positively on the organization and complies with company policies and procedures.

Requirements of a Data Analyst

  • Ability to work on-site at the designated company location.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Demonstrated attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong organizational and time-management skills.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment.
  • Commitment to continuous learning and professional development.
  • Customer-focused approach with a high level of accountability and professionalism.

Qualifications Of a Data Analyst

  • College diploma, university degree, or higher in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Information Technology, Business Analytics, or a related field.
  • Minimum 2 years of experience in a data analysis, reporting, or business intelligence role.
  • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel.
  • Experience working with databases, SQL, and data visualization/reporting tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
  • Knowledge of statistical analysis and data modeling techniques.
  • Experience creating dashboards, reports, and data-driven recommendations for business stakeholders.
  • Familiarity with data governance and data integrity best practices is considered an asset.

Compensation of a Data Analyst

  • Base Salary starting at $45,000 annually 
  • Paid Wellness Days
  • Your Birthday Off
  • Comprehensive Benefits Package including health and dental coverage 
  • Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for mental health and wellness support 
  • Bonusly employee recognition and rewards platform

Title: Intermediate Mining Data Analyst
Description:
Be part of something incredible. Metals are necessary for our world, but mining historically faced issues with waste, sustainability, and inefficiencies. MineSense is changing that. Our solutions reduce mining waste and environmental impacts while increasing profits and efficiencies. Backed by the best in venture capital and government funds, and recognized by tech industry and sustainability programs, MineSense is doing what many said could not be done: improving mining for people and planet.







We are looking to add an Intermediate Mining Data Analyst to our GeoScience team. The role will report to the Manager, GeoScience and be based out of the Vancouver office. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in detailed data analysis and statistics within the mining industry. You will work with clients globally, to incorporate MineSense solutions into their ore control processes and show them the value of using our systems. If you enjoy working with data, collaborating with multidisciplinary groups, and have a mining, geology, or geophysics background, we want to hear from you!







Job Responsibilities:



Helping new and existing clients leverage MineSense technology in their grade control engineering processes, which includes developing client facing reports and presentations.


Collaborating with internal teams to develop prediction models by correlating mine grade measurements or estimates with our sensor results to identify patterns, trends or other data features that relate to grade, texture, mineralogy, or lithology.


Working with the Field Service and Product Development team to further enhance our products and services through system data analysis and data quality assurance/control.


Determining and tracking data model accuracy and precision through comparison with sampled assay grades or grade estimates.


Reporting and presenting data results/trends to internal teams, as well as external clients.


Working closely with the Data team to create queries, scripts, and reports while ensuring data is well organized and maintained.


Building and maintaining good working relationships with our clients.


Promotes a positive company culture through sharing of information and supporting the learning and development of all colleagues.


Additional duties as occasionally required by your manager.









Qualifications:



Degree in Earth Sciences (Geology, Geophysics), Geostatistics, Data Analytics, Mining Engineering or related field.


Minimum of 2-4 years of relevant working experience, where experience in mining is preferred.


Experience performing statistical analysis or data science in a work setting is preferred.


Mining experience with in-grade control or working with ore control models is preferred.


Experience with data analysis tools such as python or other statistical packages.


Experience with SQL is preferred.


Experience and passion for solving analytical problems involving diverse data sets using quantitative approaches to generate insights.


Attention to detail and an interest in problem solving.


Oral and written proficiency in English is required.


Additional oral and written proficiency in Spanish is strongly preferred.


The ability to travel and support global offices is required (10-30%).


Legally able to work in Canada.












The salary offered to the successful candidate will be based on their experience, knowledge and skills they bring to MineSense, market data based on their location as well as internal equity. Keeping this mind, the salary offered can be outside of the range posted. 

 

Our total rewards package offers market-researched benefits, promotes internal equity, and supports team members at all levels of our organization, and includes the following:  

 

Our total rewards package includes: 

·  A competitive salary and bonus.

·  Matching RRSP or 401K program (for Canadian and US employees)

·  A comprehensive benefit program including an employee assistance program. 

·  Learning and career development programs and resources (live sessions and on-demand)

·  Transit Subsidy (for roles based in Vancouver, BC)

·  Hybrid work (for roles that do not require to be onsite)

·  3 weeks' vacation or more depending on the position

·  Global and local company organized social events and celebrations to connect with your team

 

Here's what you can expect as a part of the MineSense Team: 

 

When you join MineSense, a trailblazing Canadian mining technology company, you become a part of our purpose to maximize global metals and minimize global impacts. We’re not just a company; we’re a Community of Innovators who forge a path toward a sustainable future. Here’s why we want you to be excited: 




Purpose-Driven: At MineSense, we don’t just clock in—we live our purpose. We’re on a mission to revolutionize the mining industry, and every day, we’re one step closer to a cleaner, greener world. 

Values That Resonate: Our core values—Innovation, Belonging, Resilience, and Adding Value—aren’t just buzzwords. They’re the bedrock of our culture. When you step into our team, you’re not just an employee; you’re a steward of these values. 

Impactful Work: Imagine contributing to a cleaner, more sustainable planet. At MineSense, you’re shaping the future of cleantech and developing cutting-edge technology that literally hasn't been developed before. Your work matters, and it echoes beyond our walls. 

People-Centric: We’re not just about profits; we’re about people. Our organization cares deeply about its employees. When you join us, you become part of a community that supports growth, well-being, and success. 

 

Ready to make your mark? Join MineSense and be part of something bigger than yourself. 

  

MineSense is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing accommodation to individuals with disabilities. Should you require accommodation during the recruitment or hiring process, please feel free to indicate your needs while applying. Information received related to accommodation needs of applicants will be addressed confidentially. 

  

All applications are welcome, even if you don't meet all the requirements, so please do not hesitate to apply. 

 







We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Business Analyst (Asset Management focus)
Description:
Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM) is inviting applications for two position for Business Analyst (Asset Management focus) in Finance & Asset Management.




Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM) is committed to reflecting the community it serves & recognizing Diversity & Inclusion as one of the Municipality’s core values. We have an Employment Equity Policy & we welcome applications from African Nova Scotians & Other Racially Visible Persons, Women in occupations or positions where they are underrepresented in the workforce, Indigenous/Aboriginal People, Persons with Disabilities & 2SLGBTQ+ Persons. Applicants are encouraged to self-identify on their electronic application.




Reporting to the Manager Asset Management Systems, the Business Analyst is responsible for monitoring and improving the quality of data stored in the asset management systems, collecting new and updated data, and providing advice and support to end users.




Working as part of the Asset Management Systems team, the Analyst works closely with teams throughout the municipality to provide dedicated support to capital projects, operational activities, and data collection exercises. They are trusted partners that link the day-to-day operations of the municipality with the long-term strategic direction of the Asset Management policy, helping ensure that municipal assets are well managed.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:




Business Unit Partnerships

Develop relationships within operational business units to share asset management knowledge, stay aware of asset-related initiatives, and proactively advance asset management priorities.




Asset Data Management

Evaluate existing asset data using criteria for completeness, quality, and currency
Produce and distribute standardized reports outlining the quality of the asset data and highlighting priority areas for improvement
Investigate and correct discrepancies and errors in the data
Assist in the development of new or changed asset data types
Keep current with new developments in the asset management systems, asset management practices, and GIS tools
Collaborate with the Asset Management team and management in the establishment of long-term strategic directions for asset data collection




Asset Data Collection

Provide technical and process assistance to data collection exercises led by other departments
Lead data collection exercises carried out by the Asset Management Office, including collection of data from existing sources and in the field
Evaluate technical options for data collection and make recommendations




System Configuration

Provide technical advice on the configuration of the asset management systems in relation to the GIS
Liaise with the Information Technology department to evaluate proposed changes to the GIS and advise on impacts on the asset management systems




Process Management & Documentation

Evaluate existing processes related to the management of asset data and provide advice and recommendations on improvements
Develop new processes as required
Document new and existing processes
Work closely with employees at all levels of the organization to identify opportunities for improvements
Monitor and report on the outcome of processes




Training & Support

Provide training to users of the asset management systems. This may be as informal one-on-one training or structured group sessions
Create training materials
Respond to user inquiries and problem reports
May perform other related duties as assigned




QUALIFICATIONS

Education & Experience

Diploma or Degree at the post-secondary level in a discipline related to spatial data, asset management, or public administration.
3+ years of experience in managing spatial data and performing/supporting data collection
3+ years experience developing reports using tools such as Crystal Reports, Excel, or Power BI
3+ years experience providing customer service and support to non-technical users
Some experience with building strong relationships with stakeholders including senior management, clients, and technical staff
Translating business needs into technical requirements and solutions
Performing education / training
Some experience with process design, development, and implementation
Basic project management skills
General knowledge and some formal training in Business Analysis Skills and Asset Management is preferred




Technical/Job Specific Knowledge and Abilities

Proficiency in Esri tools and technology
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel
Good understanding of reporting and presentation tools
Good understanding of general data management practices
General understanding of business process analysis

Security Clearance Requirements: Applicants may be required to complete employment security screening check




Please note–Testing may be conducted as a component of the selection process to assess technical & job specific knowledge. Candidates who are selected for testing may be tested in a group setting scheduled at the employer’s discretion.




Competencies - Valuing Diversity, Analytical Thinking, Communications ,Decision Making ,Managing Change ,Organizational Awareness ,Risk Management ,Teamwork & Cooperation ,Value & Ethics ,Visioning, Strategic Thinking & Innovation




WORK STATUS: Permanent, Full time




Hours of Work: Monday to Friday, 8:30 am-4:30 pm 35 hours/week




SALARY: Non-Union,NU6 ($73,094-$94,587). Salary will be determined based on relevant experience and qualifications related to the specified pay band and job. Placement above the midpoint of the pay band is typically reserved for candidates who significantly exceed the minimum qualifications, such as those with exceptional experience, specialized skills, or demonstrated achievements relevant to the role. This ensures fair and consistent compensation practices across the organization.




WORK LOCATION: 40 Alderney Drive, Dartmouth




CLOSING DATE: Applications will be received up to 11:59pm on Tuesday, July 21, 2026.




Please note: We thank all applicants for their interest in this position. Only those applicants selected for interview/testing will be contacted.




To ensure a fair and equitable hiring process, candidates are expected to complete all parts of the recruitment process, including assessments, assignments, and interviews independently and without the use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools or other forms of external assistance. The use of AI to complete any component of the hiring process is not permitted and may result in disqualification from consideration.




Shortlisted candidates will be required to provide proof of their qualifications, including, but not limited to, educational credentials, professional licenses, and relevant certifications.




During the recruitment process, applicants have the right to request an accommodation. Applicants invited to participate in an assessment process (such as interview or testing) and who require an accommodation, should discuss their needs with the Talent Acquisition Advisor when invited to the assessment process. For more information on our accommodation process please click on the link; Accommodations | Hiring | Employment | Halifax.

Title: Evaluation and Measurement Analyst
Description:
Overview

At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

The Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst is responsible for assessing the effectiveness and impact of learning programs by supporting the design of measurement strategies, analyzing training data, and providing actionable insights to improve learning outcomes and business performance. This role works closely with course owners, SMEs, and stakeholders to ensure training delivers measurable value.

Data Collection & Analysis


Collect and validate data from multiple sources (e.g., LMS, surveys, assessments)
Analyze quantitative and qualitative data to identify trends, gaps, and improvement opportunities
Perform statistical analysis (e.g., pass rates, item difficulty, distractor analysis) to assess assessment quality


Reporting & Insights


Review dashboards and reports to communicate training performance and outcomes
Translate data into clear, actionable insights for course owners and stakeholders
Provide recommendations for course improvements based on findings


Continuous Improvement


Monitor training effectiveness throughout the lifecycle (pre-, during, and post-delivery)
Partner with SMEs and course owners to refine content, assessments, and delivery methods
Support post-course evaluations and follow-up analysis (e.g., behavior and performance impact)


What You Will Do

As a Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst, you will play a key role in helping KPMG Business School understand and improve the effectiveness of its learning programs. Working closely with course owners, subject matter experts (SMEs), and other stakeholders, you will transform learning data into meaningful insights that drive better learning experiences and business outcomes.

In this role, you will


Collect, validate, and analyze data from multiple sources, including learning management systems, surveys and assessments.
Identify trends, patterns, learning gaps, and opportunities for improvement through quantitative and qualitative analysis.
Conduct measurement and evaluation activities, including assessment analysis, learner feedback reviews, pass rate analysis, item difficulty reviews, and other evaluation methodologies.
Maintain reporting dashboards and metrics to monitor learning effectiveness and learner performance.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and recommendations for stakeholders and course owners.
Support the evaluation of learning initiatives throughout the training lifecycle, from pre-deployment through post-course analysis.
Collaborate with course owners and SMEs to identify opportunities to improve course content, assessments, learner engagement, and overall learning impact.
Contribute to the development and continuous improvement of evaluation frameworks, measurement strategies, and reporting standards.
Support the preparation and presentation of findings, recommendations, and evaluation results to various stakeholders.
Assist with special projects and initiatives related to learning analytics, measurement, and continuous improvement.


What You Bring To The Role

You are a curious and analytical professional who enjoys turning data into meaningful insights. You have a passion for learning and development and are motivated by the opportunity to improve learning experiences through evidence-based decision-making.

You bring


3 years experience in learning analytics, training evaluation, data analysis, research, or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify trends, interpret data, and make practical recommendations.
Experience working with data visualization and reporting tools such as Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
Knowledge of learning measurement and evaluation methodologies, such as the Kirkpatrick Model or other evaluation frameworks, is an asset.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and quality.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills, with the ability to work across teams and influence decision-making through data-driven insights.
Experience working with Learning Management Systems (LMS) and learning technologies is considered an asset.
A continuous improvement mindset and a passion for enhancing learning outcomes through measurement and evaluation.


KPMG Ontario Region Pay Range Information

The expected base salary range for this position is $55,000 to $82,000 and may be eligible for bonus awards. The determination of an applicant’s base salary within this range is based on the individual’s location, skills & competencies, and unique qualifications. In addition, KPMG offers a comprehensive and competitive Total Rewards program.

KPMG BC Region Pay Range Information

The expected base salary range for this position is $55,000 to $82,000 and may be eligible for bonus awards. The determination of an applicant’s base salary within this range is based on the individual’s location, skills & competencies, and unique qualifications. In addition, KPMG offers a comprehensive and competitive Total Rewards program.

The Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst is responsible for assessing the effectiveness and impact of learning programs by supporting the design of measurement strategies, analyzing training data, and providing actionable insights to improve learning outcomes and business performance. This role works closely with course owners, SMEs, and stakeholders to ensure training delivers measurable value.

Data Collection & Analysis

  • Collect and validate data from multiple sources (e.g., LMS, surveys, assessments)
  • Analyze quantitative and qualitative data to identify trends, gaps, and improvement opportunities
  • Perform statistical analysis (e.g., pass rates, item difficulty, distractor analysis) to assess assessment quality

Reporting & Insights

  • Review dashboards and reports to communicate training performance and outcomes
  • Translate data into clear, actionable insights for course owners and stakeholders
  • Provide recommendations for course improvements based on findings

Continuous Improvement

  • Monitor training effectiveness throughout the lifecycle (pre-, during, and post-delivery)
  • Partner with SMEs and course owners to refine content, assessments, and delivery methods
  • Support post-course evaluations and follow-up analysis (e.g., behavior and performance impact)

What You Will Do

As a Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst, you will play a key role in helping KPMG Business School understand and improve the effectiveness of its learning programs. Working closely with course owners, subject matter experts (SMEs), and other stakeholders, you will transform learning data into meaningful insights that drive better learning experiences and business outcomes.

In this role, you will

  • Collect, validate, and analyze data from multiple sources, including learning management systems, surveys and assessments.
  • Identify trends, patterns, learning gaps, and opportunities for improvement through quantitative and qualitative analysis.
  • Conduct measurement and evaluation activities, including assessment analysis, learner feedback reviews, pass rate analysis, item difficulty reviews, and other evaluation methodologies.
  • Maintain reporting dashboards and metrics to monitor learning effectiveness and learner performance.
  • Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and recommendations for stakeholders and course owners.
  • Support the evaluation of learning initiatives throughout the training lifecycle, from pre-deployment through post-course analysis.
  • Collaborate with course owners and SMEs to identify opportunities to improve course content, assessments, learner engagement, and overall learning impact.
  • Contribute to the development and continuous improvement of evaluation frameworks, measurement strategies, and reporting standards.
  • Support the preparation and presentation of findings, recommendations, and evaluation results to various stakeholders.
  • Assist with special projects and initiatives related to learning analytics, measurement, and continuous improvement.

What You Bring To The Role

You are a curious and analytical professional who enjoys turning data into meaningful insights. You have a passion for learning and development and are motivated by the opportunity to improve learning experiences through evidence-based decision-making.

You bring

  • 3 years experience in learning analytics, training evaluation, data analysis, research, or a related field.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify trends, interpret data, and make practical recommendations.
  • Experience working with data visualization and reporting tools such as Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
  • Knowledge of learning measurement and evaluation methodologies, such as the Kirkpatrick Model or other evaluation frameworks, is an asset.
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and quality.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
  • Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills, with the ability to work across teams and influence decision-making through data-driven insights.
  • Experience working with Learning Management Systems (LMS) and learning technologies is considered an asset.
  • A continuous improvement mindset and a passion for enhancing learning outcomes through measurement and evaluation.

Title: Business Intelligence Engineer, IT PnD Data and Analytics
Description:
Description

IT PnD Data and Analytics is seeking a Business Intelligence Engineer to join our BI team.

As a Business Intelligence Engineer with our team, you will be working in a large, complex and dynamic, data-driven environment. You will be responsible for developing automated metrics that provide relevant insight to organizational leaders, building and maintaining dashboards, and performing complex data analytics.

This position requires the candidate to develop a deep understanding of the organization and key goals, earn trust with senior leadership, influence without authority, and provide relevant data insights.

To succeed, you must have the ability to engage deeply with diverse and talented business partners, earn trust using facts and data, and advise and influence decisions of senior business leaders through effective verbal and written communication.

This candidate must possess excellent analytical skills, project management experience, and a track record of delivering results in a complex and ambiguous environment.

Key job responsibilities


Perform complex business analysis to help drive organizational decision-making
Complete root cause analysis and provide recommendations to senior leadership to address opportunities
Develop automated metrics that will give senior business leaders insight into the performance of their respective teams and the organization as a whole
Develop and maintain Tableau dashboards
Develop key metrics and shared goals that determine success for a particular area
Take on shared ownership of the weekly organizational metrics scorecard


A day in the life

A day in the life of a BI engineer at IT PnD involves building large-scale reporting solutions using tools like Tableau and QuickSight. The day typically starts with a team stand-up to align on priorities, followed by deep work on developing and refining data transformation, building reports and automating processes. Collaboration with data scientists, analysts, and stakeholders is key to ensure the delivery of right metrics. The day ends with monitoring, code reviews, and documenting progress, all while keeping scalability, efficiency, and Amazon’s high standards for data quality in mind.

Basic Qualifications


3+ years of analyzing and interpreting data with Redshift, Oracle, NoSQL etc. experience
Experience with data visualization using Tableau, Quicksight, or similar tools
Experience with data modeling, warehousing and building ETL pipelines
Experience writing complex SQL queries
Experience in Statistical Analysis packages such as R, SAS and Matlab
Experience using SQL to pull data from a database or data warehouse and scripting experience (Python) to process data for modeling


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in the data/BI space
Experience in analyzing and interpreting data with Redshift, Oracle, NoSQL etc.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

The base salary range for this position is listed below. As a total compensation company, Amazon's package may include other elements such as sign-on payments and restricted stock units (RSUs). Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, basic life & AD&D insurance), Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP), Deferred Profit Sharing Plan (DPSP), paid time off, and other resources to improve health and well-being. We thank all applicants for their interest, however only those interviewed will be advised as to hiring status.

Title: Senior Data Analyst - GFT Halifax
Description:
Job Description

What is the Opportunity?

The Metadata/Data Lineage team is undertaking multiple complex enterprise-wide initiatives as part of RBC’s ongoing plan to improve data governance and data lineage management across the organization. In this role you will be responsible for strategically planning and managing successful implementation of data lineage extraction and documentation. The role will coordinate, develop, lead, communicate and execute activities to ensure objectives are accomplished on time.

What Will You Do?


Analyze, validate, and document data lineage from complex legacy systems across AML, Risk, Compliance, Finance, and Wealth Management
Design and execute SQL queries and Python scripts to extract data relationships and lineage information from undocumented or minimally documented legacy systems
Build and maintain reusable frameworks and tools that scale across multiple systems and departments
Research and prototype AI/agent-based approaches to automate data lineage discovery
Collaborate cross-functionally with business teams, data engineers, and platform teams to understand data flows and validate lineage
Work independently to identify blockers, develop solutions, and escalate issues appropriately
Perform data quality assessments and identify gaps or inconsistencies in lineage data
Document findings and approaches to help scale lineage discovery to other systems across the bank


What Do You Need to Succeed?

Must Have


3-5 years of data analysis, data engineering, or software development experience
Strong SQL and Python programming skills for data extraction, parsing, and tool development
Experience with regular expressions and Bash/Unix scripting for data manipulation
Demonstrated ability to learn new systems quickly, especially legacy or undocumented code
Internal locus of control: ability to identify and remove your own blockers and drive solutions independently


Nice to Have


Experience with AI/agent frameworks, LLMs, and multi-model LLM concepts (MCP)
Familiarity with mainframe systems, JCL, or other legacy enterprise systems
Experience with data modeling, schema design, or metadata management
GitHub Actions or CI/CD pipeline deployment experience


What’s in it for You?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
A world-class training program in financial services
Flexible work/life balance options
Opportunities to do challenging work


Job Skills

Big Data Management, Cloud Computing, Database Development, Data Mining, Data Warehousing (DW), ETL Processing, Group Problem Solving, Quality Management, Requirements Analysis

Title: Inventory Analyst
Description:
Reporting to the Merchandise Manager, the Inventory Analyst is responsible for ensuring inventory is in the right place, at the right time, to maximize sales, maintain inventory health, improve inventory productivity, and support an exceptional customer experience across all channels. This role owns retail allocations, inventory movement, purchase order allocation, and inventory reporting while partnering closely with Merchandising, Planning, Retail, Wholesale and Operations to drive inventory performance.




Must be based in Vancouver, BC

Hybrid: 3 days in office, 2 days remote

Salary band: $65,000 - $80,000 CAD




Core Responsibilities

Allocation & Inventory Management

Own the day-to-day health of inventory across all channels, proactively identifying stock imbalances, inventory risks and opportunities, and taking action to optimize inventory productivity.
Execute daily and weekly inventory allocations across retail stores to maximize sales and inventory productivity.
Monitor store inventory levels, sell-through and weeks of supply to ensure inventory is balanced across the retail channel.
Identify opportunities to rebalance inventory through inter-store, cross-channel and country transfers.
Support store grading and size-level allocations to ensure inventory is distributed effectively.

Inventory Operations

Manage oversell reporting and coordinate timely resolution with cross-functional teams.
Execute inventory transfers between Canada and the US to support demand across all channels.
Manage country splits on purchase orders, ensuring inventory is allocated accurately prior to receipt.
Maintain inventory accuracy by investigating discrepancies and partnering with Operations to resolve issues.
Support inventory reconciliations and ongoing inventory health initiatives.

Inventory Analysis & Reporting

Produce weekly inventory reporting highlighting key risks, opportunities and recommendations.
Develop and maintain inventory health dashboards, tracking key metrics across retail, wholesale and ecommerce.
Monitor inventory productivity, stock availability, sell-through and weeks of supply across all channels.
Analyze sales and inventory trends to make recommendations that improve allocation decisions.
Support seasonal inventory forecasting and pre-season allocation planning.
Provide reporting and insights to support merchandising, planning and executive decision-making.

Cross-Functional Partnership

Partner closely with Merchandising, Planning, Retail, Wholesale, Operations and Logistics to execute inventory strategies.
Communicate inventory risks, opportunities and recommendations proactively.
Support new store openings, wholesale launches, promotional events and other cross-functional initiatives.
Continuously look for opportunities to improve inventory processes, reporting and operational efficiencies.

Key Traits & Experience We're Looking For
Bachelor's degree in Business, Commerce or a related field.
3–5 years of experience in allocation, inventory management, merchandise planning or a related retail role.
Strong understanding of retail inventory management, allocation principles and retail math.
Advanced Excel skills with the ability to analyze large datasets and translate data into actionable insights.
Experience working with ERP and inventory management systems is considered an asset.
Highly organized with exceptional attention to detail and accuracy.
A proactive problem solver who enjoys identifying opportunities and continuously improving processes.
Able to prioritize multiple competing deadlines in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.
Strong communication skills with the ability to build collaborative relationships across cross-functional teams.
Curious, analytical and driven by using data to make informed business decisions.

Title: Data Analyst, Governance & Resilience
Company: West Edmonton Mall Property Inc. (West Edmonton Mall and Fantasyland Hotel)
Description:
The Data Governance & Resilience Analyst is responsible for discovering, cataloging, and classifying organizational data assets to support security, governance, compliance, business continuity, and AI readiness.

This role operates within the information security and risk management domain, with a primary focus on data governance and oversight. The position is responsible for identifying where data resides, understanding what data exists across the organization, and ensuring it is appropriately classified. It supports compliance by assessing data against operational requirements, regulatory obligations, and risk management standards, helping safeguard the organization’s information, assets and maintain strong governance practices.

The role collaborates closely with the data analytics team and other subject matter experts to ensure accurate data discovery and alignment across data sources.

Primary Responsibilities

Data Discovery & Asset Management


Conduct systematic data discovery and inventory across on-premises, cloud, and hybrid environments to identify and catalog all data repositories.
Map data flows between systems to support impact analysis and architectural decision-making.
Maintain an authoritative data inventory and data lineage documentation to ensure visibility remains current as systems, integrations, and data flows evolve.


Data Governance & Classification


Implement and maintain data classification frameworks aligned with organizational risk appetite and regulatory requirements.
Establish governance policies, roles, and accountability structures in collaboration with data owners.
Define data quality standards and support the tracking of metrics for completeness, accuracy, and consistency in collaboration with data owners and system teams.
Facilitate data stewardship activities and enforce metadata management practices.


Compliance & Regulatory Alignment


Ensure data handling practices comply with federal and provincial privacy legislation and sector-specific regulations, in collaboration with Legal, Privacy, Security, and IT stakeholders.
Document data processing activities, retention schedules, and disposal procedures for regulatory requirements.
Support audit and compliance reporting with evidence of governance controls.


Business Continuity & Data Availability


Assess criticality of data assets to support Business Impact Analysis (BIA) and recovery prioritization.
Monitor data availability metrics and coordinate with IT teams to address resilience gaps.
Identify data dependencies for critical business processes to support continuity planning.


AI Readiness Assessment


Identify data gaps, bias risks, or compliance barriers to responsible AI adoption.
Establish data readiness scoring frameworks and document governance, ethics, and risk considerations for AI-enabled applications.


Desired Qualifications


A diploma or bachelor's degree in Information Management, Information Systems, Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or a related field is required.
Three (3) to five (5) years of experience in data governance, information management, compliance roles is desired.
Foundational knowledge of data governance frameworks, data classification schemes, and privacy regulations such as PIPEDA, GDPR.
Familiarity with data catalog platforms, metadata management tools, SQL databases, and cloud data storage environments is an asset.
Strong analytical and documentation skills with the ability to create clear process maps, data flow diagrams, and governance documentation.
Effective written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders and collaborate across IT, security, legal, and business teams.
Industry certifications such as CDPSE or CIPP/C are considered an asset.


Terms Of Employment


This is a full-time position based on a 37.5 hour work week from Monday through Friday, with potential participation in after-hour duties as required.
This position requires a physical presence at the office, with opportunities for hybrid work based on team availability and management discretion.
This is a salaried position, commensurate with experience and qualifications.
Must be able to pass a basic security clearance.

The Data Governance & Resilience Analyst is responsible for discovering, cataloging, and classifying organizational data assets to support security, governance, compliance, business continuity, and AI readiness.

This role operates within the information security and risk management domain, with a primary focus on data governance and oversight. The position is responsible for identifying where data resides, understanding what data exists across the organization, and ensuring it is appropriately classified. It supports compliance by assessing data against operational requirements, regulatory obligations, and risk management standards, helping safeguard the organization’s information, assets and maintain strong governance practices.

The role collaborates closely with the data analytics team and other subject matter experts to ensure accurate data discovery and alignment across data sources.

Primary Responsibilities

Data Discovery & Asset Management

  • Conduct systematic data discovery and inventory across on-premises, cloud, and hybrid environments to identify and catalog all data repositories.
  • Map data flows between systems to support impact analysis and architectural decision-making.
  • Maintain an authoritative data inventory and data lineage documentation to ensure visibility remains current as systems, integrations, and data flows evolve.

Data Governance & Classification

  • Implement and maintain data classification frameworks aligned with organizational risk appetite and regulatory requirements.
  • Establish governance policies, roles, and accountability structures in collaboration with data owners.
  • Define data quality standards and support the tracking of metrics for completeness, accuracy, and consistency in collaboration with data owners and system teams.
  • Facilitate data stewardship activities and enforce metadata management practices.

Compliance & Regulatory Alignment

  • Ensure data handling practices comply with federal and provincial privacy legislation and sector-specific regulations, in collaboration with Legal, Privacy, Security, and IT stakeholders.
  • Document data processing activities, retention schedules, and disposal procedures for regulatory requirements.
  • Support audit and compliance reporting with evidence of governance controls.

Business Continuity & Data Availability

  • Assess criticality of data assets to support Business Impact Analysis (BIA) and recovery prioritization.
  • Monitor data availability metrics and coordinate with IT teams to address resilience gaps.
  • Identify data dependencies for critical business processes to support continuity planning.

AI Readiness Assessment

  • Identify data gaps, bias risks, or compliance barriers to responsible AI adoption.
  • Establish data readiness scoring frameworks and document governance, ethics, and risk considerations for AI-enabled applications.

  • A diploma or bachelor's degree in Information Management, Information Systems, Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or a related field is required.

  • Effective written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders and collaborate across IT, security, legal, and business teams.

  • This position requires a physical presence at the office, with opportunities for hybrid work based on team availability and management discretion.

Title: Business Intelligence & AI Analyst with Brightisle
Place: St John’s, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
Description:
Business Intelligence & AI Analyst

Turn Data into Insight. Turn Ideas into Action.

At Brightisle, we believe great work happens when people are empowered to think differently, collaborate openly, and enjoy what they do. Our values aren't just words on a wall; they guide how we work every day:

Find a Better Way – We encourage Bright ideas and nurture innovation, challenging the status quo

Make It Happen – We see challenges as opportunity and stay focused on solutions.

Everyone Matters – We value people and look for the good in everyone.

Be Honest and Kind – We are ethical, truthful and kind. A little humility goes a long way.

Have Fun – We seek opportunities enjoy laughter and happiness every day.

We're looking for a curious, analytical, and forward-thinking Business Intelligence & AI Analyst to help us transform data into meaningful insights, drive smarter decision-making, and explore innovative ways to use AI and automation across our organization.

What You'll Do

As our Business Intelligence & AI Analyst, you'll help shape how data and technology support strategic decision-making across the organization. In this role you will:


Build and maintain Power BI dashboards, executive scorecards, KPI reports, and performance metrics that help leaders make informed decisions.
Partner with teams across the organization to understand business needs and turn data into actionable insights.
Collect, integrate, validate, and analyze data from multiple systems and sources.
Identify trends, risks, opportunities, and performance improvement areas, and provide recommendations that drive results.
Translate complex information into compelling stories and visualizations that make data easy to understand.
Lead business intelligence and analytics projects from concept through implementation.
Explore opportunities to automate processes using tools such as Power Automate, Microsoft Copilot, and other AI-enabled solutions.
Support the responsible adoption of AI technologies, workflows, and agents across the organization.
Monitor and improve data quality, consistency, and governance practices.
Provide training and support to help teams get the most value from reporting and BI tools.
Stay ahead of industry trends and bring new ideas that help us continuously improve.


You Might Be a Great Fit If You...


Love solving problems and finding better ways to do things.
Can balance big-picture thinking with strong attention to detail.
Enjoy working with people from different teams and helping them succeed.
Are naturally curious about AI, automation, and emerging technologies.
Can explain complex concepts in a simple, engaging way.
Take initiative, manage projects effectively, and follow through on commitments.
Believe that collaboration, kindness, and accountability lead to great results.


What You'll Bring


Diploma or degree in Business, Data Analytics, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field.
3+ years of experience in business intelligence, data analytics, reporting, or a similar role.
Strong experience with Power BI, advanced Excel, SQL, and data visualization/reporting tools.
Proven ability to analyze complex data, develop actionable insights, and communicate findings to diverse audiences.
Experience designing and automating reporting processes, workflows, and business solutions using tools such as Power Automate.
Practical experience using AI tools such as Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT, or similar technologies in a business environment.
Strong project management, stakeholder engagement, communication, and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities.


Why Brightisle?

Because people matter here.

We're committed to creating a workplace where employees feel supported, respected, and empowered to do their best work. We work hard, collaborate often, celebrate successes, and genuinely enjoy being part of a team that makes a difference.

Ready to help us Find a Better Way?

If you love dashboards, data stories, process improvement, and emerging technologies, we'd love to hear from you. This posting will remain open until we have found the one!

At Brightisle, we are committed to creating an accessible and inclusive hiring experience. If you require accommodations at any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know by emailing hr@brightisle.ca. We'd be happy to work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Data Analyst II, Fraud
Description:
Job Description:

At Remitly, we believe everyone deserves the freedom to access, move, and manage their money wherever life takes them. Since 2011, we've tirelessly delivered on our promise to customers sending money globally, providing secure, simple, and reliable ways to manage their money, ensuring true peace of mind.

Whether it's supporting loved ones back home, growing a business across continents, or pursuing new opportunities abroad, we're not just here to move money— we're here to move our global customers forward.

We're looking for builders, reimaginers, and global thinkers who want to work at the intersection of technology, trust, and transformation. If that's you and you're ready to do the most meaningful work of your career—we invite you to join over 2,800 passionate Remitlians worldwide who are united by our vision to transform lives with trusted financial services that transcend borders

About the Role:

As a Data Analyst, Fraud, you will be part of Remitly's Identity & Trust Analytics Team. Your mission is to leverage analytical methods to identify, prevent, and mitigate fraud impacting our customers globally. You will develop a deep understanding of Remitly's fraud detection systems, regional risk profiles, and operational processes to improve fraud defenses while maintaining a seamless experience for legitimate customers. You'll partner with Product, Engineering, Business Management, and Compliance to customize and optimize fraud models and workflows, integrating data from legacy and new platforms. This hybrid role requires 2–3 days per week in the Vancouver office.

You Will:


Become an expert on Remitly's fraud data (e.g. chargebacks, promo abuse, scams, onboarding risk).
Build data-driven models and dashboards to analyze and monitor fraud exposure, balancing customer experience with operational efficiency.
Collect, normalize, and analyze data from legacy prebriefs, transaction logs, and onboarding systems to identify fraud trends and behavior anomalies.
Partner with Business Management and Product to continuously improve customer verification, KYC, KYB and compliance workflows.
Design and evaluate experiments and A/B tests to measure fraud control effectiveness and minimize false positives.
Develop and track risk metrics that quantify fraud exposure, policy compliance, and system performance.
Provide actionable insights and influence the development of fraud detection strategies, tools, and automation.


You Have:


3+ years in an analytics or fraud/risk analysis role within fintech, e-commerce, or digital payments.
Experience with fraud or trust analytics.
Bachelor's degree in mathematics, engineering, economics, or another quantitative discipline (or equivalent experience).
Proficiency with SQL and hands-on experience analyzing large datasets (Python, R, or statistical modeling experience preferred).
Strong understanding of fraud taxonomies (chargebacks, identity theft, scams, onboarding risk, and promo abuse).
Demonstrated ability to distill business problems, develop hypotheses, perform analysis, and communicate clear recommendations.
Excellent data storytelling and communication skills; ability to translate complex findings into business decisions.


Compensation Details. The starting base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $125000. In Canada, Remitly employees are shareholders in our Company and equity is part of our total compensation plan. Your recruiter can share more information about medical benefits offered, as well as other financial benefits and total compensation components offered with this role.

Our Benefits


Four weeks vacation
Health Benefits
Mental Health & Family Forming Benefits
RRSP plan with company match
Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)
Life Insurance & Disability
Continuing education and travel benefits


Our Connected Work Culture: Driving Innovation, Together

At Remitly, we believe that true innovation sparks when we come together. Our Connected Work Culture fosters dynamic in-person collaboration, where ideas ignite and challenging problems find solutions faster. For corporate team members, we have an in-office expectation of at least 50% of the time monthly, typically achieved by coming in three days a week. This creates a consistent, meaningful overlap that supports team norms and business needs. Managers also have the flexibility to set higher expectations based on their team's specific needs. These intentional in-office moments are vital for deepening relationships, fueling creativity, and ensuring your impact is felt where it matters most.

Remitly is an E-Verify Employer

At Remitly, we are dedicated to ensuring that our workplace offers equal employment opportunities to all employees and candidates, in full compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

Remitly is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Title: Enterprise Data Analyst
Description:
INTRODUCTION

At Specialty Program Group Canada (SPG Canada), we are insurance entrepreneurs united by a bold vision: to aggregate strengths and deliver unmatched value as Canada’s largest Delegated Underwriting Authority Enterprise (DUAE).




We exist to satisfy insurance needs in our target markets faster, better, and even where others might not. We do this by uniting leading underwriting talent and specialty insurance brands under one coast-to-coast platform, delivering speed, service, and expertise at a national scale.




But what truly sets us apart is our people. At SPG Canada, you will be part of a collaborative, growing team that values innovation, professional development, and shared success. Our culture is built on entrepreneurial thinking, open communication, and a strong sense of purpose. We believe in empowering our employees to take ownership, contribute ideas, and grow their careers within a supportive and dynamic environment.




Our legacy MGA brands include Cansure, i3 Underwriting, Totten Insurance Group, Beacon, Anderson McTague & Associates, Mode, and Avro. Our value to our partners is further enhanced through our in-house claims management team, Specialty Claims Canada.




Together, we are building more than a market leader — we are building a place where talented professionals can thrive.




SPGCanada.ca




OVERVIEW

We are looking for an Enterprise Data Analyst to join our Data & Intelligence team.




Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.




The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.

The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Data & Intelligence team, which is responsible for the data used by products delivered in partnership to the field. The Analyst will work cross-functionally to drive improvements in our Data-as-a-Product solutions and will support data testing, data quality, and data governance projects as directed.




This is a hybrid position based in our Vancouver, BC office.




RESPONSIBILITIES

In this position you will:

Support various data warehouse and platform enhancement initiatives — new subject areas, improved data quality, faster data availability, internal compliance, and simplified usage — contributing to the regular, iterative release of data within the SPGC ecosystem.
Liaise with IT and Operations teams to ensure technical and business solutions fully meet stakeholder needs for BI reporting, ad-hoc extracts, data science projects, and customer relationship management.
Support business, contractual, and compliance data needs by creating and maintaining reporting pipelines and delivering one-off reports to internal and external partners.
Perform data profiling; plan, execute, and review user acceptance test (UAT) cases. Establish minimum viable product and acceptance criteria for development sprints and undertake UAT for data-related projects.
Execute data projects assigned by your line manager, ensuring business requirements are properly understood and user stories are well crafted to enable efficient, accurate ETL/ELT development.
Develop and prioritize development stories, participate in Agile ceremonies, and contribute to after-action reviews for data projects.
Establish programmatic processes to proactively measure and manage data quality (ideally automated) using SQL and related languages; coordinate remediation of data defects and data quality problems.
Collaborate with the Data & Intelligence team to implement common data definitions and metadata documentation. Serve as an EDW and data-source subject matter expert.




QUALIFICATIONS AND CHARACTERISTICS

You’re a good fit if you have:

Bachelor’s degree in a related quantitative field, or 5 years of related insurance industry data experience.
Strong, hands-on SQL proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, and data validation.
Microsoft Fabric experience (Google Cloud data architecture such as BigQuery accepted as an alternative platform background).
Strong, hands-on SQL and Python proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, data validation, and transformation/automation work in Microsoft Fabric notebooks.
Power BI and DAX for reporting and dashboarding.
Practical exposure to data warehousing, business intelligence, data modeling, and master data management concepts.
Demonstrated SCRUM/Agile experience.
Understanding of data governance, data integration, and master data management best practices.
A consultative style that leverages both business and technical resources.

Strong assets (nice to haves):

Familiarity with the EPIC broker management system.
Exposure to NetSuite / NetSuite Analytics Warehouse.




BENEFITS

By joining Specialty Program Group Canada, you’ll become part of a rapidly growing company focused on providing innovative insurance solutions to support independent brokers in Canada. As a member of our team, you’ll benefit from:

Dental care
Extended health care
Paid time-off
RRSP match
Vision care




SALARY RANGE

The expected salary range for this position is from $90,000 to $100,000 and will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. Specialty Program Group Canada and affiliated brands are proud to offer comprehensive benefit and total compensation packages which include extended health and dental benefits, disability insurance, RRSP matching, paid time off benefits, and a variety of potential bonus opportunities.




APPLY TODAY

Interested applicants are asked to send your cover letter and resume to careers@spgcanada.ca. Please note that only those chosen for an interview will be contacted.
