Title: Enterprise Data Analyst
Description:
INTRODUCTION

At Specialty Program Group Canada (SPG Canada), we are insurance entrepreneurs united by a bold vision: to aggregate strengths and deliver unmatched value as Canada’s largest Delegated Underwriting Authority Enterprise (DUAE).




We exist to satisfy insurance needs in our target markets faster, better, and even where others might not. We do this by uniting leading underwriting talent and specialty insurance brands under one coast-to-coast platform, delivering speed, service, and expertise at a national scale.




But what truly sets us apart is our people. At SPG Canada, you will be part of a collaborative, growing team that values innovation, professional development, and shared success. Our culture is built on entrepreneurial thinking, open communication, and a strong sense of purpose. We believe in empowering our employees to take ownership, contribute ideas, and grow their careers within a supportive and dynamic environment.




Our legacy MGA brands include Cansure, i3 Underwriting, Totten Insurance Group, Beacon, Anderson McTague & Associates, Mode, and Avro. Our value to our partners is further enhanced through our in-house claims management team, Specialty Claims Canada.




Together, we are building more than a market leader — we are building a place where talented professionals can thrive.




SPGCanada.ca




OVERVIEW

We are looking for an Enterprise Data Analyst to join our Data & Intelligence team.




Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.




The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric are core to this role, along with Power BI. Experience with Google Cloud data architecture (e.g., BigQuery) is an accepted alternative platform background. Experience in the insurance industry is preferred.

The Enterprise Data Analyst will work collaboratively with business partners in technology and operations, along with the field, to gather information, analyze results, investigate and identify risks and processes, and drive new and existing data products in the SPGC ecosystem.




This role sits within the Data & Intelligence team, which is responsible for the data used by products delivered in partnership to the field. The Analyst will work cross-functionally to drive improvements in our Data-as-a-Product solutions and will support data testing, data quality, and data governance projects as directed.




This is a hybrid position based in our Vancouver, BC office.




RESPONSIBILITIES

In this position you will:

Support various data warehouse and platform enhancement initiatives — new subject areas, improved data quality, faster data availability, internal compliance, and simplified usage — contributing to the regular, iterative release of data within the SPGC ecosystem.
Liaise with IT and Operations teams to ensure technical and business solutions fully meet stakeholder needs for BI reporting, ad-hoc extracts, data science projects, and customer relationship management.
Support business, contractual, and compliance data needs by creating and maintaining reporting pipelines and delivering one-off reports to internal and external partners.
Perform data profiling; plan, execute, and review user acceptance test (UAT) cases. Establish minimum viable product and acceptance criteria for development sprints and undertake UAT for data-related projects.
Execute data projects assigned by your line manager, ensuring business requirements are properly understood and user stories are well crafted to enable efficient, accurate ETL/ELT development.
Develop and prioritize development stories, participate in Agile ceremonies, and contribute to after-action reviews for data projects.
Establish programmatic processes to proactively measure and manage data quality (ideally automated) using SQL and related languages; coordinate remediation of data defects and data quality problems.
Collaborate with the Data & Intelligence team to implement common data definitions and metadata documentation. Serve as an EDW and data-source subject matter expert.




QUALIFICATIONS AND CHARACTERISTICS

You’re a good fit if you have:

Bachelor’s degree in a related quantitative field, or 5 years of related insurance industry data experience.
Strong, hands-on SQL proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, and data validation.
Microsoft Fabric experience (Google Cloud data architecture such as BigQuery accepted as an alternative platform background).
Strong, hands-on SQL and Python proficiency — query authoring, performance tuning, data validation, and transformation/automation work in Microsoft Fabric notebooks.
Power BI and DAX for reporting and dashboarding.
Practical exposure to data warehousing, business intelligence, data modeling, and master data management concepts.
Demonstrated SCRUM/Agile experience.
Understanding of data governance, data integration, and master data management best practices.
A consultative style that leverages both business and technical resources.

Strong assets (nice to haves):

Familiarity with the EPIC broker management system.
Exposure to NetSuite / NetSuite Analytics Warehouse.




BENEFITS

By joining Specialty Program Group Canada, you’ll become part of a rapidly growing company focused on providing innovative insurance solutions to support independent brokers in Canada. As a member of our team, you’ll benefit from:

Dental care
Extended health care
Paid time-off
RRSP match
Vision care




SALARY RANGE

The expected salary range for this position is from $90,000 to $100,000 and will be impacted by factors such as the successful candidate’s skills, experience and working location, as well as the specific position’s business line, scope and level. Specialty Program Group Canada and affiliated brands are proud to offer comprehensive benefit and total compensation packages which include extended health and dental benefits, disability insurance, RRSP matching, paid time off benefits, and a variety of potential bonus opportunities.




APPLY TODAY

Interested applicants are asked to send your cover letter and resume to careers@spgcanada.ca. Please note that only those chosen for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Management Analyst
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

The Data Operations team is the backbone of data integrity at RBC Global Asset Management. The team creates and maintains securities, issuers, accounts, and benchmarks that power the systems supporting investment operations across the organization. This work enables Investment teams, Trade Operations, Fund Operations, Performance, and other stakeholders to execute trades, onboard clients, report performance, and advance GAM-wide strategic initiatives with confidence.

As a Data Management Analyst, you'll be a key contributor to the team's day-to-day operations, ensuring the accuracy and quality of data that flows through the business. This role offers significant growth potential for someone eager to develop their technical and analytical skills while building a strong foundation in asset management data operations. You'll work closely with experienced team members, contribute to process improvements, and have the opportunity to evolve into more senior responsibilities as you grow with the team.

What will you do?


Maintain and enrich RBC Global Asset Management's data warehouse, including security classification, analytics, and reference data across all investment groups
Perform data quality checks for core data and create securities on GAM's platforms with a high degree of accuracy
Work in conjunction with team members to ensure data standards are defined and enforced consistently
Collaborate with internal clients to identify, document, and source data requirements
Validate data, catch errors early, and escalate issues appropriately to prevent downstream impacts
Support short- to mid-term project implementations and contribute to team deliverables
Document business and technical processes to support knowledge sharing and operational continuity
Develop toolsets and methods to improve data quality and availability
Proactively seek opportunities to contribute beyond assigned tasks and support team objectives
Identify patterns, recurring issues, and suggest proactive solutions to improve workflows


What do you need to succeed?

Must-have:


1+ years of experience in asset management or investment management operations
Understanding of financial instruments (such as fixed income, equity, and derivative securities) and how data flows through the investment management business
Strong attention to detail with the ability to validate data and ensure accuracy
Coachability & growth mindset: Receptive to feedback, eager to learn, and comfortable asking for help when needed
Ability to prioritize: Works effectively in a fast-paced, deadline-oriented environment with strong follow-through skills


Nice-to-have:


Strong Excel skills and working knowledge of SQL and/or Python
Data quality focus: Meticulous approach to validating data, catching errors early, and ensuring accuracy before work impacts downstream processes
Ownership mentality: Takes initiative, follows through consistently, and proactively seeks work beyond assigned tasks
Willingness to challenge: Constructively questions existing processes and suggests improvements to drive better outcomes
Team player: Supports colleagues, shares knowledge, and delivers consistent quality work
Emerging strategic thinking: Begins to identify patterns and recurring issues, and suggests proactive solutions
Experience working with business and/or technology stakeholders in an Enterprise environment
Creative problem-solver who can explore innovative solutions to operational challenges


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
Flexible work/life balance options
Opportunities to do challenging work
Opportunities to take on progressively greater accountabilities
Access to a variety of job opportunities across business and geographies


The expected salary range for the above position is $55,000 - $90,000 depending on factors including but not limited to the candidate’s experience, skills, registration status; market conditions; and business needs.

This position may be eligible to receive a discretionary/variable incentive payment.

Drives RBC’s high-performance culture

Generates sustainable shareholder returns and above market shareholder value

Critical Thinking, Data Administration, Database Structures, Data Modeling, Data Movement, Detail-Oriented, Group Problem Solving, Information Capture, Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), Teamwork

Title: Data Analyst - Coquitlam
Description:
Data Analyst

Are you a Data Analyst with a passion problem solving? Factors Group are a local and fast-growing manufacturing and health and wellness company where your work will have a direct and meaningful impact on business growth. As part of a collaborative and continuous improvement environment, you’ll have the opportunity to both help us as we migrate to Fabric, and work on projects you’re passionate about, all while building your skills and advancing your career.

Permanent, Full-time

Monday – Friday, 8:00am – 4:30pm

Hybrid – 2000 Brigantine Drive, Coquitlam, BC

$75,000 – $90,000 per annum

In this role, you will:


Analyze and interpret data from multiple business systems to support informed decision-making.
Design and maintain dashboards and reports using Power BI, Tableau, and Excel.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to gather requirements and improve reporting solutions.
Identify and resolve data discrepancies to ensure data integrity and consistency across systems.
Support data governance initiatives and contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality and accessibility.


You have:


A Bachelor’s degree or diploma in Computer Science, Business Systems Analysis, or a related field.
3+ years of experience in data analysis, including data modeling and analytics.
Strong proficiency in Power BI, Tableau, Excel, and Power Query (DAX/LOD).
Working knowledge of t-SQL for data querying and manipulation (Python is an asset).
Experience working with diverse data sources, including databases, cloud platforms, and APIs.


You get:


Great benefits package (healthcare, health & personal spending accounts, wellness days, Employee & Family Assistance Program)
Company-provided RRSP
$300 per year to spend on company products
Employee discount pricing
Employee appreciation events & rewards
Free onsite parking
Bus pass reimbursement
Exclusive discounts with various vendors


Why us?


We’re much bigger than you think – 30+ natural health brands loved across North America.
Proudly Canadian-owned & operated
We are committed to sustainability across people, planet, and profit, embedding responsible practices into how we operate and grow as a business.


Learn more about us here: https://ca.naturalfactors.com/pages/our-nature

This position will close when a competitive candidate pool has been identified. We thank you all for your applications and a member of our team will contact you soon. All the best!

All Internal applicants will be given priority for consideration for the first five business days that this position is posted. After that five days, Internal applicants will be considered along with all other applicants; however, are guaranteed at minimum a call from the Talent Acquisition team.

Title: Operations Data Analyst
Description:
ABOUT 49North

49North is a Canadian defence company delivering secure, multi-domain C4ISR capabilities that provide decision advantage in complex and contested environments. A wholly-owned subsidiary of MDA Space, building on more than five decades of trusted Canadian defence delivery, 49North operates as a sovereign Canadian provider of mission-critical C4ISR systems, bringing disciplined prime-contractor execution to defence and security programs.

Visit 49North | An MDA Space Company.

49North is rooted in the execution of large, complex, and mission-critical programs developed within 49NORTH. This legacy reflects a record of delivery where engineering rigour, operational reliability, and national trust are non-negotiable.

49North Defence strengthens Canada’s sovereign defence capability by delivering integrated mission systems and multi-domain C4ISR integration, enabling trusted Canadian and coalition operations, assured interoperability, and sustained readiness.

Role

The role of Operations Data Analyst reports to the Director, Mission Assurance and resides within the Administration function of the larger Programs & Operations Team of 49North, an MDA Space business. As an Operations Data Analyst, you will work with business stakeholders to realize business intelligence through structured data analysis, processing and visualization. This role also includes Process Specialist responsibilities, you will work with process stakeholders to define, realize (implement), deploy and maintain in-scope processes as well as administrate supporting systems, notably the 49North Business Process System (BPS). As an ISO 9001 certified Quality System and plans to implement Capability Maturity Model Integration (CMMI), you will help continuously improve select enterprise systems to meet local needs and assist in the deployment of OneMDA Corporate improvement initiatives.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities of the Operations Data Analyst includes those associated with continual improvement as a Process Specialist. In terms of data analysis, two aspects are considered: business intelligence initiation and business intelligence realization.

Business Intelligence Initiation

Works With Business Leadership And Performs The Following


Identifies the means to quantitatively or qualitatively support assessment of key business objectives and their corresponding Critical Success Factors through identification of Key Performance Indicators and associated metrics
Creates plans describing the bringing together of data sources, working with Corporate stakeholders (e.g. IT) as necessary


Business Intelligence Realization


Identifies and assesses data sources for suitability and takes actions to remediate data quality and integrity as necessary
Connects data sources in a way that enables collective analysis
Develops dashboards, reports and data visualizations that provide meaningful insights to performance
Performs analyses identifying trends, patterns, anomalies and opportunities for improvement
Provides and/or presents evidence-based data interpretations and recommendations


In terms of continual improvement, two aspects are considered: facilitating implementation and change initiatives and administrating supporting systems.

Implementation and Change Initiatives Facilitation

Sources of improvement efforts can include ongoing activities (e.g. maintaining the BPS), local business initiatives (e.g. implementing CMMI), and enabling the deployment of Corporate-sponsored OneMDA initiatives.


Collaborates with leaders and subject matter experts to understand business challenges and information needs
Administrates the identification, prioritization and effort reporting of process improvement initiatives
Assesses processes (e.g. preforms value stream mapping), including creating as-is and to-be depictions of process, to identify and eliminate inefficiencies, waste, and ineffectiveness
Facilitates discovery activities working with process stakeholders and subject matter experts
Creates/revises process descriptions (e.g. procedures) and associated process artifacts (e.g. forms, templates) and training materials


Supporting Systems Administration


Establishes and maintains the process improvement roadmap reflecting the priorities of Operational Excellence and, as applicable, upgrades to the content of the BPS
Maintains the BPS through the evaluation of process Change Requests (CRs) and administration of Change Control Board (CCB) meetings with Process Owners
Participates in the review of Lessons Learned reports to identify potential opportunities for improvement (OFIs)
Leads the disposition of OFI records
Serves as the local MS SharePoint administrator and maintains the BPS infrastructure and associated instructional information on use and administration of the BPS
Performs process change impact assessments for proposed or planned changes
Establishes and maintains a process improvement performance dashboard and periodically reports on process improvement performance


Requirements (experience/skills)


A two- or three-year accredited diploma or certificate program in data analysis
Minimum 2 years of experience in the definition, implementation, maintenance and administration of business processes
Working knowledge of and administrator-level hands-on experience with Power BI
Experience with database management systems and related query language, notably SQL
Microsoft SharePoint site-level administrator experience including HTML and Java scripting capabilities
Experience with a structured change implementation methodology as applied to business processes is required. Experience in process value-stream mapping is considered an asset
Willingness to extend one’s own technical capabilities through learning
Experience working within a quality management system or an ISO- or CMMI-certified workplace is considered an asset
Experience working with Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems (e.g. Infor LN) is considered an asset
History of taking the initiative while diplomatically keeping leadership stakeholders informed along the way
Strong interpersonal skills, particularly in the areas of peer collaboration and teamwork; comfortable interfacing with the senior management team and able to ‘sell’ improvement initiatives
Proven communication skills with an ability to present to multiple stakeholder groups and achieve their buy-in for improvement initiatives


We’re a dream team of purpose-driven, collaborative and passionate people, and we are constantly looking for others to join 49North to ignite new and innovative approaches to problem-solving that push us forward.

Comments/Special Considerations

Successful candidates must obtain and hold security clearance at the secret level, and pass security assessment for the Controlled Goods Program (CGP).

Benefits Statement

49North provides competitive compensation and benefits packages for its employees at all locations. As a team member of 49North, you and your qualified dependents are eligible to participate in a benefit plan that ensures a comprehensive level of protection through competitive health care including; extended healthcare and flexible drug plans, dental and vision benefits, disability income protection, life insurance, group retirement savings plans; and an employee and family assistance program.

Equal Opportunity Statement

49North is an equal opportunity employer prizing diversity and inclusion. We are committed to treating all employees and applicants for employment with respect and dignity. If you require assistance and/or reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please feel free to notify us.

Accommodations

49North is proud to provide accommodation(s) during the recruitment process. Should you require any accommodations, please indicate this on your application/cover letter and we will work with you to meet your accessibility needs.

#IND

The compensation range for this position is $75,000.00 - $122,760.00 annually. The specific compensation offered will be based on the qualifications and experience of the successful candidate.

  • Performs analyses identifying trends, patterns, anomalies and opportunities for improvement
  • Provides and/or presents evidence-based data interpretations and recommendations

In terms of continual improvement, two aspects are considered: facilitating implementation and change initiatives and administrating supporting systems.

Implementation and Change Initiatives Facilitation

Sources of improvement efforts can include ongoing activities (e.g. maintaining the BPS), local business initiatives (e.g. implementing CMMI), and enabling the deployment of Corporate-sponsored OneMDA initiatives.

  • Collaborates with leaders and subject matter experts to understand business challenges and information needs
  • Administrates the identification, prioritization and effort reporting of process improvement initiatives
  • Assesses processes (e.g. preforms value stream mapping), including creating as-is and to-be depictions of process, to identify and eliminate inefficiencies, waste, and ineffectiveness
  • Facilitates discovery activities working with process stakeholders and subject matter experts
  • Creates/revises process descriptions (e.g. procedures) and associated process artifacts (e.g. forms, templates) and training materials

Supporting Systems Administration

  • Establishes and maintains the process improvement roadmap reflecting the priorities of Operational Excellence and, as applicable, upgrades to the content of the BPS
  • Maintains the BPS through the evaluation of process Change Requests (CRs) and administration of Change Control Board (CCB) meetings with Process Owners
  • Participates in the review of Lessons Learned reports to identify potential opportunities for improvement (OFIs)
  • Leads the disposition of OFI records
  • Serves as the local MS SharePoint administrator and maintains the BPS infrastructure and associated instructional information on use and administration of the BPS
  • Performs process change impact assessments for proposed or planned changes
  • Establishes and maintains a process improvement performance dashboard and periodically reports on process improvement performance

Requirements (experience/skills)

  • A two- or three-year accredited diploma or certificate program in data analysis
  • Minimum 2 years of experience in the definition, implementation, maintenance and administration of business processes
  • Working knowledge of and administrator-level hands-on experience with Power BI
  • Experience with database management systems and related query language, notably SQL
  • Microsoft SharePoint site-level administrator experience including HTML and Java scripting capabilities
  • Experience with a structured change implementation methodology as applied to business processes is required. Experience in process value-stream mapping is considered an asset
  • Willingness to extend one’s own technical capabilities through learning
  • Experience working within a quality management system or an ISO- or CMMI-certified workplace is considered an asset
  • Experience working with Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems (e.g. Infor LN) is considered an asset
  • History of taking the initiative while diplomatically keeping leadership stakeholders informed along the way
  • Strong interpersonal skills, particularly in the areas of peer collaboration and teamwork; comfortable interfacing with the senior management team and able to ‘sell’ improvement initiatives
  • Proven communication skills with an ability to present to multiple stakeholder groups and achieve their buy-in for improvement initiatives

We’re a dream team of purpose-driven, collaborative and passionate people, and we are constantly looking for others to join 49North to ignite new and innovative approaches to problem-solving that push us forward.

Comments/Special Considerations

Successful candidates must obtain and hold security clearance at the secret level, and pass security assessment for the Controlled Goods Program (CGP).

Benefits Statement

49North provides competitive compensation and benefits packages for its employees at all locations. As a team member of 49North, you and your qualified dependents are eligible to participate in a benefit plan that ensures a comprehensive level of protection through competitive health care including; extended healthcare and flexible drug plans, dental and vision benefits, disability income protection, life insurance, group retirement savings plans; and an employee and family assistance program.

Equal Opportunity Statement

49North is an equal opportunity employer prizing diversity and inclusion. We are committed to treating all employees and applicants for employment with respect and dignity. If you require assistance and/or reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please feel free to notify us.

Accommodations

49North is proud to provide accommodation(s) during the recruitment process. Should you require any accommodations, please indicate this on your application/cover letter and we will work with you to meet your accessibility needs.

#IND

The compensation range for this position is $75,000.00 - $122,760.00 annually. The specific compensation offered will be based on the qualifications and experience of the successful candidate.

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Product Analytics
Description:
Job Requisition ID #

26WD99763

Position Overview

Data is central to how we understand customer behavior, measure product success, and build better experiences for our customers. Autodesk is seeking a Senior Data Analyst - Product Analytics to join the Product Development & Manufacturing Solutions organization. In this role, you'll partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, Design, Finance, Sales, and Marketing to transform product data into actionable insights that influence product strategy and business outcomes.

As a trusted analytics partner, you will help answer critical questions about feature adoption, customer engagement, retention, and product health. You'll define meaningful success metrics, develop scalable measurement frameworks, and uncover opportunities to improve the customer experience through data.

We're looking for someone who thrives in ambiguity, is naturally curious, and enjoys asking difficult questions that lead to better decisions. You combine strong analytical thinking with business acumen and exceptional communication skills to translate complex analyses into compelling stories that drive action.

At Autodesk, we value a hybrid work culture. Remote work is supported in Toronto and Vancouver, Canada. If you live near one of our offices, you're welcome to work there full- or part-time based on your preference.

Responsibilities


Partner with Product Managers and Engineering teams throughout the product lifecycle to define success metrics and measurement strategies before features launch
Analyze product telemetry, clickstream, and user behavior data to identify opportunities that improve customer engagement, adoption, and retention
Design, maintain, and evolve scalable product KPIs and data models that provide consistent measurement across products
Build self-service dashboards, reports, and visualizations that enable teams to independently explore product performance while maintaining trusted business definitions
Conduct deep-dive analyses to understand product usage, customer journeys, and feature performance, translating findings into actionable recommendations
Develop hypotheses, evaluate product changes, and support experimentation and A/B testing where applicable
Partner with Data Engineering to improve event instrumentation, data quality, and analytics infrastructure
Communicate analytical findings to executive and cross-functional audiences through clear storytelling and data visualization
Improve analytics documentation, metric definitions, and best practices to promote consistency and scalability across teams


Minimum Qualifications


5+ years of experience in Product Analytics, Data Analytics, or Business Analytics, including experience influencing product or business decisions through data
Demonstrated experience presenting analytical findings and recommendations to senior leadership and executive audiences
Expertise in SQL and working with modern cloud data warehouse environments
Advanced experience building dashboards and visualizations using Power BI (required); experience with Tableau or Looker is a plus
Experience analyzing event-based product telemetry, clickstream, or user behavior datasets
Strong understanding of analytical methods, hypothesis-driven problem solving, and translating ambiguous business questions into measurable analyses
Experience working with modern analytics engineering and data pipeline technologies (DBT, Airflow, GitHub, or similar)
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience)


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with experimentation frameworks, A/B testing, or product measurement methodologies
Experience using Python (pandas, Jupyter, NumPy, or similar) for exploratory analysis and automation
Familiarity with product analytics platforms such as Amplitude, Mixpanel, Pendo, or similar tools
Experience integrating product analytics with Sales and Marketing data sources (Salesforce, Marketo, etc.)
Experience working within SaaS or cloud software organizations


Learn More

About Autodesk

Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made.

We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world.

When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us!

Salary transparency

Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. For Canada based roles, we expect a starting base salary between $107,000 and $156,200. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience and geographic location, and may exceed this range. In addition to base salaries, our compensation package may include annual cash bonuses, commissions for sales roles, stock grants, and a comprehensive benefits package.

Belonging

We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/global-belonging

Are you an existing contractor or consultant with Autodesk?

Please search for open jobs and apply internally (not on this external site).

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Reporting to the Manager, Business Intelligence & Analytics, the Senior Data Analyst will have a deep understanding of Data, AI, and Analytics solutions. You will be responsible for designing, developing, and delivering effective storytelling solutions using Power BI and Bird’s modern analytics platform.

We are seeking an individual who is proactive, curious, and driven—someone who understands not just how to build dashboards, but how to enable a culture of data analytics.

This role is ideal for an individual with a strong sense of the big picture: how data governance, storytelling, tools, and people come together to enable better decisions. You will play a key role in advancing Bird’s analytics maturity by delivering trusted insights, promoting effective data storytelling, and contributing to the Data & AI Center of Excellence (CoE) through standards, enablement, and data fluency.

The ideal candidate is comfortable navigating complexity, managing multiple priorities, and working across both Agile and Waterfall delivery models—partnering effectively with business, engineering, and leadership teams.

This position follows a hybrid work model with regular in office presence.

What You Will Be Working On


Design, develop, and deliver high‑quality analytics solutions using Power BI, ensuring accuracy, reliability, and consistency of data and metrics.
Apply effective data storytelling principles to transform complex information into clear, compelling, and decision‑ready insights.
Partner with stakeholders to understand business context, ask the right questions, and translate analytical needs into meaningful dashboards and reports.
Contribute to the evolution of the Data & AI Center of Excellence (CoE), helping shape standards, best practices, templates, and shared ways of working.
Promote data governance concepts by reinforcing trusted metrics, clear definitions, consistent logic, and proper documentation.
Support and advance data fluency and literacy across the organization by helping stakeholders better interpret, question, and use analytics effectively.
Perform analytical investigations and root‑cause analysis to explain trends, variances, and anomalies in business performance.
Collaborate with Data Engineering and IT teams to ensure analytics solutions align with data Lakehouse architecture and enterprise platforms.
Demonstrate a strong understanding of Data Lakehouse concepts, with exposure to tools such as Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, or Microsoft Synapse Analytics, sufficient to collaborate effectively with engineering teams.
Navigate and contribute within both Agile and Waterfall project environments, adapting communication and delivery style as needed.
Manage multiple priorities simultaneously while maintaining quality, structure, and stakeholder confidence.
Maintain strong, proactive communication with stakeholders and contribute to successful project outcomes.
Stay current with analytics trends and Microsoft’s analytics ecosystem (Power BI, Lakehouse platforms, and related technologies), applying learnings pragmatically.



What We Are Looking For


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Analytics, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in a Senior Data Analyst or Business Intelligence role.
Strong hands‑on experience with Power BI, with an emphasis on insight, clarity, and usability rather than just visual complexity.
A proactive, self‑driven mindset — comfortable taking ownership, identifying gaps, and helping move analytics forward.
Demonstrated understanding of what building a data‑driven culture actually means, beyond tools alone.
Experience contributing to or working within a Center of Excellence–style model for Data, AI, Analytics, or BI.
Solid grasp of data governance fundamentals, including trusted metrics, standard definitions, and consistency of logic.
Strong communication and organizational skills, with the ability to work effectively across both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Experience working in both Agile and Waterfall delivery environments.
Ability to manage competing priorities with structure, judgment, and professionalism.
General understanding of the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps / Power Automate) without requiring deep specialization.



Preferred Qualifications


Experience delivering insights from structured, semi‑structured, and unstructured data sources.
Working knowledge of SQL and structured data querying.
Familiarity with modern data lakehouse architectures and exposure to platforms such as Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, or Synapse Analytics.
Power BI or relevant Microsoft analytics certification.
Experience enabling or mentoring others through data education, documentation, or informal



For Those Who Seek To Redefine

The greatest achievements in history are borne from the greatness within people – where human potential meets vision, and passion fuels evolution. Unlocking this potential is the most important thing we do at Bird.

As a leader in Canadian construction for over 100 years, the impact of our team is etched deeply within the core of our legacy. Beyond Bird, this impact is felt in the fundamental aspects of our everyday lives. From the critical infrastructure we depend on, to the energy and resources that keep us moving - we are powering our communities and shaping Canada’s skylines coast-to-coast-to-coast. Entrenched in the foundation of a culture built more than a century ago is an enduring quest to reimagine what is possible. Our impact is greater than ever, and we are looking for those who seek to redefine their story.

What We Believe In

We Put Safety First

A healthy and safe work environment is non-negotiable. We build a culture of operational and psychological safety through engagement, learning and leadership.

We Lead With Honesty

We speak and act with integrity, clarity and care so people can trust our word and our work. Being honest means we can deliver the best outcomes and consistent results.

We Are Stronger Together

Success is a team effort. Our inclusive workplace enables our combined expertise, humility and creativity to unlock our greater potential.

We Are Driven To Do Great Work

We built our name on quality. We have a passion for excellence in our work and relationships that honours our businesses and our industry.

We Create Opportunity

Rooted in a solid foundation, we adapt and grow to face the future. We are committed to elevating each other to chart the best path forward in an evolving world.

At Bird, we value Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DE&I) and believe it is essential to our success. We will continue to listen, learn, and take action in our commitment to building progress in our organization, and the industry as a whole. Cultivating an environment where all employees feel included, seen, and have a sense of belonging is core to Bird’s culture. We commit to proactively employing a workforce that reflects the communities in which we work, fostering an environment of continuous learning, and never compromising on our values.

Title: Advanced Data Analyst - EA SPORTS™ FC
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

Advanced Data Analyst, Experimentation Lead - FC Ultimate Team

As one of the largest sports entertainment platforms in the world, EA SPORTS FC is redefining football with genre-leading interactive experiences, connecting a global community of fans to The World's Game through innovation and unrivaled authenticity.

With more opportunity than ever to design, innovate and create new, immersive experiences that bring joy, inclusivity, and connection to fans everywhere, we invite you to join our passionate and dynamic team as we pioneer the future of football fandom.

The FC Data & Analytics team is seeking an Advanced Data Analyst to join our Ultimate Team (UT) function. Reporting to a Senior Manager, you will lead the Experimentation program for FC Premium Analytics. You’ll also collaborate closely with data, production, and product management partners to drive informed decision-making within a dynamic live service environment.

We are looking for a candidate who is excited at the prospect of cultivating and uncovering actionable insights from live player data and contributing to the sustained success of one of the industry's most prominent live service products. In this role, you will act as a data leader, driving design, implementation, and evaluation of numerous player-facing experiments.

What You'll Do On Our Team


Collaborate with production teams to define telemetry requirements, ensuring robust data collection and validity.
Design and implement player-facing experimentation using best practices in a complex, live environment
Write and maintain complex SQL and scripting workflows to extract, transform, and validate large-scale telemetry datasets
Build and maintain scalable dashboards that translate complex datasets into clear, actionable business insights for cross-functional partners
Explore user behavioral patterns through experimentation to identify opportunities for improving player experiences and to inform live content release strategies
Present analytical findings clearly and effectively to stakeholders of all seniority levels to facilitate informed decision-making
Act as a subject-matter expert in all things experimentation, helping to drive adoption of a data-driven, test-and-learn focused culture across FC’s development team


What We're Looking For


Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Operations Research) or equivalent practical experience.
5+ years of technical experience in data analytics, with a focus on performing quantitative analysis with large datasets to guide decision-making
Expert knowledge or equivalent practical experience in leading an experimentation function for a live product
Proficiency in SQL with the ability to extract data from complex, multi-source systems.
Hands-on experience with data visualization software (e.g., Tableau, Power BI).
Strong programming skills (e.g., Python, R) with experience writing scripts for data transformation and statistical analysis
Passion for gaming or previous experience in the gaming industry is considered a strong plus.


Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$114,300 - $156,200 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Operations Data Analyst
Description:
ABOUT 49North

49North is a Canadian defence company delivering secure, multi-domain C4ISR capabilities that provide decision advantage in complex and contested environments. A wholly-owned subsidiary of MDA Space, building on more than five decades of trusted Canadian defence delivery, 49North operates as a sovereign Canadian provider of mission-critical C4ISR systems, bringing disciplined prime-contractor execution to defence and security programs.

Visit 49North | An MDA Space Company.

49North is rooted in the execution of large, complex, and mission-critical programs developed within 49NORTH. This legacy reflects a record of delivery where engineering rigour, operational reliability, and national trust are non-negotiable.

49North Defence strengthens Canada’s sovereign defence capability by delivering integrated mission systems and multi-domain C4ISR integration, enabling trusted Canadian and coalition operations, assured interoperability, and sustained readiness.

Role

The role of Operations Data Analyst reports to the Director, Mission Assurance and resides within the Administration function of the larger Programs & Operations Team of 49North, an MDA Space business. As an Operations Data Analyst, you will work with business stakeholders to realize business intelligence through structured data analysis, processing and visualization. This role also includes Process Specialist responsibilities, you will work with process stakeholders to define, realize (implement), deploy and maintain in-scope processes as well as administrate supporting systems, notably the 49North Business Process System (BPS). As an ISO 9001 certified Quality System and plans to implement Capability Maturity Model Integration (CMMI), you will help continuously improve select enterprise systems to meet local needs and assist in the deployment of OneMDA Corporate improvement initiatives.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities of the Operations Data Analyst includes those associated with continual improvement as a Process Specialist. In terms of data analysis, two aspects are considered: business intelligence initiation and business intelligence realization.

Business Intelligence Initiation

Works With Business Leadership And Performs The Following


Identifies the means to quantitatively or qualitatively support assessment of key business objectives and their corresponding Critical Success Factors through identification of Key Performance Indicators and associated metrics
Creates plans describing the bringing together of data sources, working with Corporate stakeholders (e.g. IT) as necessary


Business Intelligence Realization


Identifies and assesses data sources for suitability and takes actions to remediate data quality and integrity as necessary
Connects data sources in a way that enables collective analysis
Develops dashboards, reports and data visualizations that provide meaningful insights to performance
Performs analyses identifying trends, patterns, anomalies and opportunities for improvement
Provides and/or presents evidence-based data interpretations and recommendations


In terms of continual improvement, two aspects are considered: facilitating implementation and change initiatives and administrating supporting systems.

Implementation and Change Initiatives Facilitation

Sources of improvement efforts can include ongoing activities (e.g. maintaining the BPS), local business initiatives (e.g. implementing CMMI), and enabling the deployment of Corporate-sponsored OneMDA initiatives.


Collaborates with leaders and subject matter experts to understand business challenges and information needs
Administrates the identification, prioritization and effort reporting of process improvement initiatives
Assesses processes (e.g. preforms value stream mapping), including creating as-is and to-be depictions of process, to identify and eliminate inefficiencies, waste, and ineffectiveness
Facilitates discovery activities working with process stakeholders and subject matter experts
Creates/revises process descriptions (e.g. procedures) and associated process artifacts (e.g. forms, templates) and training materials


Supporting Systems Administration


Establishes and maintains the process improvement roadmap reflecting the priorities of Operational Excellence and, as applicable, upgrades to the content of the BPS
Maintains the BPS through the evaluation of process Change Requests (CRs) and administration of Change Control Board (CCB) meetings with Process Owners
Participates in the review of Lessons Learned reports to identify potential opportunities for improvement (OFIs)
Leads the disposition of OFI records
Serves as the local MS SharePoint administrator and maintains the BPS infrastructure and associated instructional information on use and administration of the BPS
Performs process change impact assessments for proposed or planned changes
Establishes and maintains a process improvement performance dashboard and periodically reports on process improvement performance


Requirements (experience/skills)


A two- or three-year accredited diploma or certificate program in data analysis
Minimum 2 years of experience in the definition, implementation, maintenance and administration of business processes
Working knowledge of and administrator-level hands-on experience with Power BI
Experience with database management systems and related query language, notably SQL
Microsoft SharePoint site-level administrator experience including HTML and Java scripting capabilities
Experience with a structured change implementation methodology as applied to business processes is required. Experience in process value-stream mapping is considered an asset
Willingness to extend one’s own technical capabilities through learning
Experience working within a quality management system or an ISO- or CMMI-certified workplace is considered an asset
Experience working with Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems (e.g. Infor LN) is considered an asset
History of taking the initiative while diplomatically keeping leadership stakeholders informed along the way
Strong interpersonal skills, particularly in the areas of peer collaboration and teamwork; comfortable interfacing with the senior management team and able to ‘sell’ improvement initiatives
Proven communication skills with an ability to present to multiple stakeholder groups and achieve their buy-in for improvement initiatives


We’re a dream team of purpose-driven, collaborative and passionate people, and we are constantly looking for others to join 49North to ignite new and innovative approaches to problem-solving that push us forward.

Comments/Special Considerations

Successful candidates must obtain and hold security clearance at the secret level, and pass security assessment for the Controlled Goods Program (CGP).

Benefits Statement

49North provides competitive compensation and benefits packages for its employees at all locations. As a team member of 49North, you and your qualified dependents are eligible to participate in a benefit plan that ensures a comprehensive level of protection through competitive health care including; extended healthcare and flexible drug plans, dental and vision benefits, disability income protection, life insurance, group retirement savings plans; and an employee and family assistance program.

Equal Opportunity Statement

49North is an equal opportunity employer prizing diversity and inclusion. We are committed to treating all employees and applicants for employment with respect and dignity. If you require assistance and/or reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please feel free to notify us.

Accommodations

49North is proud to provide accommodation(s) during the recruitment process. Should you require any accommodations, please indicate this on your application/cover letter and we will work with you to meet your accessibility needs.

#IND

The compensation range for this position is $75,000.00 - $122,760.00 annually. The specific compensation offered will be based on the qualifications and experience of the successful candidate.

Title: Senior Data Analyst - EA Sports FC
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

Senior Data Analyst - FC Ultimate Team

As one of the largest sports entertainment platforms in the world, EA SPORTS FC is redefining football with genre-leading interactive experiences, connecting a global community of fans to The World's Game through innovation and unrivaled authenticity.

With more opportunity than ever to design, innovate and create new, immersive experiences that bring joy, inclusivity, and connection to fans everywhere, we invite you to join our passionate and dynamic team as we pioneer the future of football fandom.

The FC Data & Analytics team is seeking a Senior Data Analyst to join our Ultimate Team (UT) function. Reporting to a Senior Manager, you will collaborate closely with production and product management partners to drive data-informed decisions within a dynamic live service environment.

We are looking for a candidate who is excited at the prospect of uncovering actionable insights from live player data and contributing to the sustained success of one of the industry's most prominent live service products. In this role, you will act as a data investigator, visualizer, and designer, helping to enhance the core experiences of our global player base.

What You'll Do On Our Team


Conduct quantitative analysis and financial modelling to evaluate feature design opportunities, assess risks, and support strategic investment decisions in UT.
Collaborate with production teams to define telemetry requirements, ensuring robust data collection and validity.
Write and maintain complex SQL and scripting workflows to extract, transform, and validate large-scale telemetry datasets.
Build and maintain scalable dashboards that translate complex datasets into clear, actionable business insights for cross-functional partners.
Explore user behavioral patterns to identify opportunities for improving player experiences and to inform live content release strategies.
Present analytical findings clearly and effectively to stakeholders of all seniority levels to facilitate informed decision-making.


What We're Looking For


Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science) or equivalent practical experience.
2+ years of technical experience in data analytics, with a focus on performing quantitative analysis with large datasets to guide decision-making.
Proficiency in SQL with the ability to extract data from complex, multi-source systems.
Hands-on experience with data visualization software (e.g., Tableau, Power BI).
Strong programming skills (e.g., Python, R) with experience writing scripts for data transformation and statistical analysis.
Passion for gaming or previous experience in the gaming industry is considered a plus.


Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$92,900 - $129,200 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Title: Business Analyst - Data
Description:
MatchBox Consulting Group is currently seeking a Business Analyst – Data for a 7 month contract role with a client of ours located in Vancouver.

Responsibilities


Leading the discovery of new data foundational and/or business-initiated projects and gathering requirements using effective business analysis techniques
Consulting with stakeholders and acting as a conduit between stakeholders and the technical squad including Architect, Technical Team etc.
Translating business requirements into detailed and actionable specifications and requirements documents
Creating understandable visual representations to communicate complex concepts and models for various target audiences
Documenting business processes during the investigation and discovery with stakeholders or other parties to create certified documents used by the enterprise in terms of functional knowledge
Organizing and facilitating user acceptance testing with business users
Identifying and documenting current and future business process, changes, and improvements to facilitate change management
Facilitating and managing stakeholder engagement


Requirements


3+ years of experience as a Business Analyst, related to Data & Analytics
Experience with working in Agile/waterfall methodologies
Familiar with the Data & Analytics product lifecycle
Possess a high level of analytical and critical thinking
Solid understanding of data models (ERDs)
Strong SQL knowledge
Experience working in Power Automate
Excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to communicate with all levels of stakeholders to be able to explain, articulate, and document concepts, business processes, systems, and business requirements efficiently
Strong organization and prioritization skills while managing multiple, competing priorities
Ability to understand the business processes in the technical landscape of Data & Analytics and help translate into an actionable document for the development team
Experience using JIRA


For more current job opportunities, Follow MatchBox at linkedin.com/company/matchbox-recruitment/

_________________________

About MatchBox

Headquartered in downtown Vancouver and serving clients nationally, MatchBox is one of the leading recruitment and solutions firms that specializes in the fields of IT & Technology, Engineering & Technical, Real Estate & Construction, and Accounting & Finance. We offer unrivaled expertise with our team's extensive experience in the recruitment and professional services industry. We are dedicated to building great connections and creating strong opportunities within the workforce and the labor market.

Equal Opportunities

MatchBox is committed to providing equal opportunities for all applicants. We welcome and encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, including members of minority groups such as racialized individuals, people with disabilities, LGBTQ+ individuals, and Indigenous peoples. It is a priority for us that all candidates are treated fairly and without discrimination. Our recruitment teams receive ongoing training on using objective criteria for evaluating candidates and other related topics to create an inclusive and welcoming environment for all.

Work Permits

Please note that we require all interested candidates to obtain the necessary work permits from the Government of Canada prior to submitting an application. This position is open exclusively to individuals residing in Canada and legally authorized to work in Canada. Applications not meeting these criteria will not be considered.

Further Consideration

We receive a high volume of applications and are only able to contact candidates who are selected for further consideration.

Find out more at www.matchboxhr.com

Title: Quantitative Equity Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Quantitative Equity Data Analyst to join our Quantitative Equity Team! We are a high-performance team embedded in a top-performing quant fund that manages over $75 billion in financial assets. We are dedicated to the mission-critical operation of our investment engine—a well-tuned machine responsible for generating key decisions that drive trades and investment insights. Leveraging data analytics, finance knowledge and cutting-edge technology, we aim to ensure that new and useful data products are continuously ready for research.

Do you love the idea of integrating large proprietary data sources into valuable applications? Are you a wizard at transforming ‘messy reality’ into high quality data assets? As a core member of our team, you will collaborate with investment and data experts to tackle challenging problems, and to continuously expand our capabilities in data. Based on the west coast in Vancouver, we value mentorship, collaboration, and growth in a supportive and innovative environment. Join us to make a significant impact on our investment outcomes and overall success.

What You Will Do

This is an exciting full-time role for individuals who are enthusiastic about learning the quantitative equity investment management business and excited to tackle a broad range of data, investment, and technology challenges.

You will start with a comprehensive training program, learning about various elements of quantitative equity investment management and our investment process.

You will then continue to a specialized role utilizing data analytics and data science skillsets to enable the Quantitative Equity Team’s research. Your role will involve identifying and assessing brand new data, data exploration, and proposing robust ways to integrate data products from many sources.

You will be supported with coaching and mentorship from senior members of the team, and we will create the conditions for you to grow your career steadily over time. This is an opportunity for ambitious individuals

to drive their career growth in data.

Role

While we take the approach of tailoring the career path to individual strengths, we expect successful candidates for this role to develop excellence in the following tasks:


Create processes to assess, explore, and link billions of data points.
Test structured and unstructured data sets for data quality and reliability.
Assess new data opportunities for quality and usefulness.
Evaluate your data solutions for sensibility, accuracy, and bias.
Collaborate with quantitative researchers – support alpha projects with your data expertise.
Collaborate with technology specialists – help trade off application performance and operational feasibility.
Support production deployments of your work to ensure long-term reliability.
Enable clarity of and access to your work through robust documentation and exposition.
Present findings and recommendations to key stakeholders in a clear, concise, and compelling manner.


What You Bring

Strong analytical skills with experience mapping complex operational workflows and business rules

Deep expertise in business analysis within the financial services, retail banking, or credit union sectors

Experience in large-scale modernization or enterprise transformation projects


What We Offer


Opportunity to work on impactful, large-scale modernization initiatives
Collaborative and supportive engineering culture
Exposure to modern cloud-native technologies and platforms
Flexible and dynamic remote delivery environment

Title: Business System Analyst
Description:
Position Summary

The Senior Business Systems Analyst serves as a critical liaison between business stakeholders, product management, engineering teams, and external partners to deliver scalable technology and data solutions that support business objectives. This role is responsible for analyzing business requirements, defining solution designs, managing backlog refinement, facilitating cross-functional collaboration, and ensuring successful delivery of initiatives within an Agile environment. The ideal candidate brings deep expertise in business systems analysis, data architecture, Anaplan, retail systems, and cloud-based technologies, while driving continuous process improvement and operational excellence across the organization.




Key Responsibilities

Requirements Management

Partner with Product Managers, Product Owners, business stakeholders, and technical teams to understand, analyze, and document business and system requirements for initiatives, projects, and EPICs.
Lead requirements gathering workshops and stakeholder discussions to identify business needs and solution requirements.
Create and maintain functional specifications, process documentation, and user-facing feature documentation when required.
Ensure requirements are clearly defined, traceable, and aligned with business objectives.

User Story Development & Backlog Management

Develop, refine, and maintain detailed user stories and acceptance criteria aligned with EPICs and business requirements.
Facilitate backlog grooming and refinement sessions to ensure stories are ready for development.
Identify, document, and manage dependencies across teams, systems, and projects.
Ensure all stories meet the agreed Definition of Ready (DoR) before sprint planning.
Maintain a healthy, prioritized backlog supporting delivery objectives and Agile flow targets.
Contribute to effort estimation activities and support sprint planning processes.

Cross-Functional & Third-Party Collaboration

Coordinate with internal and external teams to align project deliverables, dependencies, timelines, and priorities.
Facilitate discussions and issue resolution with third-party vendors, partner teams, and external stakeholders.
Escalate risks, blockers, and dependencies as needed to ensure successful project execution.
Support post-implementation activities, including documentation, issue resolution, and stakeholder communication.

Quarterly Planning & Solution Analysis

Analyze business and technical EPICs during planning cycles to identify requirements, risks, assumptions, dependencies, and solution options.
Collaborate with Engineering, UX, QA, Analytics, SRE, and Center of Excellence teams to define comprehensive solution approaches.
Translate business requirements into actionable implementation plans and user stories.
Provide analysis and business context to support accurate level-of-effort estimations and prioritization decisions.
Validate assumptions with subject matter experts and stakeholders through detailed analysis and discovery activities.

Testing & Quality Assurance

Review test plans and acceptance criteria to ensure traceability to documented requirements.
Validate that business needs and functional expectations are accurately reflected in testing activities.
Support user acceptance testing and defect triage processes as required.

Operations & Governance Support

Maintain awareness of third-party maintenance schedules, service disruptions, and operational dependencies.
Ensure appropriate vendor and support contact information is maintained and accessible.
Promote data governance, security, compliance, and best practices across systems and processes.

Process Improvement

Champion continuous improvement initiatives and promote Agile best practices.
Lead or contribute to process optimization efforts that improve team effectiveness, delivery predictability, and stakeholder satisfaction.
Collaborate with peer analysts to share lessons learned, standardize practices, and strengthen business analysis capabilities across the organization.




Required Skills

Education & Experience

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Analytics, or a related discipline.
Minimum 10 years of experience in Business Systems Analysis, Data Engineering, Business Analysis, or related technology roles.
Proven experience working within Agile and Scrum delivery environments.

Technical Skills

Strong expertise in Anaplan model design, implementation, optimization, and support.
Deep understanding of data architecture, data modeling, ETL processes, system integrations, and data management practices.
Experience with cloud platforms, preferably AWS (Azure experience also valuable).
Knowledge of data governance, security, compliance, and data quality best practices.
Familiarity with analytics, reporting, machine learning concepts, and modern data platforms.

Business & Domain Knowledge

Experience working with retail business processes, retail operations, and retail data ecosystems.
Strong ability to translate complex business requirements into scalable technical solutions.
Understanding of enterprise application ecosystems and cross-functional business processes.
Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and commitment to continuous improvement.

Title: Production Data Analyst for Feature Animation - ILM Vancouver
Description:
This role is considered Hybrid, which means the employee will work 2-3 days onsite at a Company designated location and occasionally from home.

Position Summary

The Production Data Analyst for Feature Animation oversees production data and analysis for assigned projects. This position requires a comprehensive understanding of the feature animation production lifecycle. Working closely with Producers and other production staff, you will manage the day-to-day activity of a project as well as evaluating insights and reporting, including both internal and client report templates.

What You’ll Do



Manage and optimize internal and client reporting templates across the feature animation portfolio.
Support Producer in creating and managing reports.
Develop and maintain show(s) master sequence, quota schedule, and scenario planner with the Producers and Production staff.
Prepare standardized weekly and monthly reports and other special reports as needed.
Compile and deliver internal weekly status reports for the Producer(s).
Responsible for reviewing weekly detail reports for accuracy and making reclassifications as required.
Partner with Producers and Production Managers to ensure show inventory is flowing through the pipeline and that all departments monitor their daily inventory levels, providing accurate output figures and forecasts.
Pro-actively make recommendations for improvements to production and workflows, particularly with respect to scheduling, flow of information, milestones, and client reporting.
Support Feature Animation forecasting and planning.
Work to understand the wants and needs of all teams to help prioritize workloads on a short- and long-term basis.
Appropriately elevate issues and solutions to Producer(s).
Mentor Production Staff on assessing their bids and schedules and generating reports.



What We’re Looking For



3–4 years of production experience, including a minimum of 2 years of specialized experience within feature animation.
Expert proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets, with a proven track record of managing and analyzing large datasets.
Strong, demonstrated analytical skills.
Energetic, resourceful, and hands-on collaborator with a strong service orientation.
Ability to thrive in fast-paced, high-pressure environments, where you handle multiple tasks while meeting strict deadlines.
Adaptable and flexible, possessing a deep comprehension of how to navigate and support a creatively driven production environment.
Exceptional cross-functional collaborator who uses diplomacy to handle complex issues, solve problems, and align multiple stakeholders.
Strong communication and active listening skills.
Growth-oriented mindset focused on driving improvements.



JoinILM

The hiring range for this position in Vancouver is C$67,000 to C$85,700 per year based on a 40-hour work week. The amount of hours scheduled per week may vary based on business needs. The base pay actually offered will take into account internal equity and also may vary depending on the candidate’s geographic region, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. A full range of medical, financial, and/or other variable pay or benefits, may be offered dependent on the level and position offered.
