Title: Data Quality Assurance Analyst - FTT
Description:
Marketing Statement

A career at TransLink and our family of companies means working with people with a wide range of skills and perspectives, all teaming up towards a common goal: preserving and enhancing the region's world-envied quality of life. Together, we connect the region and enhance its livability by providing a sustainable transit and transportation network, embraced by our communities and people.

At TransLink we are dedicated to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of the communities in which we live. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive, equitable and accessible workplace, recognizing the unique value and skills every individual brings.

Looking for a great place to work where your contributions are valued and you can make a difference in a vibrant city? At TransLink, one of BC’s Top Employers, you'll help make Metro Vancouver a better place to live, built on transportation excellence. Put your future in motion!

Summary

Responsibilities

The Data Quality Assurance (QA) Analyst is responsible for ensuring the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of TransLink’s enterprise data assets and analytical systems. This role focuses heavily on data validation, ETL testing, and database quality assurance, supporting the delivery of trusted, high-quality data for business intelligence, analytics, and operational decision-making.

The Analyst creates and executes data-centric testing strategies, develops automated data validation frameworks, and partners closely with Data Architects, Solution Architects, Data Analysts, Data Warehouse Analysts, Functional Analysts, Business Intelligence Analysts, and other roles to ensure that data pipelines, integrations, and applications perform accurately and consistently across systems.

Education

Qualifications

Diploma in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Computer Systems Engineering, or Mathematics from a recognized post-secondary institution, including recent coursework in database management, data warehousing, cloud computing, and software testing is preferred.

Experience

Minimum three (3) years of experience in data testing, data validation, or ETL/warehouse QA within large-scale or cloud data environments including proven experience in testing data pipelines, ETL processes, and cloud-based data integrations, advanced proficiency in SQL and Microsoft SQL Server.

Experience validating Azure Data Factory or Azure Synapse Analytics workflows, background in test automation and scripting using Python or equivalent languages and familiarity with data observability, data profiling, and data governance frameworks preferred.

Up to one (1) year of on-the-job experience may be required to gain familiarity with TransLink’s specific systems and practices.

Other Information

Recruitment Process: An applicant will be required to demonstrate their suitability for this position by meeting the minimum level of qualifications and experience in order to be invited into the selection process. Work Schedule

37.5 hours per week.

This is a full-time temporary position for 12 months.

Hybrid

This position offers the flexibility of working both on-site and remotely within B.C.

Salary $8,236 - $9,896 per month (Actual salary offered will be commensurate with education, experience and internal parity).

The Total Compensation Package includes Extended Health, Transit Pass and enrollment in the Public Service Pension Plan upon eligibility. Title: Decision Scientist
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

Decision Scientist

Workplace Type: Hybrid (Austin, TX or Vancouver, BC)

Reporting to: Director, Decision and Data Science

Office of the CXO

The Office of the CXO drives effectiveness across the EA Experiences organization with a focus on excellence in business operations, comprehensive fan intelligence, plus internal technology and business solutions. We are horizontal connectors empowering teams across the Experiences organization with the strategic prioritization, investments, resources, data, insights, and technology required to accelerate business outcomes.

Your Impact

You will transform complex data into actionable insights that improve fan experiences and support business decisions. Working with large-scale gaming and customer datasets, you'll partner with teams across EA to solve business challenges using statistical analysis, experimentation, and machine learning.

Responsibilities


Analyze structured and unstructured datasets to identify trends and business insights.
Build, validate, and improve predictive and prescriptive models using Python or R.
Design and evaluate experiments to measure product and business outcomes.
Develop dashboards and visualizations that communicate analytical findings.
Partner with cross-functional teams to translate business questions into analytical solutions.
Present recommendations through clear visualizations and business-focused storytelling.
Apply responsible AI practices, including model validation, fairness, and bias monitoring.


Required Qualifications


Master's degree in Statistics, Operations Research, Data Science, or a related field; equivalent experience considered.
Experience using Python or R for statistical analysis and modeling.
Experience with SQL and relational databases.
Experience using visualization tools such as Tableau, Streamlit, or RShiny.
Experience applying statistical methods to business problems.


Core Skills And Experience


2+ years applying statistical modeling in a business environment.
Experience preparing, analyzing, and interpreting large datasets.
Knowledge of experimentation and causal inference methods.
Experience communicating analytical findings through dashboards and presentations.
Familiarity with Git and cloud-based analytical environments.


Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$104,500 - $142,800 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Evaluation and Measurement Analyst
Description:
Overview

At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

The Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst is responsible for assessing the effectiveness and impact of learning programs by supporting the design of measurement strategies, analyzing training data, and providing actionable insights to improve learning outcomes and business performance. This role works closely with course owners, SMEs, and stakeholders to ensure training delivers measurable value.

Data Collection & Analysis


Collect and validate data from multiple sources (e.g., LMS, surveys, assessments)
Analyze quantitative and qualitative data to identify trends, gaps, and improvement opportunities
Perform statistical analysis (e.g., pass rates, item difficulty, distractor analysis) to assess assessment quality


Reporting & Insights


Review dashboards and reports to communicate training performance and outcomes
Translate data into clear, actionable insights for course owners and stakeholders
Provide recommendations for course improvements based on findings


Continuous Improvement


Monitor training effectiveness throughout the lifecycle (pre-, during, and post-delivery)
Partner with SMEs and course owners to refine content, assessments, and delivery methods
Support post-course evaluations and follow-up analysis (e.g., behavior and performance impact)


What You Will Do

As a Training Evaluation & Measurement Analyst, you will play a key role in helping KPMG Business School understand and improve the effectiveness of its learning programs. Working closely with course owners, subject matter experts (SMEs), and other stakeholders, you will transform learning data into meaningful insights that drive better learning experiences and business outcomes.

In this role, you will


Collect, validate, and analyze data from multiple sources, including learning management systems, surveys and assessments.
Identify trends, patterns, learning gaps, and opportunities for improvement through quantitative and qualitative analysis.
Conduct measurement and evaluation activities, including assessment analysis, learner feedback reviews, pass rate analysis, item difficulty reviews, and other evaluation methodologies.
Maintain reporting dashboards and metrics to monitor learning effectiveness and learner performance.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and recommendations for stakeholders and course owners.
Support the evaluation of learning initiatives throughout the training lifecycle, from pre-deployment through post-course analysis.
Collaborate with course owners and SMEs to identify opportunities to improve course content, assessments, learner engagement, and overall learning impact.
Contribute to the development and continuous improvement of evaluation frameworks, measurement strategies, and reporting standards.
Support the preparation and presentation of findings, recommendations, and evaluation results to various stakeholders.
Assist with special projects and initiatives related to learning analytics, measurement, and continuous improvement.


What You Bring To The Role

You are a curious and analytical professional who enjoys turning data into meaningful insights. You have a passion for learning and development and are motivated by the opportunity to improve learning experiences through evidence-based decision-making.

You bring


3 years experience in learning analytics, training evaluation, data analysis, research, or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify trends, interpret data, and make practical recommendations.
Experience working with data visualization and reporting tools such as Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
Knowledge of learning measurement and evaluation methodologies, such as the Kirkpatrick Model or other evaluation frameworks, is an asset.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy and quality.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work effectively in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills, with the ability to work across teams and influence decision-making through data-driven insights.
Experience working with Learning Management Systems (LMS) and learning technologies is considered an asset.
A continuous improvement mindset and a passion for enhancing learning outcomes through measurement and evaluation.


KPMG Ontario Region Pay Range Information

The expected base salary range for this position is $55,000 to $82,000 and may be eligible for bonus awards. The determination of an applicant’s base salary within this range is based on the individual’s location, skills & competencies, and unique qualifications. In addition, KPMG offers a comprehensive and competitive Total Rewards program.

KPMG BC Region Pay Range Information

The expected base salary range for this position is $55,000 to $82,000 and may be eligible for bonus awards. The determination of an applicant’s base salary within this range is based on the individual’s location, skills & competencies, and unique qualifications. In addition, KPMG offers a comprehensive and competitive Total Rewards program.

Providing you with the support you need to be at your best

Our Values, The KPMG Way

Integrity, we do what is right | Excellence, we never stop learning and improving | Courage, we think and act boldly | Together, we respect each other and draw strength from our differences | For Better, we do what matters

KPMG in Canada is a proud equal opportunities employer and we are committed to creating a respectful, inclusive and barrier-free workplace that allows all of our people to reach their full potential. A diverse workforce is key to our success and we believe in bringing your whole self to work. We welcome all qualified candidates to apply and hope you will choose KPMG in Canada as your employer of choice.

Adjustments and accommodations throughout the recruitment process

At KPMG, we are committed to fostering an inclusive recruitment process where all candidates can be themselves and excel. We aim to provide a positive experience and are prepared to offer adjustments or accommodations to help you perform at your best. Adjustments (informal requests), such as extra preparation time or the option for micro breaks during interviews, and accommodations (formal requests), such as accessible communication supports or technology aids, are tailored to individual needs and role requirements. You will have an opportunity to request an adjustment or accommodation at any point throughout the recruitment process. If you require support, please contact KPMG’s Employee Relations Service team by calling 1-888-466-4778.

AI Usage

We embrace the use of artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance the candidate experience and streamline our recruitment processes. AI tools may help with organizing applications or surfacing relevant qualifications. However, no hiring decisions are made using AI. Every hiring decision is made by our hiring managers and recruitment professionals, who are equipped with training that empowers them to use these tools responsibly. AI technologies used in our recruitment process undergo detailed risk assessments, including security and privacy requirements, that align with KPMG’s Trusted AI framework.

We believe technology should empower human judgment, not replace it. It’s one of the many ways we’re delivering on our vision of being a technology-first, people-driven firm.

Title: Lead Data Engineer (Audience Intelligence)
Description:
Lead Data Engineer (Azure / Databricks / Python)




Who we are

We are Two Circles. We are a Sports & Entertainment Marketing business. We grow audiences and revenues. We do that by knowing fans best. We work with clients to help them understand & influence what their fans are doing - the way fans spend their money, the events that fans attend, the channels fans respond to, the content fans watch and more. And we use the understanding this gives us to help our clients grow. Grow their audiences and grow their revenues - both direct to consumer and business to business revenues. Our platforms and services are trusted by over 900 clients globally, including the English Premier League, Red Bull, UEFA, VISA, the NFL, Nike and Amazon. We are almost 1000 people, based out of 14 offices, and we deliver work for sports and entertainment businesses of all shapes and sizes all over the world.

 

What you will do

We're looking for a talented Data Engineer to join our squad of >100 Data & BI Engineers, working on our Audience Intelligence Platform. You will be helping to build the leading data management and analytics platform for the sports industry. In this role, you'll work with cutting-edge technologies to design, build, and optimize scalable data solutions, enabling teams, leagues, and organizations to unlock the full potential of their data. If you're passionate about data engineering, enjoy collaborating in a fast-paced environment, and want to make an impact in the sports industry, we'd love to hear from you!

 

Your role will be varied and evolve as quickly as you can learn and develop. Your initial duties will involve:


Design, build, and test scalable data platform solutions in Azure, leveraging tools like ADF, Databricks, and Function Apps, using Python.
Develop high-quality data products, ensuring efficiency, reliability, and performance across our data pipelines.
Collaborate closely with product and marketing teams to deliver data-driven insights and solutions that provide high business value.
Write clean, maintainable code following best practices and development frameworks, ensuring robust data engineering standards.
Continuously improve and optimize data workflows, monitoring performance and implementing enhancements to drive innovation.


Requirements



Requirements

You will have experience with the following:

Experience in designing, building, and testing solutions within a data lakehouse environment.
Proficiency in Python and PySpark for data processing and analytics.
Hands-on experience with Azure, preferably ADF, Databricks, and Function Apps.
Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines using Azure DevOps or similar tools.
Ability to work within established development frameworks and adhere to coding standards.
Excellent communication skills, with an ability to articulate blockers and timelines to non-technical stakeholders.

 

While the above skills are important to this role, we believe that attitude and ability to learn new skills are the most important factors for success in this team. If you are missing some of the experience listed above but this sounds like you, we'd love to hear from you.

 

Strong teamwork skills, with a collaborative mindset and desire to learn from others.
Strong SQL skills for querying and managing data.
A proactive attitude with a passion for continuous learning and growth.
High attention to detail and commitment to delivering quality work.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment, with the ability to estimate tasks and deliver incremental value effectively.


Benefits



Professional Growth: Work on a variety of projects, enhancing your testing skills across different applications and technologies.

Impactful Work: Play a key role in delivering high-quality solutions that shape the future of the sports and entertainment industries.

Collaborative Environment: Be part of a team that values ideas, fosters a supportive atmosphere, and encourages continuous learning and improvement.

Innovative Culture: Join a company committed to revolutionizing fan and stakeholder engagement through cutting-edge technology.

Equal Opportunity Employer: Two Circles is an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.


The range below represents the low and high end of the base salary someone in this role may earn as an employee of Two Circles. Salaries will vary based on various factors including but not limited to professional and academic experience, training, associated responsibilities, and other business and organizational needs. The range listed is just one component of our total compensation package for employees. Salary decisions are dependent on the circumstances of each hire.

Our platforms and services are trusted by over 900 clients globally, including the English Premier League, Red Bull, UEFA, VISA, the NFL, Nike and Amazon. We are almost 1000 people, based out of 14 offices, and we deliver work for sports and entertainment businesses of all shapes and sizes all over the world.

 

What you will do

We're looking for a talented Data Engineer to join our squad of >100 Data & BI Engineers, working on our Audience Intelligence Platform. You will be helping to build the leading data management and analytics platform for the sports industry. In this role, you'll work with cutting-edge technologies to design, build, and optimize scalable data solutions, enabling teams, leagues, and organizations to unlock the full potential of their data. If you're passionate about data engineering, enjoy collaborating in a fast-paced environment, and want to make an impact in the sports industry, we'd love to hear from you!

 

Your role will be varied and evolve as quickly as you can learn and develop. Your initial duties will involve:

  • Design, build, and test scalable data platform solutions in Azure, leveraging tools like ADF, Databricks, and Function Apps, using Python.
  • Develop high-quality data products, ensuring efficiency, reliability, and performance across our data pipelines.
  • Collaborate closely with product and marketing teams to deliver data-driven insights and solutions that provide high business value.
  • Write clean, maintainable code following best practices and development frameworks, ensuring robust data engineering standards.
  • Continuously improve and optimize data workflows, monitoring performance and implementing enhancements to drive innovation.

Requirements

Requirements

You will have experience with the following:

  • Experience in designing, building, and testing solutions within a data lakehouse environment.
  • Proficiency in Python and PySpark for data processing and analytics.
  • Hands-on experience with Azure, preferably ADF, Databricks, and Function Apps.
  • Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines using Azure DevOps or similar tools.
  • Ability to work within established development frameworks and adhere to coding standards.
  • Excellent communication skills, with an ability to articulate blockers and timelines to non-technical stakeholders.

 

While the above skills are important to this role, we believe that attitude and ability to learn new skills are the most important factors for success in this team. If you are missing some of the experience listed above but this sounds like you, we'd love to hear from you.

 

  • Strong teamwork skills, with a collaborative mindset and desire to learn from others.
  • Strong SQL skills for querying and managing data.
  • A proactive attitude with a passion for continuous learning and growth.
  • High attention to detail and commitment to delivering quality work.
  • Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment, with the ability to estimate tasks and deliver incremental value effectively.

Benefits

Professional Growth: Work on a variety of projects, enhancing your testing skills across different applications and technologies.

Impactful Work: Play a key role in delivering high-quality solutions that shape the future of the sports and entertainment industries.

Collaborative Environment: Be part of a team that values ideas, fosters a supportive atmosphere, and encourages continuous learning and improvement.

Innovative Culture: Join a company committed to revolutionizing fan and stakeholder engagement through cutting-edge technology.

Equal Opportunity Employer: Two Circles is an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

The range below represents the low and high end of the base salary someone in this role may earn as an employee of Two Circles. Salaries will vary based on various factors including but not limited to professional and academic experience, training, associated responsibilities, and other business and organizational needs. The range listed is just one component of our total compensation package for employees. Salary decisions are dependent on the circumstances of each hire.

Title: Analyst - Deltek Vision/Vantagepoint
Description:
TYLin is a globally recognized, full-service infrastructure consulting firm committed to providing innovative, cost-effective, constructible designs for the global infrastructure market. With over 3,000 employees throughout the Americas, Asia, and Europe, the firm provides support on projects of varying size and complexity. Together, we enhance conventional designs with smarter, more resilient systems. We provide people with better mobility. We steward precious resources by finding more sustainable solutions.




Job Summary

Global Infrastructure is seeking an experienced Analyst with hands-on Deltek Vision or Vantagepoint expertise to support our current Vision environment and assist in transition to Vantagepoint. This role will immediately contribute to system support, data validation, testing, and reporting, with minimal ramp-up time.




Responsibilities & Qualifications




Key Responsibilities

Provide functional support for Deltek Vision/Vantagepoint, including troubleshooting, issue resolution, and user support
Lead and facilitate requirements gathering sessions with business stakeholders
Translate business needs into clear, actionable requirements and documentation
Support data migration validation, system testing, and UAT activities
Create and maintain reports within Deltek Vision/Vantagepoint (required)
Analyze project financial and operational data to support business needs
Develop user documentation and support training efforts
Identify and recommend process improvements and system efficiencies




Required Qualifications

3–5 years of direct, hands-on experience with Deltek Vision or Deltek Vantagepoint in a support, analyst, or configuration role
Experience within the AEC (Architecture, Engineering, Construction) industry
Proven experience working with project financial data (budgets, billing, revenue, cost, etc.)
Demonstrated ability to create reports within Deltek Vision/Vantagepoint
Experience supporting system testing, UAT, and/or data migration activities
Strong requirements gathering and documentation skills
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision and contribute immediately
Strong communication skills with business stakeholders




Preferred Qualifications

Experience with Deltek Vantagepoint implementations or migrations
Familiarity with SSRS, SQL, or other reporting tools
Experience creating training materials and conducting user training




Important Note

This role requires prior Deltek Vision or Vantagepoint experience. Candidates without direct experience in these systems will not be considered.







Additional Information

TYLin is committed to pay equity. As part of that commitment, we have provided the base compensation range of $70,000 - $85,000 annually for this position and local market. We recognize that each candidate brings a unique set of skills, experience, education and competencies to the table that will be reflected in our offer. TYLin is proud to offer exciting career development opportunities.




TYLin offers a comprehensive total rewards package. Our benefits may include extensive medical, disability and life insurance coverage, continuing education benefits, paid time off, employee assistance program and a retirement savings plan with company matching contributions.

We encourage all candidates to explore our total rewards offering.




Collaboration, mission-driven focus, discipline, and leadership skills developed through military service benefit us all. Whether you are a Veteran, Guardsman or Reservist, a Military Spouse or you are planning to transition to civilian life in the near future, we welcome and value your application.

TYLin is an equal opportunity employer, and we prohibit discrimination and harassment of any type as protected by federal, provincial, state or local laws. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. The company and its employees are required to comply with all local health authority, legal or lawful client requirements.

Title: Business Intelligence Engineer, IT PnD Data and Analytics
Description:
Description

IT PnD Data and Analytics is seeking a Business Intelligence Engineer to join our BI team.

As a Business Intelligence Engineer with our team, you will be working in a large, complex and dynamic, data-driven environment. You will be responsible for developing automated metrics that provide relevant insight to organizational leaders, building and maintaining dashboards, and performing complex data analytics.

This position requires the candidate to develop a deep understanding of the organization and key goals, earn trust with senior leadership, influence without authority, and provide relevant data insights.

To succeed, you must have the ability to engage deeply with diverse and talented business partners, earn trust using facts and data, and advise and influence decisions of senior business leaders through effective verbal and written communication.

This candidate must possess excellent analytical skills, project management experience, and a track record of delivering results in a complex and ambiguous environment.

Key job responsibilities


Perform complex business analysis to help drive organizational decision-making
Complete root cause analysis and provide recommendations to senior leadership to address opportunities
Develop automated metrics that will give senior business leaders insight into the performance of their respective teams and the organization as a whole
Develop and maintain Tableau dashboards
Develop key metrics and shared goals that determine success for a particular area
Take on shared ownership of the weekly organizational metrics scorecard


A day in the life

A day in the life of a BI engineer at IT PnD involves building large-scale reporting solutions using tools like Tableau and QuickSight. The day typically starts with a team stand-up to align on priorities, followed by deep work on developing and refining data transformation, building reports and automating processes. Collaboration with data scientists, analysts, and stakeholders is key to ensure the delivery of right metrics. The day ends with monitoring, code reviews, and documenting progress, all while keeping scalability, efficiency, and Amazon’s high standards for data quality in mind.

Basic Qualifications


3+ years of analyzing and interpreting data with Redshift, Oracle, NoSQL etc. experience
Experience with data visualization using Tableau, Quicksight, or similar tools
Experience with data modeling, warehousing and building ETL pipelines
Experience writing complex SQL queries
Experience in Statistical Analysis packages such as R, SAS and Matlab
Experience using SQL to pull data from a database or data warehouse and scripting experience (Python) to process data for modeling


Preferred Qualifications


Experience in the data/BI space
Experience in analyzing and interpreting data with Redshift, Oracle, NoSQL etc.


Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

The base salary range for this position is listed below. As a total compensation company, Amazon's package may include other elements such as sign-on payments and restricted stock units (RSUs). Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, basic life & AD&D insurance), Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP), Deferred Profit Sharing Plan (DPSP), paid time off, and other resources to improve health and well-being. We thank all applicants for their interest, however only those interviewed will be advised as to hiring status.

CAN, BC, Vancouver - 76,400.00 - 127,600.00 CAD annually

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Are you energized by building the infrastructure that turns raw data into business decisions? Do you thrive in environments where engineering rigour, cross-functional collaboration, and a bias for automation define success? If you’re ready to help shape the data foundation of a fast-moving ecommerce technology company, Cymax Group Technologies wants you on our team.

About Cymax Group Technologies

At Cymax Group Technologies, we enable leading brands and retailers to compete and win in the digital marketplace through advanced technology, data-driven insights, and optimized logistics. We are a tech-enabled brand accelerator operating in home, lifestyle, and adjacent categories. Our proprietary platforms—Channel Gate and Freight Club—combine automation, AI, and more than two decades of ecommerce expertise to streamline multi-channel selling, increase operational efficiency, and drive scalable growth.

We support hundreds of partners across North America, helping them navigate and outperform within complex marketplaces such as Amazon, Walmart, Wayfair, and others. Our focus is simple: deliver performance, remove friction, and create sustainable competitive advantage for our partners.

Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing technology companies, Cymax Group fosters a high-performance culture built on grit, agility, and accountability—where we take ownership, use data to drive clear decisions, think strategically while executing with precision, and consistently raise the bar to deliver results today that position us for tomorrow.

The Role

We are looking for a technically strong and collaborative Data Engineer to join our Pricing & Data Science team. You’ll design, build, and maintain the scalable data pipelines and architectures that power our analytics and business intelligence capabilities—working across a hybrid cloud and on-premises environment in a fast-moving ecommerce context.

This role supports both our eCommerce and Freight Club business units and works closely with data analysts, data scientists, software engineers, and business stakeholders to ensure our data infrastructure supports current and future needs.

This is a hybrid role, based in the Vancouver, BC area. The ideal candidate will work 2 days in the Vancouver office, with the flexibility to work remotely for the remainder of the week.

What You’ll Do

Pipeline & Architecture


Design, develop, and deploy scalable ETL/ELT processes to ingest, transform, and store structured and unstructured data from various internal and external sources.
Optimize data workflows for performance, reliability, and cost-efficiency in a hybrid cloud (Azure/Databricks) and on-prem environment (SQL Server).
Maintain and enhance our data warehouse, data lake, and other storage solutions, ensuring they are performant and resilient.
Evaluate and integrate new tools and technologies to support evolving data needs and industry best practices.


Data Quality & Governance


Implement data quality, validation, and governance frameworks to ensure high data integrity across the organization.
Automate routine data operations and develop monitoring systems to proactively detect and resolve issues.
Develop and maintain clear documentation of data pipelines, processes, and architectural decisions.


Collaboration & Enablement


Partner with analytics and data science teams to understand their requirements and deliver clean, well-modelled datasets that enable self-service analytics and advanced modelling.
Collaborate cross-functionally with analysts, data scientists, software engineers, and business stakeholders to ensure infrastructure keeps pace with business needs.
Leverage AI tools to accelerate engineering workflows, applying sound judgement to validate outputs and maintain quality.


Who You Are


Proven proficiency in data engineering, including ETL/ELT pipeline development and SQL-based data warehousing.
Strong SQL skills and hands-on experience with modern data warehousing solutions.
Comfortable working across hybrid environments—supporting both on-prem (SQL Server) and cloud-based data warehouses (Databricks).
Proficiency in Python, Scala, or Java for data pipeline development.
Experience with orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Prefect, Dagster), integration platforms (e.g., Celigo), and version control systems (e.g., Git).
A systems thinker—you can trace data from source to output, spot reliability gaps, and propose fixes before they become incidents.
Automation-minded—you look for ways to eliminate manual, repetitive work and build durable solutions.
A clear communicator who can document decisions and explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.


Nice to Have


Experience with ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite) as a data source, including familiarity with their data structures and APIs.
Exposure to business intelligence tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker) and semantic layer concepts.
Background in ecommerce, logistics, or supply chain data environments.


What We Offer


Impact: Your pipelines and architecture will directly enable the data decisions that drive business performance.
Growth: Opportunities for professional development and career advancement within a scaling analytics team.
Hybrid-First Environment: Flexibility and autonomy in how and where you work.
Culture: A collaborative, high-trust team where engineering quality and continuous improvement are genuinely valued.


Our Values

Our core values are the compass that guides our actions, unites our team, and shapes the future of our company:


Grit & Agility: Forge Ahead, Pivot Fast
Accountability: Bring It, Own It, Commit
Go the Extra Mile: We Go Above & Beyond
Understand the Map & the Terrain: Master the Details & the Big Picture
Data Drives Decisions: Absorb Complexity & Transmit Clarity
Think Beyond Today: Tomorrow’s Vision, Today’s Action


Compensation & Benefits

We are committed to transparency in our hiring process. The expected salary range for this role is $85,000–$110,000 depending on various factors including experience, skills, and market benchmarks for the Vancouver area. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location.

We offer a comprehensive range of benefits which includes:


Health and wellness support for our team members and their families, including an employee assistance program.
100% paid health and dental benefits in Canada.
Paid time off.
Easy access to online and phone-based counselling services.
A hybrid-first, flexible work environment.


Join Our AI Journey at Cymax Group

At Cymax Group, we thrive on innovation and entrepreneurship. As an AI-first organization, we are on an exciting journey to continuously grow and evolve our focus on AI, empowering our people at all levels. Our fast-paced environment encourages agility and nimbleness, enabling our team to adapt swiftly to changing market dynamics. We’re passionate about continuous learning and growth, and our data-driven decision-making ensures we make informed choices that lead to success.

If you’re excited about building the data infrastructure that powers an AI-empowered team, you’ll feel right at home here.

Join us and bring your passion for data engineering to drive meaningful infrastructure and innovation in our ecommerce journey. Please submit your resume detailing your relevant experience and why you are a great fit for this role.

Accessibility

We can provide accessibility accommodations during the recruitment process. Should you require any accommodation, please let us know and we will work with you to meet your needs.

Title: Data Engineer 1
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ more than 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Data Engineer to join our IT team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Manager, Data & Analytics, you will be responsible for working with one of the largest, most comprehensive datasets in Canada while delivering and pioneering solutions that will disrupt the retail industry using the latest tools and technologies.

You will be responsible for:


Designing and constructing high-performance, scalable data pipelines to operationalize our state-of-the-art solutions ranging from quantum computing pipelines to deep learning based recommender systems
Creating innovative solutions to tackle computationally intensive challenges and business issues
Writing production Python/Scala scripts in a Spark/Databricks environment to support the data pipelines that feed Machine Learning models
Leveraging cloud environments and following best practices while building solutions in the data lake as it evolves to meet the needs of the business
Performing data wrangling, feature engineering, and dimension reduction with data of all shapes and sizes, from parquet files to images
Partnering with Machine Learning Engineers, Data Scientists, and Cloud Architects to deliver models into production
Coaching, mentoring, and providing feedback to team members and junior data engineers
Discovering novel datasets and data sources and making them available for modeling and reporting
Using your expertise in big data technologies to champion innovation, design, and implement data-stores and analytic models, oversee large-scale data lake platforms, and design and implement efficient data pipelines from source systems to data-stores
Defining and implementing Quality Assurance best practices that support the implementation of data transformations and the availability of high-quality data.



You have:


A Bachelor’s degree in a STEM discipline at minimum, a Master’s is preferred.
A minimum 2 years of experience in creating and managing large-scale, distributed data

Title: Population Health Informaticist
Description:
Population Health Informaticist (AI Training)

About The Role

At Alignerr, we partner with the world's leading AI research teams and labs to build and train cutting-edge AI models. We're looking for experienced Population Health Informaticists to apply their expertise to AI projects focused on improving how intelligent systems understand, analyze, and act on population-level health data.

Your domain knowledge will directly shape the quality and accuracy of AI tools built for public health — meaningful work that extends beyond the screen.


Organization: Alignerr
Type: Hourly Contract
Location: Remote
Commitment: 10–40 hours/week


What You'll Do


Evaluate and improve AI-generated analyses of population health datasets for accuracy, depth, and clinical relevance
Identify gaps, errors, or misinterpretations in AI outputs related to health trends, disparities, and community outcomes
Provide structured expert feedback on data pipelines, dashboard logic, and health surveillance frameworks
Apply your knowledge of epidemiology, health equity, and public health informatics to guide AI reasoning
Work independently and asynchronously on task-based assignments — fully on your own schedule


Who You Are


Experienced working with healthcare, public health, or population-level datasets
Skilled in data analysis, visualization, or health informatics tools and reporting frameworks
Able to translate complex analytical findings into clear, actionable insights
Detail-oriented with strong written communication skills
Comfortable evaluating and critiquing technical health data work at scale


Nice to Have


Background in epidemiology, biostatistics, public health, or health informatics
Experience with data annotation, quality assurance, or evaluation systems
Familiarity with AI or machine learning workflows in a health context
Prior work in health equity, surveillance systems, or population health management


Why Join Us


Work on cutting-edge AI projects with top research labs shaping the future of health AI
Fully remote and flexible — work on your own schedule, from anywhere
Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and collaboration with a global expert community
Apply your expertise to projects that have real public health significance
Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

Title: Data Developer
Description:
The salary range for this position is CAD $38.90/Hr. - CAD $55.92/Hr.

Come work as a Data Developer with Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH)!

Vancouver Coastal Health is looking for a Data Developer to join the Data and Analytics team. Apply today to join our team!

As a Data Developer with Vancouver Coastal Health you will


Provide advanced technical expertise in leading other data Developers to build and deploy data solutions and pipelines across on-premises, public, private, and hybrid cloud environments.
Support projects covering all programs/services within Data and Analytics and throughout Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH).
Be key member of the department’s team, and be responsible for designing, maintaining, and troubleshooting data models, data pipelines, and implementing business logic needed to meet a wide range of business requirements.
Work closely with project managers, leadership and other functions across the department and VCH.
Collaborate with the Principal Data Developers, VCH business and analytics teams, external vendors (e.g. Microsoft, AWS) and other internal or external teams on cross-functional projects.
Have a primary focus of ensuring Data and Analytics’ ability to deliver and promote on-premises and cloud-based data solutions within the approved enterprise environments, the Senior Data Developer will lead the effort in the areas of design, migration, optimization, and support experience in those environments.



This work is framed within VCH’s commitments to our pillars of Indigenous Cultural Safety, Anti-Racism, Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, and Planetary Health.

Education & Experience


Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems Management or a data intensive related field plus at least seven (7) years’ experience as a Data Engineer two (2) of which will have been in a senior role or an equivalent combination of education, training and experience.
Data Engineering related certification from at least one cloud service provider required (e.g. Microsoft Azure Data Engineer Associate).



Knowledge & Abilities


Demonstrated ability to implement data infrastructure, database, and data solutions with on-prem and cloud tools.
Demonstrated hands-on experience optimizing data solutions and pipelines ensuring scalability, performance, and cost effectiveness are addressed.
Demonstrated proficiency developing in SQL languages such as T-SQL, PL/SQL, Databricks SQL.
Demonstrated proficiency in the use of Python, Scala, and Spark with an ability to write efficient and scalable code for data processing/transformation tasks.
Demonstrated understanding of coding best practices.
Experience in data modeling, ETL development, and data warehousing architecture with solid knowledge of various industry standards.
Understanding of software data development, deployment and release processes, and automated test frameworks.
Experience with cloud platforms such as Azure, AWS, or GCP and related data services (e.g Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Azure Data Lake).
Strong analytical, prioritizing, interpersonal, problem-solving, presentation, budgeting, project management (from conception to completion), & planning skills.
Develops and maintains strong relationships with key internal and external partners to promote and enhance services provided with sensitivity to diversity and indigenous lens.
Practices the recognition, inclusion and support of all types and aspects of diversity at all stages of engagement, with an understanding of the issues faced by people from equity-deserving groups.
Strong interpersonal skills including strengths in working in a team environment with a client/user orientation.
Ability to work effectively with multi-disciplinary teams with different lines of reporting.
Excellent oral and written communication skills with the ability to effectively communicate complex ideas and processes in simple terminology to internal and external stakeholders, including all levels of management.
Demonstrated attention to detail and maintenance of high quality standards.
Demonstrated ability to work independently, meeting deadlines under pressure, with minimal supervision and frequent interruptions.
Works effectively under time pressure to meet deadlines, balance work priorities and resolve problems in a timely manner.
Physical ability to perform the duties of the position.



The hours of work including days off and work area may be subject to change consistent with operational requirements and the provision of the Collective Agreement and applicable statutes. Successful applicants may be required to complete a Criminal Records Review Check.

As per Ministry of Health policy, all health care workers working in publicly-funded health care facilities are required to report their past receipt of certain vaccines or history of certain infections. Collecting these records will allow for offering of any missing vaccines, and for appropriate actions to be taken in the event of any future exposure to a communicable disease or during outbreaks. For all new hires and appointments to Vancouver Coastal Health, you will be asked to provide this information as part of the onboarding process.

Why Join Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH)

VCH is a world class innovator in medical care, research and teaching, delivering service to more than one million BC residents. At VCH, we embrace thinking boldly, taking smart risks, and 'going first' when we believe it will lead to the best possible outcomes for patients and their families. We invite you to join us in creating healthy lives in healthy communities by showcasing our passion for care, connection to the communities we serve and our culture of teamwork that makes VCH a great place to work.


Comprehensive health benefits package, including MSP, extended health and dental and municipal pension plan
Grow your career with employer-paid training and leadership development opportunities
Wellness supports, including counselling, critical incident and innovative wellness services are available to employees and their immediate families
Award-winning recognition programs to honour staff, medical staff and volunteers
Access to exclusive discount offers and deals for VCH staff



At Vancouver Coastal Health, our vision of healthy lives in healthy communities inspires us and our values and pillars guide us. We support equitable practices, diverse teams and inclusive environments, which are vital to creating welcoming spaces where everyone can bring their whole selves to work and feel supported. We encourage applications from equity-deserving communities, including Indigenous Peoples and members of racialized groups, people with disabilities and people of all gender identities and expressions and sexual orientation.

Vancouver Coastal Health is proud to be recognized as one of Canada’s Top 100 Employers, BC’s Top Employers, Canada’s Best Diversity Employers and Canada's Top Employers for Young People in 2026.

Only short-listed applicants will be contacted for this posting.

***Employees of VCH must apply online via the Internal Career Portal on CareerHub, you are currently viewing the External Career Portal. Refer to the https//my.vch.ca/working-here/job-postings site for instructions on how to view internal job postings and how to apply as an employee. Current VCH employees who apply to this posting using this external site will be considered as an external candidate. Seniority will not apply.***

Thank you for your interest in Vancouver Coastal Health.

Title: Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst
Description:
Why You’ll Love Softchoice

We are a software-focused IT solutions and services provider that equips organizations to be agile and innovative, and for their people to be engaged, connected, and creative at work. That means moving them to the cloud, helping them build the workplace of tomorrow, and enabling them to make smarter decisions about their technology. By doing these things we help them create success for their customers and their people.

We stand proudly for our people and support their success through career development and advancement. We are recognized and respected for our culture of inclusion and belonging, continuously striving to do what’s good for our people and communities.

The Impact You Will Have

The Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst will be responsible for owning the design, delivery, and maintenance of People and Culture (P&C) reporting and business analysis. This role serves as the subject-matter expert on workforce data—driving reporting strategy, ensuring data integrity across P&C systems, and providing consultative guidance to senior leaders that shapes how the organization makes people decisions. As a senior team member, you will partner cross-functionally with business analysts and operational leaders across Softchoice to combine people data with business performance data, turning insight into action. You will lead end-to-end analytics initiatives with minimal direction, influence without authority, and bring executive presence to every stakeholder interaction.

What You’ll Do


Develop and own reporting dashboards using data mining analytics and reporting tools (SuccessFactors Story, ADP Vantage, PowerBI, Power Platform) to drive performance monitoring and executive decision-making.
Design, develop, and maintain data and statistical models, including complex data manipulation from multiple sources that generate actionable insights for leadership.
Serve as the P&C analytics subject-matter expert, proactively advising senior leaders on key metrics, trends, and data-driven recommendations that inform workforce strategy
Conduct a review of the intake and prioritization of reporting requests and ensure reporting data is presented in an understandable and user-friendly format.
Engage stakeholders at all levels in defining requirements, developing technical specifications, analyzing data flow, and completing testing for report execution.
Conduct audits on data extracted to ensure the highest quality is being used for reporting.
Perform quality assurance checks on system configurations including assessment of changes in SAP bi-annual releases to determine the impact.
Use HR system and process expertise to analyze and troubleshoot issues, identify root causes, and determine and execute resolution.
Support the implementation and maintenance of data and reporting strategy and governance within P&C and throughout the partnership with business areas.
Champion the empowerment of stakeholders at various levels in leveraging reporting tools to support strategic and operational needs.
Respond to benchmarking surveys, and prepare analysis on employee data across P&C Systems
Serve as the SME for P&C analytics, by owning the timely generation of recurring metrics.
Ensure reporting roles and access are defined and maintained in partnership with IT governance and SuccessFactors Administrators
Create and maintain Knowledge-Based Documentation (KBDs) related to new developments and enhancements in reports and dashboards for knowledge sharing and job shadowing.
Onboard new HR Technology team members on P&C reporting tools, including training and change management.
Works with other HR Technology team members to build awareness of data and process changes impacting business metrics.
Maintains current knowledge of reporting trends and best practices for long-term solutions.



What You’ll Bring To The Table


Bachelor’s degree in business analytics, management analytics, math, statistics, computer science or related field
A minimum of 5 years’ experience working with Analytics tools at an advanced level with 2 years in PowerBI and Power Platform tools.
Experience with data visualization tools with at least 1 full implementation of Planning, Forecasting BI, and Predictive Analytics
Enrolled or completing Data Analytics certification or programs is an asset (Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst or People Analytics programs)
Demonstrated experience in using queries and syntax to conduct data mining, transformation, cleansing, and visualization.
Intermediate to Advanced experience with Microsoft Office products and SQL for querying relational databases.
A broad understanding of HR business fundamentals with at least 4 years of HR operations experience
Experience in 1-2 full-cycle SAP SuccessFactors implementation projects is an asset.
Experience with owning governance, controls, processes, data modeling, master data management, data standardization
Should be able to model positive behavior in the engagement with others and display the desired values and culture of the organization.
Demonstrated ability to drive initiatives end-to-end with minimal direction, taking full ownership from problem identification through solution delivery.
Works collaboratively to provide services that are sought after and recognized for being of high quality.
Executive presence with the ability to distill complex technical information into clear, actionable narratives for senior leadership and non-technical audiences.
Must be able to handle multiple tasks and projects with deadlines and adaptable to changes in priority level that require a high level of organization and planning skills.
Must be able to work quickly and efficiently without sacrificing the quality of their work.
Detail-oriented with proven analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong consultative skillset—able to frame technology issues, plans, and solutions from both a business and technical perspective, providing both pros and cons for recommended solutions to peers, people leaders, and senior leadership.
Excellent documentation skills (Process and training manuals, workbook updates)



Compensation

A reasonable estimate of the current base pay range for this position is $76,500 to $102,000 annually + 10% Target Incentives.

Actual salary will be based on a variety of factors, including location, experience, skill set, education, and related certification. The range for this position in other geographic locations may differ.

Softchoice offers a comprehensive and competitive benefit plan to all full-time employees, which includes:


Health and Wellbeing: Medical and Dental Care, Employee & Family Assistance Program
Financial Benefits: RRSP/DPSP Retirement Savings Plan with Company Matching, Life and Disability Insurance
Paid Time Off: Vacation and Sick Leave, Holidays, Parental Leave, Volunteer Days, Bereavement Leave
Additional Perks: Employee Discount Program



Not sure if you qualify? Think about applying anyway:

We understand that not everyone brings 100% of the skills and experience for the role.

At Softchoice, we offer opportunities to a diverse group including those with a variety of workplace experiences and backgrounds. Whether you are new to corporate tech, returning to work after a gap in employment, or looking to transition and take the next step in your career, we are excited to learn more about you and encourage you to apply.

Why You’ll Love Working Here


The People: You’ll thrive in our collaborative environment, surrounded by incredible colleagues who foster support and innovation, driving our collective success
High-Performing Culture: At Softchoice, we are dedicated to achieving our goals and committed to success for our customers and each other
Flexibility: Plan your workdays in a way that suits you best
Award-Winning Workplace: Proudly recognized as a Great Place to Work for 20 consecutive years
Inclusive Culture: We are committed to an inclusive culture where every team member can be their authentic self
Competitive Benefits: Benefit from competitive perks that start on day one



Inclusion & Equal Opportunity Employment

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, inclusion & belonging. People seeking employment at Softchoice are considered without regard to any protected category including but not limited to, race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation.

Require accommodation? We are ready to help:

We are proud to provide interview & employment accommodation during the recruitment and hiring process. If you require any accommodation to apply or interview for a position, please reach out directly to asktalentacquisition@softchoice.com . We are committed to working with you to best meet your needs.

Our Commitment To Your Experience

We are committed to the safety of all applicants and team members. With that in mind, we have implemented digital interviewing for everyone. We understand that you may need to interview with distractions around you (such as children or furry friends) and we will be doing the same.

Before you start with us, we will conduct a criminal record check, verify your education, and check your references.

When you join Softchoice, we will onboard you remotely. Don't worry. It's quick, simple and you'll be connected with your new team in no time.

Job Requisition ID: 7503

EoE/M/F/Vet/Disability

  • A broad understanding of HR business fundamentals with at least 4 years of HR operations experience
  • Experience in 1-2 full-cycle SAP SuccessFactors implementation projects is an asset.
  • Experience with owning governance, controls, processes, data modeling, master data management, data standardization
  • Should be able to model positive behavior in the engagement with others and display the desired values and culture of the organization.
  • Demonstrated ability to drive initiatives end-to-end with minimal direction, taking full ownership from problem identification through solution delivery.
  • Works collaboratively to provide services that are sought after and recognized for being of high quality.
  • Executive presence with the ability to distill complex technical information into clear, actionable narratives for senior leadership and non-technical audiences.
  • Must be able to handle multiple tasks and projects with deadlines and adaptable to changes in priority level that require a high level of organization and planning skills.
  • Must be able to work quickly and efficiently without sacrificing the quality of their work.
  • Detail-oriented with proven analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Strong consultative skillset—able to frame technology issues, plans, and solutions from both a business and technical perspective, providing both pros and cons for recommended solutions to peers, people leaders, and senior leadership.
  • Excellent documentation skills (Process and training manuals, workbook updates)


Compensation

A reasonable estimate of the current base pay range for this position is $76,500 to $102,000 annually + 10% Target Incentives.

Actual salary will be based on a variety of factors, including location, experience, skill set, education, and related certification. The range for this position in other geographic locations may differ.

  • Health and Wellbeing: Medical and Dental Care, Employee & Family Assistance Program

  • Paid Time Off: Vacation and Sick Leave, Holidays, Parental Leave, Volunteer Days, Bereavement Leave

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Remarcable is purpose-built for trade contractors, helping them manage procurement, tools, and warehouse operations with unmatched visibility and control. Remarcable As we scale across North America, data is at the core of how we deliver on that promise.

As our Data Engineer, you'll build and maintain the AWS Lakehouse infrastructure, ELT pipelines, and data models that power decisions across our product, operations, and leadership teams. You'll also build the AI/ML data infrastructure that drives the next generation of Remarcable's intelligent features — work that will be felt by contractors and back-office teams alike.

This is a high-ownership role for someone who loves clean data architecture and wants their work to directly shape a product transforming the construction industry.

Key Responsibilities:


Build and maintain scalable ELT pipelines and AWS Lakehouse infrastructure (S3, Redshift, Athena, Glue) to deliver reliable data across the organization
Design multi-layer data architecture and dbt transformation models that produce clean, well-documented datasets
Enforce data quality, governance, and security standards including PII handling using AWS Lake Formation and IAM policies
Own pipelines for core SaaS metrics including ARR, churn, NRR, and product usage
Build AI/ML data infrastructure including feature stores, training datasets, and embedding pipelines supporting SageMaker and Bedrock workflows


Who You Are


3+ years of experience in a Data Engineering or similar role (Analytics Engineer, Backend Engineer with data focus).
Strong SQL skills — complex queries, window functions, performance tuning.
Experience building and maintaining ELT/ETL pipelines in production.
Proficiency in Python for data engineering tasks (scripting, pipeline logic, tooling).
Hands-on experience with a modern cloud data warehouse


Who We Are

Remarcable is a cloud based platform that helps electrical contractors and distributors streamline purchasing processes to save time and money.

Dedicated to the Electrical Contractor Industry, Remarcable provides cloud-based Procurement & Tool Management Software nationwide. With multiple workflows, two applications in one software, and direct contractor accounting integrations, Remarcable significantly, and efficiently, increases communication, streamlines workflows, and provides visibility for all users.

Our team is composed of contractor and distribution experts located coast to coast. Through collaborations with industry leaders, we've gained insight into the struggles they face. Together, we believe in providing a solution that brings efficiency, visibility, and better communication to streamline the relationship between the contractor and distributor partners.

Our Mission

To advance the adoption of technology in the construction industry and bring better efficiency, visibility, and communication to our customers.

#ZR

Please be aware of potential phishing scams. Remarcable will never advance job applicants money or ask them to send money (via Venmo, Zelle, Paypal etc.) to preferred vendors. If you are concerned about the application process, please contact Remarcable directly at (216)770-3322.

Remarcable is not associated with info-remarcable.com. For information about applying for a position with Remarcable, please reference remarcable.com.

Compensation Range: CA$130K - CA$150K

Title: Senior Analyst, Creative Analytics
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

Senior Analyst, Creative Analytics

Workplace Type: Hybrid (Vancouver or Austin)

Reporting Line: Director, Franchise Marketing Analytics

Studio/Department Blurb

The Office of the CXO drives effectiveness across the EA Experiences organization with a focus on business operations, fan intelligence, and technology solutions. Within this group, the Fan Intelligence team delivers insights that deepen understanding of player behavior, audience trends, and engagement across EA’s portfolio. We partner with franchise and marketing teams to connect audience insights with content and creative performance.

Impact

You will lead creative analytics initiatives that connect fan behavior with content performance, shaping marketing strategy and improving creative effectiveness across franchises.

Responsibilities


Lead analysis across player, media, and social datasets
Evaluate creative effectiveness across trailers, campaigns, and creators
Build scalable dashboards and creative measurement frameworks
Synthesize audience and content data into actionable insights
Guide partners on content strategy, budgets, and asset planning
Embed analytics into creative and campaign workflows


Required Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in STEM, economics, or related field
5+ years of analytics experience in marketing, media, or product analytics
Advanced SQL skills across multiple data sources
Experience with statistical modeling (regression, experimentation)
Experience using R or Python for modeling and analysis


Core Skills And Experience


Experience analyzing creative or marketing performance
Advanced data visualization and dashboard design
Experience with media measurement or experimentation frameworks
Experience influencing decisions through data
Experience working with global, cross-functional teams


Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$92,900 - $129,200 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Business Intelligence Quality Assurance Analyst
Description:
Job Description

On behalf of our public sector client, Affinity is seeking a Business Intelligence Quality Assurance Analyst to provide quality assurance services in support of the Liquid Waste Services Business and Improvement Team. The successful respondent will be responsible for establishing, maturing, and sustaining a formal QA function for Power BI reports, dashboards, data models, and related analytics products that support business operations, regulatory compliance, resource planning, and performance management.

Responsibilities


Develop and execute test strategies, plans, and cases for Power BI solutions, while maintaining QA standards, regression test suites, release checklists, and other core QA deliverables that support consistent, reliable, and business-ready reporting for operational, compliance, resource planning, and performance management purposes.
Perform functional testing and regression testing across all analytics components to confirm reporting solutions remain reliable for business operations, compliance reporting, resource optimization, and KPI monitoring.
Ensure end-to-end data validation, including source-to-report accuracy, to confirm that reporting outputs support operational decisions, regulatory and compliance reporting, resource planning, and KPI measurement and optimization.
Establish and enforce a formal QA gate prior to release to support reliable business reporting, compliance readiness, and controlled deployment of operational analytics.
Identify, document, prioritize, and track defects and issues using standard tools (e.g., DevOps or equivalent)
Collaborate with developers to ensure timely resolution of defects
Validate fixes and confirm issues are resolved before production deployment
Validate data integrity and consistency across shared Power BI models and downstream reports to support accurate operational reporting, compliance requirements, resource planning, and KPI analysis.
Assess the impact of changes to data models on dependent reports and dashboards, including potential effects on operational decision-making, regulatory compliance, resource planning, and KPI reporting.
Ensure high standards of data accuracy, completeness, consistency, and traceability to support operational decision-making, regulatory compliance, audit readiness, resource allocation, and business performance reporting.
Standardize QA practices and introduce repeatable testing frameworks and regression coverage to improve reporting reliability, reduce rework, and support efficient use of business and technical resources.
Identify opportunities to improve testing efficiency, reduce rework, optimize resource use, and support more predictable delivery of reporting solutions that enable effective business operations and performance management.
Review business requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, and reporting specifications to ensure they are complete, clear, testable, and aligned with operational, regulatory, compliance, resource planning, and KPI reporting requirements.
Validate that dashboards and reports accurately reflect approved business rules, operational processes, regulatory obligations, resource planning assumptions, and key performance indicators used to monitor service delivery and business performance.
Support continuous improvement of business operations by identifying data quality, process, control, or reporting gaps that may affect compliance, resource allocation, operational efficiency, or KPI performance, and recommending mitigation or improvement opportunities.
Identify and recommend opportunities for test automation, reusable validation scripts, and process improvements to support standardized and efficient QA practices.
Develop and maintain reusable test assets, including regression test suites and data validation scripts, to improve testing consistency and coverage.


Qualifications


Strong understanding of:
Data validation and testing methodologies
SQL and data querying
Data modeling concepts (semantic models, relationships, measures)
Experience with test management and defect tracking tools (e.g., Azure DevOps, Jira)
Experience validating data from SQL Server, ETL pipelines, semantic models, and Power BI reports.
Experience with data profiling and data quality assessment
Understanding of DAX (Data Analysis Expressions) for validating measures and calculations
Strong understanding of Star schema & snowflake schema design
Strong attention to detail with the ability to identify data inconsistencies and anomalies
Ability to assess downstream impacts of data/model changes
Structured and methodical approach to testing and validation.
Additional preferred qualifications:
Power Query(M)
Microsoft Fabric


Affinity Earn

Know someone who’s great for this, or any of our open roles? Earn up to $4,000/year for each successful referral through Affinity Earn. You can also earn up to $50,000 for helping us find new clients. Learn about our referral program at https://affinity-group.ca/earn/ or browse our jobs & follow us at https://www.linkedin.com/company/affinity-staffing/jobs/

About Affinity

Affinity Group is a technology and business consulting and services company. We believe in creating long term relationships between clients and consultants that foster a mutually beneficial partnership. Affinity is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need.

For more information on Affinity, please visit www.affinity-group.ca
