Title: Application Analyst
Description:
Position: Application Analyst

Location: Vancouver, Canada

Department: Information Technology

Reports To: Manager, Applications

Position Summary

The Application Analyst is part of the global IT team responsible for the delivery and operations of Seapeak’s business systems. The Application Analyst will apply critical thinking in supporting and delivering services to a constantly evolving business and technical landscape. Strong relationships will be established with IT and process stakeholders to understand support requirements and how the supported systems are used.

Major Responsibilities


Establish strong relationships with process stakeholders to understand and prioritize work items; create specifications, process/data maps, and use cases, with a specific lens on identifying opportunities for process automation and low-code solutions
Identify, track, and resolve application problems; leverage automation tools to streamline day-to-day troubleshooting and performance monitoring
Manage application environments for testing; collaborate with other Analysts to design and operate data interfaces and APIs, focusing on low-code integration strategies
Champion data integrity and reliability through education and monitoring; mentor junior IT members on both system support and emerging automation practices
Support the new vessel onboarding process and provide routine/non-routine administration for application use and security access
Create or update standardized application support documentation, incorporating automated workflow guides where applicable
Coordinate with the global Vessel Systems Support group to test, implement, and support solutions on the vessels
Work with the Analytics team to improve data quality; utilize low-code tools or agentic automation concepts to optimize data warehouse integrations and identify table relationships.
Develop reports and dashboards to assist in identifying KPIs and trends, utilizing automated reporting features where possible


Requirements (Knowledge, Skills & Abilities)


3 - 5 years working with process/functional owners and providing 2nd level operational support for large business applications
Experience with RFPs and a full application lifecycle
Demonstrated ability to learn and adapt; experience or high interest in low-code environments (preferably OutSystems) and automation platforms (preferably UiPath) to improve business systems
Strong knowledge of continuous quality improvement, management information systems techniques, and change control procedures
Self-starter and results-driven; able to prioritize and complete tasks in a timely manner with minimum guidance
Proactive, dependable, and positive attitude with experience coordinating deployments across multiple stakeholder groups
Solid SQL querying experience, and experience with report/dashboard writing software such as PowerBI
Outgoing personality with excellent communication skills; comfortable delivering presentations and leading meetings
Flexibility to provide after-hours support; experience supporting remote locations in multiple time zones is an asset
Available to travel to regional offices and to vessels to service software, if required


Compensation

The typical hiring range for this position is $75,000 – $90,000 CAD per year. The final agreed upon salary may vary based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills and experience.

Benefits Package


Target Annual Bonus Plan
17 vacation days, growing to 30 days with service, plus statutory holidays
100% company paid benefits for employee and direct dependents includes extended health, dental, vision, group life insurance, AD&D, STD and LTD
Health Care Spending Account of CAD750 / year
Global Medical Assistance
Employee Assistance Program
Flexible Spending Allowance CAD 1,500/year
Hybrid working. Although you are welcome to work in our Downtown Vancouver office full time, you are able to work from home up to 2 days per week after completing the probationary period.


About Us

With combined assets of over USD 10 billon, Seapeak is one of the world’s largest independent owner and operators of Liquefied Natural Gas carriers, with ownership interests in over 50 LNG vessels. In addition, Seapeak has ownership interests in 40 Natural Gas Liquids vessels. Our sponsor, leading infrastructure investor Stonepeak, has helped to position our company with a focus on growth in our global gas transportation markets. Since early-2022, we have invested over USD 2.2 billion, adding 22 ships to our fleet.

Our business is important – we are powering the day-to-day lives of so many around the world – and we are looking to build our Team to support our growth through acquisition and newbuild orders.

At Seapeak, we are committed to building successful careers and future leaders. Join us! For the chance to be empowered, to make decisions and be part of a supportive, high-achieving Team.

We set the standard for industry. We are solution driven, accountable for results and measured by success.

  • Develop reports and dashboards to assist in identifying KPIs and trends, utilizing automated reporting features where possible

Requirements (Knowledge, Skills & Abilities)

  • 3 - 5 years working with process/functional owners and providing 2nd level operational support for large business applications
  • Experience with RFPs and a full application lifecycle
  • Demonstrated ability to learn and adapt; experience or high interest in low-code environments (preferably OutSystems) and automation platforms (preferably UiPath) to improve business systems
  • Strong knowledge of continuous quality improvement, management information systems techniques, and change control procedures
  • Self-starter and results-driven; able to prioritize and complete tasks in a timely manner with minimum guidance
  • Proactive, dependable, and positive attitude with experience coordinating deployments across multiple stakeholder groups
  • Solid SQL querying experience, and experience with report/dashboard writing software such as PowerBI
  • Outgoing personality with excellent communication skills; comfortable delivering presentations and leading meetings
  • Flexibility to provide after-hours support; experience supporting remote locations in multiple time zones is an asset
  • Available to travel to regional offices and to vessels to service software, if required

Compensation

The typical hiring range for this position is $75,000 – $90,000 CAD per year. The final agreed upon salary may vary based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills and experience.

Benefits Package

  • Target Annual Bonus Plan
  • 17 vacation days, growing to 30 days with service, plus statutory holidays
  • 100% company paid benefits for employee and direct dependents includes extended health, dental, vision, group life insurance, AD&D, STD and LTD
  • Health Care Spending Account of CAD750 / year
  • Global Medical Assistance
  • Employee Assistance Program
  • Flexible Spending Allowance CAD 1,500/year
  • Hybrid working. Although you are welcome to work in our Downtown Vancouver office full time, you are able to work from home up to 2 days per week after completing the probationary period.

About Us

With combined assets of over USD 10 billon, Seapeak is one of the world’s largest independent owner and operators of Liquefied Natural Gas carriers, with ownership interests in over 50 LNG vessels. In addition, Seapeak has ownership interests in 40 Natural Gas Liquids vessels. Our sponsor, leading infrastructure investor Stonepeak, has helped to position our company with a focus on growth in our global gas transportation markets. Since early-2022, we have invested over USD 2.2 billion, adding 22 ships to our fleet.

Our business is important – we are powering the day-to-day lives of so many around the world – and we are looking to build our Team to support our growth through acquisition and newbuild orders.

At Seapeak, we are committed to building successful careers and future leaders. Join us! For the chance to be empowered, to make decisions and be part of a supportive, high-achieving Team.

We set the standard for industry. We are solution driven, accountable for results and measured by success.

Title: IT Assistant
Description:
UBC Properties Trust was established in 1988. We build financial legacy and build innovative spaces to live and learn. We develop, service and lease residential land at UBC to create communities and build UBC’s endowment for future generations. We construct and manage rental properties to provide residential, retail and office space to support workforce housing and other academic objectives. We provide project management services to construct institutional buildings for UBC and advise on land use and development. We operate on both the Point Grey campus and UBC Okanagan; and have two property management divisions, Village Gate Homes (UBC staff and faculty rentals) and Wesbrook Properties (market rentals).

Technology plays a central role in supporting our operations, collaboration, security, and modern workplace initiatives. Our IT team supports Microsoft 365, Teams, cloud services, corporate devices, networking infrastructure, Wi-Fi systems, printers, mobile technology, and modern boardroom technology, including Teams-enabled meeting rooms with large-format displays and video conferencing capabilities. Ensuring these systems remain reliable, secure, and easy to use is essential to supporting the day-to-day work of our employees and business operations.

The Position

We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented IT Assistant to join our growing internal IT team and support a modern Microsoft-based environment spanning cloud services, devices, boardrooms, and on-premises infrastructure. The IT Assistant is the primary point of contact for day-to-day IT support and operational technology needs across the organization. The role supports staff with hardware, software, and infrastructure monitoring while working closely with the Director of IT & Cybersecurity.

This hands-on position is ideal for someone who enjoys both proactive operational work and responsive user support. A key responsibility is performing daily infrastructure and technology health checks to help ensure systems remain stable, reliable, and ready for use before issues affect staff.

The successful candidate will gain exposure to enterprise technologies including Microsoft 365, Intune, virtualization, cybersecurity tools, Teams Rooms, networking, and modern endpoint management within a collaborative, continuously improving environment.

Key Responsibilities

End-User Support


Provide Tier 1 IT support for hardware, software, account, and connectivity issues
Respond to support requests professionally and within established response expectations
Support users with Microsoft 365 applications including Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
Troubleshoot Windows and macOS devices, peripherals, printers, docking stations, and mobile devices
Escalate advanced technical issues when appropriate


Daily Infrastructure & System Checks


Perform daily operational health checks across critical IT systems and infrastructure, including:
Microsoft Teams Rooms, displays, cameras, microphones, speakers, and presentation systems
Wi-Fi infrastructure, switches, VPN connectivity, and internet services
On-prem servers including Active Directory, Scale Computing HC3 virtualization environment, backup jobs, and access control systems
Printers, scanners, and shared office technology
Document daily check results and escalate anomalies or recurring issues appropriately
Proactively resolve minor issues before they impact staff operations
Maintain and continuously improve operational checklists and documentation


Device Setup & Lifecycle Management


Configure, deploy, recover, and maintain staff laptops, desktops, mobile devices, and accessories
Support onboarding and offboarding processes
Maintain accurate inventory records including device assignments, warranties, and accessories
Perform software installations, patching, and routine endpoint maintenance
Assist with endpoint management platforms including Microsoft Intune, NinjaOne, and Jamf


Identity, Microsoft 365 & Cloud Support


Assist with account provisioning, password resets, MFA/passkey support, licensing, and access requests within Active Directory and Entra ID
Support SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams permissions and collaboration requests
Help staff follow best practices for secure cloud collaboration and data handling
Assist with Microsoft 365 administration and troubleshooting activities


On-Prem Infrastructure Support

Assist with monitoring and supporting on-prem systems under the guidance of the Director of IT & Cybersecurity, including:


Active Directory administration and troubleshooting
Scale Computing HC3 virtualization environment monitoring and maintenance assistance
Access control systems and related infrastructure
Internal application and automation servers
Backup monitoring, maintenance windows, patching, and system restarts
Infrastructure documentation and operational procedures


Cybersecurity & Operational Awareness


Assist users with identifying suspicious emails and security-related concerns
Support phishing awareness initiatives and IT security communications
Follow established IT and cybersecurity procedures
Escalate recurring operational or security concerns appropriately
Assist with cybersecurity and infrastructure platforms including Microsoft Defender, Fortinet, Wazuh, and related tools


Boardroom & Office Technology Support


Support Teams Rooms, AV systems, displays, cameras, microphones, and meeting technology
Assist staff with meeting setup and troubleshooting
Resolve general office technology issues involving printers, scanners, connectivity, and shared equipment


Documentation & Continuous Improvement


Maintain clear documentation for procedures, troubleshooting steps, inventory, infrastructure, and operational checks
Create end-user guides and internal IT documentation
Support process improvements and operational standardization initiatives
Utilize AI tools such as Microsoft Copilot to improve efficiency and documentation quality
Assist with IT projects, deployments, and technology rollouts as needed


Required

Competencies and Qualifications


Diploma, certificate, or formal coursework in Information Technology, Computer Systems, Cybersecurity, or a related field
0–2 years of experience in IT support, helpdesk, or technical support environments
Recent graduates with strong technical fundamentals are encouraged to apply
Working knowledge of:
Microsoft 365
Windows and macOS
Basic networking concepts
Active Directory and user account administration
Strong communication, customer service, and troubleshooting skills
Ability to explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical users
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
Ability to manage confidential information professionally
Comfortable balancing proactive operational tasks with reactive support requests


Nice to Have


Exposure to virtualization platforms such as Scale Computing HC3, Hyper-V, or VMware
Familiarity with endpoint management tools such as Intune, NinjaOne, or Jamf
Familiarity with ticketing systems such as NinjaOne Ticketing or ServiceNow
Basic understanding of Wi-Fi, DNS, DHCP, VPNs, and switching
Exposure to cybersecurity tools and monitoring platforms
Interest in automation, AI technologies, scripting, or infrastructure operations
Experience in coding languages; Python and SQL database is an asset


Personal Attributes


Helpful, approachable, and professional
Curious and eager to learn
Calm under pressure and adaptable in fast-moving situations
Reliable, organized, and detail-oriented
Comfortable working independently while also collaborating closely with others
Professional and discreet with confidential information


Physical Requirements


Onsite role located at UBC Campus in Vancouver, BC
Ability to lift up to 25 lbs including laptops, monitors, AV equipment, and related technology
Comfortable working in office, boardroom, storage, and light infrastructure environments


Compensation And Benefits Package

At UBC Properties Trust, our benefits reflect our core values and commitment to the well-being of our people in every way. The salary extended for this role will be determined based on a range of factors including, but not limited to, the candidate's skills, experience, education, and training, alongside market compensation benchmarks and internal equity considerations.

In addition to a competitive base salary, UBC Properties Trust offers a total rewards package that includes a performance-based incentive plan, comprehensive health and dental benefits, a defined contribution pension plan, and paid time off.

Some benefits we have to offer (among others we have):


Full Extended Benefits (health, dental, and vision)
A health and wellness spending account
RRSP Contribution
Annual performance-based bonus and retention compensation
Pet-friendly office
Minimum 3 weeks paid vacation (increases with tenure) and 10 paid sick days

Title: Business Intelligence & Analytics Specialist
Description:
Key Responsibilities
Business Engagement & Requirements: Partner with Sales, Finance, and Supply Chain to understand processes and KPIs, lead requirements sessions, and translate business needs into clear analytical solutions.
Development & Delivery: Build and maintain Power BI solutions, including intuitive reports and dashboards, wireframes, semantic models, and advanced DAX within Microsoft Fabric.
Problem Solving & Analysis: Break down complex requests, investigate data discrepancies, ensure reporting accuracy, and drive automation and process improvements.
Required Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in Computer Science, Analytics, or a related field.
Minimum 4 years of experience in a BI or analytics role.
Strong hands-on experience with Power BI and Microsoft Fabric.
Proficiency in SQL, data modeling, and DAX.
Experience with row-level security, column-level filtering, role-based access controls, and data masking/anonymization.
Preferred Qualifications
Experience in the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) industry.
Knowledge of (or willingness to learn) data architecture, ETL, and data engineering using Python/PySpark.
Microsoft Fabric Analytics Engineer or Power BI certification.
Familiarity with ERP data.
Notes
Salary range: $85,000-$100,000, plus a 10% bonus.
This role is based in the Richmond office 4 days per week.

Title: Business Advisor, Data and Analytics
Description:
The salary range for this position is CAD $38.90/Hr. - CAD $55.92/Hr.

Come work as a Business Advisor, Data & Analytics with Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH)!

Vancouver Coastal Health is looking for a Business Advisor, Data & Analytics to join the Health Information Management Team. Apply today to join our team!

As a Business Advisor, Data & Analytics with Vancouver Coastal Health you will


Examine and interpret organizational data to unlock valuable insights to inform strategic decision-making.
Support the department’s mission to enhance data-driven decision processes, identify meaningful trends, and contribute significantly to the improvement of our business operations.
Collaborates with partners across the organization to understand and gather data requirements.
Builds and applies effective data models to support business objectives.
Utilizes a variety of analytical tools and techniques to generate comprehensive reports and visualizations.
Analyzes complex data sets to provide useful insights into business outcomes.
Contributes to the development and execution of data-driven strategies for business optimization.
Ensures data accuracy and integrity across all analytical activities.
Keeps up with industry developments and innovations in data analytics and business intelligence tools.
Carries out responsibilities in accordance with health and safety requirements. Immediately reports unsafe situations by notifying supervisor or other appropriate personnel.
Performs other related duties as assigned.



This work is framed within VCH’s commitments to our pillars of Indigenous Cultural Safety, Anti-Racism, Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, and Planetary Health.

Education & Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Business Administration, or related field, with a minimum of three (3) years’ recent, related experience in data analysis, business analytics, or a similar role, with a preference for experience in the healthcare sector or in organizations of comparable complexity.
Demonstrated ability to apply data modelling, analysis, and visualization techniques in a business context.



Knowledge & Abilities


Proven experience in data analysis, business analytics, or a similar role. Advanced proficiency in analytical tools and software (e.g., SQL, Python, R, Tableau, Power BI).
Knowledge of database systems like MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, or MS SQL
Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to think analytically.
Familiarity with machine learning algorithms and predictive modeling, capable of applying these techniques to forecast trends and inform strategic decisions.
Strong understanding of data privacy and security principles, especially relevant in the healthcare industry, to ensure the integrity and confidentiality of sensitive information.
Outstanding communication skills, with the ability to translate complex data into understandable insights and recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Experience in stakeholder management, with a demonstrated ability to work collaboratively with various departments and communicate complex data insights to non-technical audiences effectively.
A collaborative team player with a proven track record of working effectively with cross-functional teams.
Detail-oriented with a strong commitment to accuracy and quality.
Develops and maintains strong relationships with key internal and external partners to promote and enhance services provided with sensitivity to diversity and indigenous lens.
Practices the recognition, inclusion and support of all types and aspects of diversity at all stages of engagement, with an understanding of the issues faced by people from equity-deserving groups.
Physical ability to perform the duties of the position.



The hours of work including days off and work area may be subject to change consistent with operational requirements and the provision of the Collective Agreement and applicable statutes. Successful applicants may be required to complete a Criminal Records Review Check.

As per Ministry of Health policy, all health care workers working in publicly-funded health care facilities are required to report their past receipt of certain vaccines or history of certain infections. Collecting these records will allow for offering of any missing vaccines, and for appropriate actions to be taken in the event of any future exposure to a communicable disease or during outbreaks. For all new hires and appointments to Vancouver Coastal Health, you will be asked to provide this information as part of the onboarding process.

Why Join Vancouver Coastal Health (VCH)

VCH is a world class innovator in medical care, research and teaching, delivering service to more than one million BC residents. At VCH, we embrace thinking boldly, taking smart risks, and 'going first' when we believe it will lead to the best possible outcomes for patients and their families. We invite you to join us in creating healthy lives in healthy communities by showcasing our passion for care, connection to the communities we serve and our culture of teamwork that makes VCH a great place to work.


Comprehensive health benefits package, including MSP, extended health and dental and municipal pension plan
Grow your career with employer-paid training and leadership development opportunities
Wellness supports, including counselling, critical incident and innovative wellness services are available to employees and their immediate families
Award-winning recognition programs to honour staff, medical staff and volunteers
Access to exclusive discount offers and deals for VCH staff



At Vancouver Coastal Health, our vision of healthy lives in healthy communities inspires us and our values and pillars guide us. We support equitable practices, diverse teams and inclusive environments, which are vital to creating welcoming spaces where everyone can bring their whole selves to work and feel supported. We encourage applications from equity-deserving communities, including Indigenous Peoples and members of racialized groups, people with disabilities and people of all gender identities and expressions and sexual orientation.

Vancouver Coastal Health is proud to be recognized as one of Canada’s Top 100 Employers, BC’s Top Employers, Canada’s Best Diversity Employers and Canada's Top Employers for Young People in 2026.

Only short-listed applicants will be contacted for this posting.

***Employees of VCH must apply online via the Internal Career Portal on CareerHub, you are currently viewing the External Career Portal. Refer to the https//my.vch.ca/working-here/job-postings site for instructions on how to view internal job postings and how to apply as an employee. Current VCH employees who apply to this posting using this external site will be considered as an external candidate. Seniority will not apply.***

Thank you for your interest in Vancouver Coastal Health.

Title: Junior Developer I
Description:
Engineers and Geoscientists British Columbia is currently searching for a Junior Developer I to join our team in Burnaby!

The Junior Developer I is responsible and accountable for:


assisting the Manager, Application Development, Associate Director, Information Systems Development, and Intermediate and Senior developers with all aspects of software design and coding;
owning key development tasks needed for the successful delivery of a project;
with assistance, creating, managing, and reporting own work packages associated with a project;
understanding directions given by manager and seniors to ensure that tasks align with project definition; and
assisting with the resolution of operational issues with developed solutions.


With assistance and oversight, the Junior Developer I is also responsible for full stack development to incrementally build, test, and deploy solutions built in .NET Core and React, which utilize web, databases, APIs, web services, reporting, integration technologies, and tools.

Our organization - one of BC's Top Employers – offers a competitive salary, comprehensive and generous benefits, an inclusive culture, and a hybrid work environment. Full details on our compensation and benefits value offering can be found below.

What You Will Be Doing


Develops and codes UIs and reports needed to implement business solutions under oversight; performs unit and system tests, and may assist with integration and end user testing
Implements reports against existing data models and specifications; will learn the meaning of the data being reported on
Collaborates with others to provide effort-based estimates for enhancements. Creates (with assistance) work package plans that list tasks and their estimated effort to deliver project goals. Updates plans weekly with progress
Works with requirements that have been previously defined; works with others to understand the proposed solution
Will be expected to participate in brainstorming session related to data models and technical designs; will participate in defining testing requirements and coding reviews
Participates in the support and problem resolution of existing systems; may be asked to assist in the deployment of services to end users
Other duties as assigned by direct leader and, as appropriate, members of the divisional leadership team


What You Bring To The Table

The successful applicant will possess the following:


A 2+ year technical diploma in Computer Programming, or equivalent, is required
A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science is preferred.
0-1 years of experience in a development role.
Knowledge of C#, .NET, data modeling, SQL/RDBMS, technical development processes and patterns, reporting, and web development (React, HTML, CSS, JavaScript), which is actively being acquired through a combination of education, self-learning, and hands-on work experience
Must have the ability and skills to work in teams, including the ability to learn, ask questions, and listen
Experience in implementing web-based solutions using HTML, CSS and frontend JavaScript frameworks
Experience defining, accessing, and working with data models
Experience designing, coding, testing, and deploying technical solutions implemented in .NET


What's In It For You

Each member of our team contributes to public safety in BC and helps us deliver on our vision of "modern regulation for a resilient world." We serve the public as an inclusive, progressive, and future-focused regulator. Our Strategic Plan also places priority on our role to advance climate action, foster equity, diversity and inclusion, and move towards reconciliation with Indigenous peoples. At Engineers and Geoscientists BC, we are committed to demonstrating our values of collaboration, excellence, integrity, and innovation.

This role offers a competitive compensation and benefits program including a salary within our hiring range of $62,500 to $69,500. Placement within the hiring range will be based on candidate experience relative to the requirements of the role. Additional details on our generous benefit offerings are found below.

Who We Are

Engineers and Geoscientists BC is proud to be recognized as one of BC's Top Employers.

Engineers and Geoscientists British Columbia is the business name of the Association of Professional Engineers and Geoscientists of the Province of British Columbia. We regulate and govern these professions under the authority of the Professional Governance Act.

We are charged with protecting the public interest by setting and maintaining high academic, experience, and professional practice standards for over 40,000 registrants. Individuals licensed by Engineers and Geoscientists BC are the only persons permitted by law to undertake and assume responsibility for engineering and geoscience projects in BC.

We are a not-for-profit organization governed by a board of elected registrants, licensees, and government appointees. The board is accountable to the public through the Ministry of Post-Secondary Education and Future Skills under the Office of the Superintendent of Professional Governance, for both the governance and management of the organization.

To learn more about our work please visit us at https://www.egbc.ca.

Title: Data Engineer 1
Description:
Come join Pattison Food Group Ltd., a Jim Pattison business, and Canada’s largest Western-based provider of food and health products.

Pattison Food Group Ltd. consists of Save-On-Foods and other well-known grocery banners. With nearly 300 retail locations, we proudly employ more than 30,000 team members.

We have an exciting opportunity for a full-time Data Engineer to join our IT team in Langley, BC.

Reporting to the Manager, Data & Analytics, you will be responsible for working with one of the largest, most comprehensive datasets in Canada while delivering and pioneering solutions that will disrupt the retail industry using the latest tools and technologies.

You will be responsible for:


Designing and constructing high-performance, scalable data pipelines to operationalize our state-of-the-art solutions ranging from quantum computing pipelines to deep learning based recommender systems
Creating innovative solutions to tackle computationally intensive challenges and business issues
Writing production Python/Scala scripts in a Spark/Databricks environment to support the data pipelines that feed Machine Learning models
Leveraging cloud environments and following best practices while building solutions in the data lake as it evolves to meet the needs of the business
Performing data wrangling, feature engineering, and dimension reduction with data of all shapes and sizes, from parquet files to images
Partnering with Machine Learning Engineers, Data Scientists, and Cloud Architects to deliver models into production
Coaching, mentoring, and providing feedback to team members and junior data engineers
Discovering novel datasets and data sources and making them available for modeling and reporting
Using your expertise in big data technologies to champion innovation, design, and implement data-stores and analytic models, oversee large-scale data lake platforms, and design and implement efficient data pipelines from source systems to data-stores
Defining and implementing Quality Assurance best practices that support the implementation of data transformations and the availability of high-quality data.



You have:


A Bachelor’s degree in a STEM discipline at minimum, a Master’s is preferred.
A minimum 2 years of experience in creating and managing large-scale, distributed data
Expertise in Python and Spark. Experience in other programming languages such as Java, Scala, or C++ is a plus
Expertise in SQL
Experience designing and deploying high-performance data processing systems with reliable data validation, monitoring, and logging practices
Rigor in high code quality, automated testing, and other engineering best practices
Experience with big data technologies such as Hadoop, Spark and Databricks
Familiarity with software development best practices, including version control, CI/CD and CAB processes
Expert knowledge of databases and modern data workloads



You are:


A strong communicator
Detail-oriented and organized
A leader with an ownership mindset
Passionate about machine learning
Able to work independently and as part of a team



What We Offer

In addition to a highly competitive salary, we offer a great range of benefits, a company pension plan, free parking, exclusive team member offers, and opportunities for development and career progression.

If you’re looking to join a team that puts people first, you’re in the right place. Apply online today!

Title: IT Support Technician
Description:
Madison Group is a diversified holding company with businesses in real estate, industrial automation, data, and media. Headquartered in Vancouver, we support 1,100+ employees coast to coast. Our central IT team keeps the technology behind these companies running smoothly, securely, and consistently across a mixed Microsoft, Google, Windows, and macOS environment.

Love variety? In this hybrid position, you’ll split your week between remote troubleshooting and on-site visits at multiple Lower Mainland offices, with a minimum of three (3) days on location each week. Because our users count on a regular, in-person IT presence, the successful candidate must be able to reliably commute to the Greater Vancouver area.

From classic break/fix support to endpoint troubleshooting, SaaS administration, and occasional hands-on office support, you’ll be the friendly IT face our users depend on. The balance of your week, typically two (2) days, can be worked remotely.

This role is best suited to someone who is comfortable taking ownership of their work. We are looking for a dependable self-starter who can interpret requests, ask thoughtful clarifying questions, work through issues with appropriate independence, and follow tasks through to completion. You won’t be expected to know everything, and you won’t be working in isolation. Our team collaborates regularly, shares knowledge, and provides support when guidance or escalation is needed.

Responsibilities

End user support

Provide Windows and macOS break/fix support, including general hardware and software troubleshooting, Microsoft 365 support, Google Workspace support, basic networking troubleshooting, and boardroom A/V assistance.

On-site support

Assist with device swaps, workstation setups, printer and cabling issues, shipping and receiving hardware, and other hands-on support needs across our Lower Mainland offices.

Endpoint management

Troubleshoot devices provisioned through Intune / Autopilot and Jamf, including profiles, compliance, application deployment, and general device management issues.

Identity and SaaS administration

Perform user lifecycle tasks in Entra ID, on-prem AD, Google Workspace, and related SaaS platforms. Assist with MFA resets, licensing, access requests, account troubleshooting, Gmail, groups, shared drives, and general SaaS administration. Prior Google Workspace administration experience is an asset, but candidates should at minimum be comfortable learning and supporting it within a reasonable timeframe.

ITSM workflow

Log, track, and resolve tickets, meet SLAs, maintain accurate notes, and contribute to clear documentation in both tickets and knowledge base articles.

What You Bring

Technical

Interpersonal


2 to 4 years of help desk, desktop support, or field support experience
Windows 10/11 and macOS troubleshooting experience
Microsoft 365 support experience
Experience with, or willingness to learn, Google Workspace administration, including Gmail, groups, users, shared drives, and access troubleshooting
Hands-on support of Intune / Autopilot and Jamf-managed devices
Entra ID / AD user and group administration
Networking fundamentals, including switches, access points, VPN, DNS, DHCP, and basic connectivity troubleshooting
Experience working in an ITSM platform and documenting work clearly
Excellent written and spoken English
Clear, friendly, and approachable customer service
Organized, dependable, and detail-oriented
Comfortable supporting users both remotely and in person
Able to take ownership of requests by interpreting requirements, asking thoughtful questions, moving work forward, and knowing when to escalate or seek guidance
Curious, adaptable, and comfortable learning on the job
Able to manage priorities in a dynamic environment


Bonus Skills


Patronum or other Google Workspace management tools
Prior Google Workspace administration experience
Prior Jamf administration or macOS management experience
PowerShell or other scripting experience
CompTIA A+ / Network+
Microsoft Azure and security fundamentals, such as AZ-900, SC-900, or equivalent
ITIL Foundation or experience working in an ITIL-aligned service desk environment
Exposure to CIS Controls, security baselines, endpoint compliance, or identity security practices
Experience supporting mixed Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace environments
Experience with PrinterLogic, TeamViewer, Jamf, Intune, or similar support tools
Additional hands-on networking or infrastructure support experience


Other Requirements


Able to commute reliably to Burnaby, Richmond, and Vancouver offices at least three (3) days per week
Able to travel between Lower Mainland office locations as required
Able to lift approximately 50 lbs and work in tight spaces when needed
Eligible to work in Canada
Able to pass a background check


What We Offer


$60,000 to $80,000, dependent on experience and qualifications
Competitive extended health and dental plan
Collaborative environment with exposure to a wide range of technologies
Mileage reimbursement and paid parking where applicable
Company-provided cell phone
Professional development budget and paid certifications
Hybrid flexibility with a blend of remote focus time and dynamic field work
Opportunity to build experience across Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, endpoint management, SaaS administration, and occasional infrastructure support


Madison is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age, or other legally protected status.

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Product Analytics
Description:
Job Requisition ID #

26WD99763

Position Overview

Data is central to how we understand customer behavior, measure product success, and build better experiences for our customers. Autodesk is seeking a Senior Data Analyst - Product Analytics to join the Product Development & Manufacturing Solutions organization. In this role, you'll partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, Design, Finance, Sales, and Marketing to transform product data into actionable insights that influence product strategy and business outcomes.

As a trusted analytics partner, you will help answer critical questions about feature adoption, customer engagement, retention, and product health. You'll define meaningful success metrics, develop scalable measurement frameworks, and uncover opportunities to improve the customer experience through data.

We're looking for someone who thrives in ambiguity, is naturally curious, and enjoys asking difficult questions that lead to better decisions. You combine strong analytical thinking with business acumen and exceptional communication skills to translate complex analyses into compelling stories that drive action.

At Autodesk, we value a hybrid work culture. Remote work is supported in Toronto and Vancouver, Canada. If you live near one of our offices, you're welcome to work there full- or part-time based on your preference.

Responsibilities


Partner with Product Managers and Engineering teams throughout the product lifecycle to define success metrics and measurement strategies before features launch
Analyze product telemetry, clickstream, and user behavior data to identify opportunities that improve customer engagement, adoption, and retention
Design, maintain, and evolve scalable product KPIs and data models that provide consistent measurement across products
Build self-service dashboards, reports, and visualizations that enable teams to independently explore product performance while maintaining trusted business definitions
Conduct deep-dive analyses to understand product usage, customer journeys, and feature performance, translating findings into actionable recommendations
Develop hypotheses, evaluate product changes, and support experimentation and A/B testing where applicable
Partner with Data Engineering to improve event instrumentation, data quality, and analytics infrastructure
Communicate analytical findings to executive and cross-functional audiences through clear storytelling and data visualization
Improve analytics documentation, metric definitions, and best practices to promote consistency and scalability across teams


Minimum Qualifications


5+ years of experience in Product Analytics, Data Analytics, or Business Analytics, including experience influencing product or business decisions through data
Demonstrated experience presenting analytical findings and recommendations to senior leadership and executive audiences
Expertise in SQL and working with modern cloud data warehouse environments
Advanced experience building dashboards and visualizations using Power BI (required); experience with Tableau or Looker is a plus
Experience analyzing event-based product telemetry, clickstream, or user behavior datasets
Strong understanding of analytical methods, hypothesis-driven problem solving, and translating ambiguous business questions into measurable analyses
Experience working with modern analytics engineering and data pipeline technologies (DBT, Airflow, GitHub, or similar)
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience)


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with experimentation frameworks, A/B testing, or product measurement methodologies
Experience using Python (pandas, Jupyter, NumPy, or similar) for exploratory analysis and automation
Familiarity with product analytics platforms such as Amplitude, Mixpanel, Pendo, or similar tools
Experience integrating product analytics with Sales and Marketing data sources (Salesforce, Marketo, etc.)
Experience working within SaaS or cloud software organizations


Learn More

About Autodesk

Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made.

We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world.

When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us!

Salary transparency

Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. For Canada based roles, we expect a starting base salary between $107,000 and $156,200. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience and geographic location, and may exceed this range. In addition to base salaries, our compensation package may include annual cash bonuses, commissions for sales roles, stock grants, and a comprehensive benefits package.

Belonging

We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/global-belonging

Are you an existing contractor or consultant with Autodesk?

Please search for open jobs and apply internally (not on this external site).

Job Requisition ID #

26WD99763

Position Overview

Data is central to how we understand customer behavior, measure product success, and build better experiences for our customers. Autodesk is seeking a Senior Data Analyst - Product Analytics to join the Product Development & Manufacturing Solutions organization. In this role, you'll partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, Design, Finance, Sales, and Marketing to transform product data into actionable insights that influence product strategy and business outcomes.

As a trusted analytics partner, you will help answer critical questions about feature adoption, customer engagement, retention, and product health. You'll define meaningful success metrics, develop scalable measurement frameworks, and uncover opportunities to improve the customer experience through data.

We're looking for someone who thrives in ambiguity, is naturally curious, and enjoys asking difficult questions that lead to better decisions. You combine strong analytical thinking with business acumen and exceptional communication skills to translate complex analyses into compelling stories that drive action.

At Autodesk, we value a hybrid work culture. Remote work is supported in Toronto and Vancouver, Canada. If you live near one of our offices, you're welcome to work there full- or part-time based on your preference.

Responsibilities

  • Partner with Product Managers and Engineering teams throughout the product lifecycle to define success metrics and measurement strategies before features launch
  • Analyze product telemetry, clickstream, and user behavior data to identify opportunities that improve customer engagement, adoption, and retention
  • Design, maintain, and evolve scalable product KPIs and data models that provide consistent measurement across products
  • Build self-service dashboards, reports, and visualizations that enable teams to independently explore product performance while maintaining trusted business definitions
  • Conduct deep-dive analyses to understand product usage, customer journeys, and feature performance, translating findings into actionable recommendations
  • Develop hypotheses, evaluate product changes, and support experimentation and A/B testing where applicable
  • Partner with Data Engineering to improve event instrumentation, data quality, and analytics infrastructure
  • Communicate analytical findings to executive and cross-functional audiences through clear storytelling and data visualization
  • Improve analytics documentation, metric definitions, and best practices to promote consistency and scalability across teams

Minimum Qualifications

  • 5+ years of experience in Product Analytics, Data Analytics, or Business Analytics, including experience influencing product or business decisions through data
  • Demonstrated experience presenting analytical findings and recommendations to senior leadership and executive audiences
  • Expertise in SQL and working with modern cloud data warehouse environments
  • Advanced experience building dashboards and visualizations using Power BI (required); experience with Tableau or Looker is a plus
  • Experience analyzing event-based product telemetry, clickstream, or user behavior datasets
  • Strong understanding of analytical methods, hypothesis-driven problem solving, and translating ambiguous business questions into measurable analyses
  • Experience working with modern analytics engineering and data pipeline technologies (DBT, Airflow, GitHub, or similar)
  • Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience)

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with experimentation frameworks, A/B testing, or product measurement methodologies
  • Experience using Python (pandas, Jupyter, NumPy, or similar) for exploratory analysis and automation
  • Familiarity with product analytics platforms such as Amplitude, Mixpanel, Pendo, or similar tools
  • Experience integrating product analytics with Sales and Marketing data sources (Salesforce, Marketo, etc.)
  • Experience working within SaaS or cloud software organizations

Learn More

About Autodesk

Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made.

We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world.

When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us!

Salary transparency

Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. For Canada based roles, we expect a starting base salary between $107,000 and $156,200. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience and geographic location, and may exceed this range. In addition to base salaries, our compensation package may include annual cash bonuses, commissions for sales roles, stock grants, and a comprehensive benefits package.

Belonging

We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/global-belonging

Are you an existing contractor or consultant with Autodesk?

Please search for open jobs and apply internally (not on this external site).

Title: Clinical Systems Analyst
Description:
Clinical Systems Analyst (AI Training)

About The Role

Your healthcare IT expertise is more valuable than ever — not just in hospitals, but in shaping the next generation of AI. At Alignerr, we partner with the world's leading AI research labs to build and train cutting-edge models. We're looking for Clinical Systems Analysts to help ensure AI understands the complex, high-stakes world of clinical technology.

This is a fully remote, flexible contract role where your real-world knowledge of EHRs, clinical workflows, and healthcare infrastructure directly improves how AI reasons about these systems.


Organization: Alignerr
Type: Hourly Contract
Location: Remote
Commitment: 10–40 hours/week


What You'll Do


Evaluate and provide expert feedback on AI-generated responses related to clinical systems, healthcare IT, and hospital workflows
Review technical scenarios involving EHR configurations, system troubleshooting, and clinical application support for accuracy and realism
Identify gaps, errors, or unsafe recommendations in AI outputs and suggest improvements
Translate your clinical and technical experience into structured, actionable feedback that helps AI models reason more accurately
Work independently and asynchronously on your own schedule


Who You Are


Experienced in supporting or administering healthcare IT systems — EHRs, clinical applications, or hospital infrastructure
Strong technical troubleshooting and system optimization skills
Able to bridge the gap between clinical workflows and technical implementation
Detail-oriented with clear written communication skills
Comfortable working independently on task-based assignments


Nice to Have


Prior experience with data annotation, data quality, or AI evaluation workflows
Background in EHR platforms such as Epic, Cerner, or Meditech
Experience in clinical informatics, health IT consulting, or systems integration


Why Join Us


Work on cutting-edge AI projects alongside top research labs and teams
Fully remote and flexible — set your own hours and workload
Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration
Contribute meaningful domain expertise to AI systems that impact healthcare
Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

Title: Quantitative Equity Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Quantitative Equity Data Analyst to join our Quantitative Equity Team! We are a high-performance team embedded in a top-performing quant fund that manages over $75 billion in financial assets. We are dedicated to the mission-critical operation of our investment engine—a well-tuned machine responsible for generating key decisions that drive trades and investment insights. Leveraging data analytics, finance knowledge and cutting-edge technology, we aim to ensure that new and useful data products are continuously ready for research.

Do you love the idea of integrating large proprietary data sources into valuable applications? Are you a wizard at transforming ‘messy reality’ into high quality data assets? As a core member of our team, you will collaborate with investment and data experts to tackle challenging problems, and to continuously expand our capabilities in data. Based on the west coast in Vancouver, we value mentorship, collaboration, and growth in a supportive and innovative environment. Join us to make a significant impact on our investment outcomes and overall success.

What You Will Do

This is an exciting full-time role for individuals who are enthusiastic about learning the quantitative equity investment management business and excited to tackle a broad range of data, investment, and technology challenges.

You will start with a comprehensive training program, learning about various elements of quantitative equity investment management and our investment process.

You will then continue to a specialized role utilizing data analytics and data science skillsets to enable the Quantitative Equity Team’s research. Your role will involve identifying and assessing brand new data, data exploration, and proposing robust ways to integrate data products from many sources.

You will be supported with coaching and mentorship from senior members of the team, and we will create the conditions for you to grow your career steadily over time. This is an opportunity for ambitious individuals

to drive their career growth in data.

Role

While we take the approach of tailoring the career path to individual strengths, we expect successful candidates for this role to develop excellence in the following tasks:


Create processes to assess, explore, and link billions of data points.
Test structured and unstructured data sets for data quality and reliability.
Assess new data opportunities for quality and usefulness.
Evaluate your data solutions for sensibility, accuracy, and bias.
Collaborate with quantitative researchers – support alpha projects with your data expertise.
Collaborate with technology specialists – help trade off application performance and operational feasibility.
Support production deployments of your work to ensure long-term reliability.
Enable clarity of and access to your work through robust documentation and exposition.
Present findings and recommendations to key stakeholders in a clear, concise, and compelling manner.


What You Bring


Analytical Mindset – You think critically and creatively and are adept at balancing intuition with statistical validation.
Financial Knowledge – Desire to acquire the financial context needed to understand your stakeholders and make data decisions with impact.
Self-Starter – You are motivated and willing to take personal accountability for quality and timeliness of work.
Collaborative Approach – You thrive in a collaborative environment and value shared success. You proactively solicit and provide input and excel at communicating complex and technical concepts.
Time Management – You are able to manage and prioritize multiple ongoing projects.
Academic Training – At a minimum, an undergraduate degree in finance, mathematics, statistics, computer science, engineering, or related discipline. Completion of a relevant graduate degree is considered an asset.


We welcome applications from candidates with different levels of experience, including interns, new graduates, and experienced professionals. The base salary range for full-time roles is $125,000 – $200,000 annually plus a competitive performance bonus. Compensation will be determined based on experience and qualifications.

To apply for this position, please submit your resume, cover letter and academic transcripts.

CC&L Financial Group is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation or expression, disability, or age. 

Your application will be reviewed by a member of the hiring team - AI is not used in the screening, assessment or selection of applications at this time.

Title: Operations Data Analyst
Description:
ABOUT 49North

49North is a Canadian defence company delivering secure, multi-domain C4ISR capabilities that provide decision advantage in complex and contested environments. A wholly-owned subsidiary of MDA Space, building on more than five decades of trusted Canadian defence delivery, 49North operates as a sovereign Canadian provider of mission-critical C4ISR systems, bringing disciplined prime-contractor execution to defence and security programs.

Visit 49North | An MDA Space Company.

49North is rooted in the execution of large, complex, and mission-critical programs developed within 49NORTH. This legacy reflects a record of delivery where engineering rigour, operational reliability, and national trust are non-negotiable.

49North Defence strengthens Canada’s sovereign defence capability by delivering integrated mission systems and multi-domain C4ISR integration, enabling trusted Canadian and coalition operations, assured interoperability, and sustained readiness.

Role

The role of Operations Data Analyst reports to the Director, Mission Assurance and resides within the Administration function of the larger Programs & Operations Team of 49North, an MDA Space business. As an Operations Data Analyst, you will work with business stakeholders to realize business intelligence through structured data analysis, processing and visualization. This role also includes Process Specialist responsibilities, you will work with process stakeholders to define, realize (implement), deploy and maintain in-scope processes as well as administrate supporting systems, notably the 49North Business Process System (BPS). As an ISO 9001 certified Quality System and plans to implement Capability Maturity Model Integration (CMMI), you will help continuously improve select enterprise systems to meet local needs and assist in the deployment of OneMDA Corporate improvement initiatives.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities of the Operations Data Analyst includes those associated with continual improvement as a Process Specialist. In terms of data analysis, two aspects are considered: business intelligence initiation and business intelligence realization.

Business Intelligence Initiation

Works With Business Leadership And Performs The Following


Identifies the means to quantitatively or qualitatively support assessment of key business objectives and their corresponding Critical Success Factors through identification of Key Performance Indicators and associated metrics
Creates plans describing the bringing together of data sources, working with Corporate stakeholders (e.g. IT) as necessary


Business Intelligence Realization


Identifies and assesses data sources for suitability and takes actions to remediate data quality and integrity as necessary
Connects data sources in a way that enables collective analysis
Develops dashboards, reports and data visualizations that provide meaningful insights to performance
Performs analyses identifying trends, patterns, anomalies and opportunities for improvement
Provides and/or presents evidence-based data interpretations and recommendations


In terms of continual improvement, two aspects are considered: facilitating implementation and change initiatives and administrating supporting systems.

Implementation and Change Initiatives Facilitation

Sources of improvement efforts can include ongoing activities (e.g. maintaining the BPS), local business initiatives (e.g. implementing CMMI), and enabling the deployment of Corporate-sponsored OneMDA initiatives.


Collaborates with leaders and subject matter experts to understand business challenges and information needs
Administrates the identification, prioritization and effort reporting of process improvement initiatives
Assesses processes (e.g. preforms value stream mapping), including creating as-is and to-be depictions of process, to identify and eliminate inefficiencies, waste, and ineffectiveness
Facilitates discovery activities working with process stakeholders and subject matter experts
Creates/revises process descriptions (e.g. procedures) and associated process artifacts (e.g. forms, templates) and training materials


Supporting Systems Administration


Establishes and maintains the process improvement roadmap reflecting the priorities of Operational Excellence and, as applicable, upgrades to the content of the BPS
Maintains the BPS through the evaluation of process Change Requests (CRs) and administration of Change Control Board (CCB) meetings with Process Owners
Participates in the review of Lessons Learned reports to identify potential opportunities for improvement (OFIs)
Leads the disposition of OFI records
Serves as the local MS SharePoint administrator and maintains the BPS infrastructure and associated instructional information on use and administration of the BPS
Performs process change impact assessments for proposed or planned changes
Establishes and maintains a process improvement performance dashboard and periodically reports on process improvement performance


Requirements (experience/skills)


A two- or three-year accredited diploma or certificate program in data analysis
Minimum 2 years of experience in the definition, implementation, maintenance and administration of business processes
Working knowledge of and administrator-level hands-on experience with Power BI
Experience with database management systems and related query language, notably SQL
Microsoft SharePoint site-level administrator experience including HTML and Java scripting capabilities
Experience with a structured change implementation methodology as applied to business processes is required. Experience in process value-stream mapping is considered an asset
Willingness to extend one’s own technical capabilities through learning
Experience working within a quality management system or an ISO- or CMMI-certified workplace is considered an asset
Experience working with Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems (e.g. Infor LN) is considered an asset
History of taking the initiative while diplomatically keeping leadership stakeholders informed along the way
Strong interpersonal skills, particularly in the areas of peer collaboration and teamwork; comfortable interfacing with the senior management team and able to ‘sell’ improvement initiatives
Proven communication skills with an ability to present to multiple stakeholder groups and achieve their buy-in for improvement initiatives


We’re a dream team of purpose-driven, collaborative and passionate people, and we are constantly looking for others to join 49North to ignite new and innovative approaches to problem-solving that push us forward.

Comments/Special Considerations

Successful candidates must obtain and hold security clearance at the secret level, and pass security assessment for the Controlled Goods Program (CGP).

Benefits Statement

49North provides competitive compensation and benefits packages for its employees at all locations. As a team member of 49North, you and your qualified dependents are eligible to participate in a benefit plan that ensures a comprehensive level of protection through competitive health care including; extended healthcare and flexible drug plans, dental and vision benefits, disability income protection, life insurance, group retirement savings plans; and an employee and family assistance program.

Equal Opportunity Statement

49North is an equal opportunity employer prizing diversity and inclusion. We are committed to treating all employees and applicants for employment with respect and dignity. If you require assistance and/or reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or the recruiting process, please feel free to notify us.

Accommodations

49North is proud to provide accommodation(s) during the recruitment process. Should you require any accommodations, please indicate this on your application/cover letter and we will work with you to meet your accessibility needs.

#IND

The compensation range for this position is $75,000.00 - $122,760.00 annually. The specific compensation offered will be based on the qualifications and experience of the successful candidate.

Title: Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst
Description:
Why You’ll Love Softchoice

We are a software-focused IT solutions and services provider that equips organizations to be agile and innovative, and for their people to be engaged, connected, and creative at work. That means moving them to the cloud, helping them build the workplace of tomorrow, and enabling them to make smarter decisions about their technology. By doing these things we help them create success for their customers and their people.

We stand proudly for our people and support their success through career development and advancement. We are recognized and respected for our culture of inclusion and belonging, continuously striving to do what’s good for our people and communities.

The Impact You Will Have

The Sr. Data and Reporting Analyst will be responsible for owning the design, delivery, and maintenance of People and Culture (P&C) reporting and business analysis. This role serves as the subject-matter expert on workforce data—driving reporting strategy, ensuring data integrity across P&C systems, and providing consultative guidance to senior leaders that shapes how the organization makes people decisions. As a senior team member, you will partner cross-functionally with business analysts and operational leaders across Softchoice to combine people data with business performance data, turning insight into action. You will lead end-to-end analytics initiatives with minimal direction, influence without authority, and bring executive presence to every stakeholder interaction.

What You’ll Do


Develop and own reporting dashboards using data mining analytics and reporting tools (SuccessFactors Story, ADP Vantage, PowerBI, Power Platform) to drive performance monitoring and executive decision-making.
Design, develop, and maintain data and statistical models, including complex data manipulation from multiple sources that generate actionable insights for leadership.
Serve as the P&C analytics subject-matter expert, proactively advising senior leaders on key metrics, trends, and data-driven recommendations that inform workforce strategy
Conduct a review of the intake and prioritization of reporting requests and ensure reporting data is presented in an understandable and user-friendly format.
Engage stakeholders at all levels in defining requirements, developing technical specifications, analyzing data flow, and completing testing for report execution.
Conduct audits on data extracted to ensure the highest quality is being used for reporting.
Perform quality assurance checks on system configurations including assessment of changes in SAP bi-annual releases to determine the impact.
Use HR system and process expertise to analyze and troubleshoot issues, identify root causes, and determine and execute resolution.
Support the implementation and maintenance of data and reporting strategy and governance within P&C and throughout the partnership with business areas.
Champion the empowerment of stakeholders at various levels in leveraging reporting tools to support strategic and operational needs.
Respond to benchmarking surveys, and prepare analysis on employee data across P&C Systems
Serve as the SME for P&C analytics, by owning the timely generation of recurring metrics.
Ensure reporting roles and access are defined and maintained in partnership with IT governance and SuccessFactors Administrators
Create and maintain Knowledge-Based Documentation (KBDs) related to new developments and enhancements in reports and dashboards for knowledge sharing and job shadowing.
Onboard new HR Technology team members on P&C reporting tools, including training and change management.
Works with other HR Technology team members to build awareness of data and process changes impacting business metrics.
Maintains current knowledge of reporting trends and best practices for long-term solutions.



What You’ll Bring To The Table


Bachelor’s degree in business analytics, management analytics, math, statistics, computer science or related field
A minimum of 5 years’ experience working with Analytics tools at an advanced level with 2 years in PowerBI and Power Platform tools.
Experience with data visualization tools with at least 1 full implementation of Planning, Forecasting BI, and Predictive Analytics
Enrolled or completing Data Analytics certification or programs is an asset (Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst or People Analytics programs)
Demonstrated experience in using queries and syntax to conduct data mining, transformation, cleansing, and visualization.
Intermediate to Advanced experience with Microsoft Office products and SQL for querying relational databases.
A broad understanding of HR business fundamentals with at least 4 years of HR operations experience
Experience in 1-2 full-cycle SAP SuccessFactors implementation projects is an asset.
Experience with owning governance, controls, processes, data modeling, master data management, data standardization

Title: Production Assistant
Description:
At Stellar, we aim to build a studio where people actually want to work. We face production’s inevitable ups and downs as a supportive, truly connected team, prioritizing open communication over anyone working in isolation. We value honesty, creativity, and looking out for one another.

The Opportunity: 

We are looking for a highly organized and eager-to-learn Production Assistant to join us on a streaming series for Marvel Animation.

This is an excellent starting point for someone looking to build a career in animation production. As a Production Assistant, you'll work alongside our Production Coordinators and Managers, supporting the day-to-day operations of multiple departments while gaining firsthand exposure to how an animation production runs from start to finish.

This role is designed to give you broad exposure across the production team. You'll learn how different departments operate, how they collaborate, and how production workflows connect across the pipeline. Along the way, you'll take on administrative and organizational responsibilities that help keep the team running smoothly while developing the foundational skills needed for a career in production.

If you enjoy staying organized, have an eye for detail, and are naturally curious, we would love to hear from you

This role requires someone who is:
- Calm under pressure
- Highly communicative
- Organized + detail oriented
- Curious and eager to learn
- Proactive about solving problems
- Comfortable adapting to shifting priorities

Success in this role comes from balancing strong organizational skills with curiosity, adaptability, and a collaborative mindset.

Meeting and Review Mastery

Attend dailies and department reviews to take incredibly accurate, clear, and actionable notes.
Promptly publish and distribute meeting notes to the team, ensuring everyone knows their next steps.

Production Support

Assist the Production Coordinators with maintaining accurate data in Flow Production Tracking (formerly ShotGrid), including shot statuses, assignments, and production information.
Help organize, name, and file digital materials, reference artwork, and client approvals so they are easily accessible to the artists.
Support a variety of production tasks across multiple departments, gaining exposure to different stages of the CG animation pipeline.

Team Communication and Administration

Act as a friendly, approachable point of contact for the artists, helping to answer basic scheduling questions or directing them to the right person.
Assist with calendar management, scheduling meetings, and prepping review playlists.
Proactively look for small ways to make the Production Coordinators and Managers days run a little smoother.
Shadow experienced Production Coordinators, learning how different departments are managed and how decisions are communicated throughout the project.

The Requirements

Must Haves:

Experience: 0 to 1 years of experience. This is an entry-level role, perfect for recent grads or those with administrative experience looking to pivot into animation.
Super Powered Organization: You have exceptional attention to detail, love a good spreadsheet, and take pride in keeping things tidy.
Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills. You aren't afraid to ask clarifying questions if you don't understand a note.
Software: Highly proficient with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive) and MS Office.
Eligibility: Legally eligible to work in Canada.

Nice to Haves:

A basic understanding of the 2D or 3D animation pipeline.
Any prior exposure to Flow Production Tracking (formerly ShotGrid) or RV is a massive bonus, but we will happily teach you.


Why Join Stellar?

Flexible Environment: We offer a flexible hybrid schedule that will vary with project and team requirements. 
Comprehensive Benefits: Extended health benefits.
Growth: Professional development support to help you stay ahead of tech and creative trends.
Culture: A collaborative, ego-free environment where technical expertise fuels creative magic.
Competitive salary range: $50,000 - $60,000, based on your experience and skills.
